### ¬ß41.10 Honest Framing & Decision Points

**The ¬ß40 ‚Üí ¬ß41 reframe**:

* ¬ß40 Waves 1-3 were framed as "advanced forward roadmap." They closed RRD recursion-bounding, adversarial cage completion, cross-session coherence, polish substrates. Each Wave shipped clean.
* ¬ß41 reframes those Waves as **engineering preamble to Tier D**, not destination. The Tier D framing was implicit; ¬ß41 makes it explicit.
* This isn't moving the goalposts ‚Äî it's making them visible.

**The fundamental decision the operator faces**:

* **Path A: Stop at Tier B (monthly review)** ‚Äî Achievable in 6-9 months. ~70% of "fully autonomous" value with ~10% of the architectural risk. Operator's role becomes batch-approver of weekly-batched proposals. Most pragmatic.
* **Path B: Push to Tier D (roadmap-level only)** ‚Äî Achievable in 18-30 months. Requires Layer 4 architectural decision. Fully realizes the operator-binding stated goal. Highest value, highest cost, highest architectural risk.
* **Path C: Recognize Tier D isn't the goal you actually want** ‚Äî The operator-in-the-loop pattern is O+V's load-bearing safety property. Other "fully autonomous" agents fail because they have no equivalent cage. Tier B might be the sweet spot architecturally.

**The operator binding 2026-05-11 confirms Path B is the destination**. ¬ß41 is the honest decomposition of that path.

**¬ß41 vs. ¬ß40**: ¬ß40 closure was structural completion of the recursion-bounding triad + UX polish + cross-session coherence. ¬ß41 is what comes after ‚Äî the actual destination. Future sessions reference ¬ß41.8 (the phased roadmap) for sequencing decisions; reference ¬ß41.7 (the architectural decision) for the load-bearing architectural choice; reference ¬ß41.5 (web browsing) for the operator-flagged capability gap; reference ¬ß41.6 (empirical evidence) for the wall-clock reality check.

**The cage doesn't get weaker as we move toward Tier D ‚Äî it gets stronger**. Boundary gate stays; hash-cap stays; antivenom stays; M10 forced-approval stays (but consults signed roadmap for scope). The operator's authority migrates from per-PR to roadmap-level. That's the actual architecture.

**¬ß41 status**: roadmap shipped 2026-05-11. Phase 0 work (Slice 2 + Slice 3 + WebBrowser substrate) is the next operator-paced sequence.

---

### ¬ß41.11 Empirical Claims Audit & Open Verification Tickets (NEW 2026-05-14)

**Origin.** During post-submission audit of the OV research paper ¬ß92 (Recent Developments addendum), the SICA-pattern empirical-demonstration claim in ¬ß92.16 thread (1) + ¬ß92.18 was found to overstate what `git log --author=Claude` actually supports. Specifically: the paper claimed "On 2026-05-11, O+V autonomously shipped two self-modification commits (Posture Aurora + Karen voice command router)" ‚Äî but both commits are dated **2026-05-10** and **authored by Derek J. Russell** (no Claude signature, no Co-Authored-By trailer, no AutoCommitter attribution). The 23 commits actually authored `Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>` in the repo are **all** appends to `docs/governance/passb_graduation_ledger.jsonl` ‚Äî autonomous bookkeeping under the Phase 9 cadence, **not** governance-code self-modification in the SICA-paper-strict sense.

**Correction commits.** Research paper ¬ß92.16 + ¬ß92.18 corrected and pushed to `origin/main` as **`ec07677c4a`** on 2026-05-14. The "demonstrated three years ahead of 2028" framing was reframed from *retroactive demonstration* to *structural readiness on a multi-month timeline well inside Anthropic's 2028 ASL-4 horizon*. The five-thread closure count in the ¬ß92.16 summary was honestly downgraded from 3-of-5 to 2-of-5 (Treefinement L2 + SWE-Bench-Pro harness binding remain real citation‚Üíimplementation closures; SICA / Constitutional / ASL-4 are positioned-but-not-empirically-closed).

**Why this section exists.** Two reasons: (i) make the empirical gaps **engineering tickets** with graduation criteria, not just paper caveats ‚Äî every overstated claim becomes a concrete ladder item the cadence can climb; (ii) preserve the audit trail for any future reviewer who walks the ¬ß92 corrections back to the PRD. The tickets below are the load-bearing items that, once closed, fully retire the ¬ß92.16 caveats ‚Äî at which point the "demonstrated SICA pattern" framing becomes *actually demonstrated*, not *positioned to demonstrate*.

#### ¬ß41.11.1 SICA-pattern empirical demonstration ‚Äî fully-autonomous governance self-modification

**The claim that needs proving.** O+V autonomously generates, validates, and commits a code mutation to a *governance file* (`iron_gate.py`, `semantic_guardian.py`, `policy_engine.py`, `risk_tier_floor.py`, `auto_committer.py`, etc.) under an autonomous signature, surviving the full Iron Gate ‚àò Semantic Guardian ‚àò TestRunner ‚àò AutoCommitter cycle with **zero operator intervention** during the generate‚Üívalidate‚Üícommit window.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- ‚úÖ Autonomous-ledger-write: 23 `Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>` commits to `docs/governance/passb_graduation_ledger.jsonl` (verifiable: `git log --author=Claude --diff-filter=A`).
- ‚úÖ NOTIFY_APPLY-mediated code generation with operator-approved commit (substrate-complete; AutoCommitter constructs the signature, operator approves the diff overlay).
- ‚úÖ AutoCommitter primitive end-to-end: `auto_committer.py` constructs commits with O+V signature, conventional commit format, risk-tier metadata, protected-branch push prevention.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ‚ùå Fully-autonomous *generate ‚Üí validate ‚Üí commit* cycle without operator intervention.
- ‚ùå Self-modification of governance code (only application code or ledger files have been auto-committed historically; governance files are still human-authored).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** **‚â•1 commit visible in `git log --author=Claude --diff-filter=M` against a governance file**, accompanied by:

1. Iron Gate AST validation green (no immutable-rule violation logged)
2. Semantic Guardian inspection green (no hard finding)
3. TestRunner green for the scoped test set
4. AutoCommitter signature present (Co-Authored-By: Claude trailer + O+V signature line)
5. Commit message conforms to conventional format with risk-tier metadata
6. **Zero operator approval events** in the audit ledger for the op span

Repeat across **3 consecutive clean soaks** under Phase 9 cadence ‚Äî at that point graduate.

**Prerequisites.**

- ¬ß41.11.4 AutoCommitter graduation-**evidence gate** returns `READY` (NOT "flag flips default-TRUE" ‚Äî the flag is already default-TRUE; the real prerequisite is the gate's per-soak-window Yellow-tier O+V evidence verdict, see ¬ß41.11.4 premise-correction)
- `risk_tier_floor` configuration permitting Yellow-tier unattended-apply on governance ops (currently floor is `notify_apply` or stricter for `ORDER_2_GOVERNANCE` per Pass B)
- Pass B Order-2 governance cage (¬ß40.1) graduated default-true OR the SICA candidate's scope kept below Order-2 (Body-only governance ops only)

**Tier placement.** ¬ß41.8 Phase 1 candidate (months 6‚Äì12 of the 18‚Äì30 month roadmap). Single highest-leverage empirical milestone ‚Äî closing this ticket alone retires ~40% of the ¬ß92.16 thread (1) caveat.

#### ¬ß41.11.2 Anti-Venom parity-scale evaluation ‚Äî Constitutional Classifiers benchmark

**The claim that needs proving.** Anti-Venom's deterministic substrate achieves **‚â§4.4% catastrophic-error rate** against an adversarial corpus of comparable scale to Anthropic's Constitutional Classifiers benchmark (*arXiv:2501.18837* ‚Äî 86%‚Üí4.4% reduction on Claude 3.5 Sonnet).

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- ‚úÖ Adversarial Cage Phase 9.4: **0/38 escapes** on hand-authored adversarial corpus (12/38 *documented* gaps ‚Äî predicted detection-gaps, not active escapes).
- ‚úÖ Semantic Firewall 11 injection detectors + 5 credential shapes + recursion ban + ScopedToolBackend mutation cage (¬ß92.1).
- ‚úÖ Iron Gate 12 immutable rules + Semantic Guardian 10 pre-apply detectors.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ‚ùå Parity-scale corpus (Constitutional Classifiers uses thousands of synthetic jailbreaks; current Adversarial Cage is 38 hand-authored).
- ‚ùå LLM-generated adversarial mutations (the ¬ß40.1 #3 "Anti-Venom self-immunization" tier ‚Äî generate jailbreaks to harden the cage).
- ‚ùå Real Claude-class generative core under the cage during adversarial evaluation (current 0/38 was deterministic-only ‚Äî no live LLM in the loop).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** Adversarial corpus expanded to **‚â•3,000 inputs** (‚â•100√ó the current 38), drawn from:

1. LLM-generated adversarial mutations (¬ß40.1 #3, gated by JARVIS_ANTIVENOM_SELF_IMMUNIZATION_ENABLED)
2. Curated jailbreak corpus from public benchmarks (HarmBench-style + Constitutional Classifiers replications)
3. Domain-specific O+V-targeted attacks (governance-mutation attempts, recursion attempts, credential exfiltration shapes)

Acceptance gate: **‚â§4.4% catastrophic-error rate** (matching Anthropic's benchmark) across the full ‚â•3,000-input corpus, with 12/38 ‚Üí ‚â§132/3000 documented-gap inflation acceptable.

**Prerequisites.**

- ‚úÖ **¬ß40.1 #3 Anti-Venom self-immunization tier ‚Äî SHIPPED 2026-05-14** (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/self_immunization.py`, ~900 LOC + 47-test spine + 6 AST pins + 5 FlagRegistry seeds). The deterministic mutation corpus generator. Closed 3-taxonomy substrate (`MutationStrategy` 8-value / `ImmunizationVerdict` 4-value / `ImmunizationOutcome` 4-value), 8 pure structural mutation operators derived from the documented-gap evasion taxonomy (alias-rebind / dunder-reconstruct / getattr-indirect / string-split / whitespace-pad / comment-decoy / unicode-confusable / identity-control), async bounded-concurrency campaign runner composing the canonical `adversarial_cage.evaluate_entry` (detection single-source-of-truth ‚Äî zero re-implementation), `cross_process_jsonl.flock_append_line` ledger, `_process_singletons.get_semaphore` concurrency, `MutationProvider`/`HardeningSink` Protocol DI. Master flag `JARVIS_ANTIVENOM_SELF_IMMUNIZATION_ENABLED` ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE. AST-pinned authority asymmetry (no orchestrator/iron_gate/policy_engine/change_engine/candidate_generator imports). `summarize_campaign()` exposes the `meets_parity_gate` boolean read against `JARVIS_ANTIVENOM_TARGET_ESCAPE_RATE` (default 0.044). **The "no hardcoding" property holds**: parity-scale corpus volume is produced dynamically (`seeds √ó strategies`), not enumerated by hand.
- AdversarialReviewer subagent extended for full-corpus parallel evaluation (currently scoped per-op, not corpus-batch) ‚Äî *remaining*
- LLM-driven `MutationProvider` implementation wired (the deterministic 8 operators ship; the optional LLM-augmentation Protocol seam exists but no concrete provider is wired yet) ‚Äî *remaining*
- Test-time compute budget: ~$50‚Äì200 per parity-scale evaluation pass (DW Tier 0 estimated) ‚Äî *remaining (only once LLM MutationProvider is wired; the deterministic path is zero-LLM-cost)*

**Tier placement.** ¬ß41.8 Phase 2 candidate (months 12‚Äì18). Closing this ticket retires the ¬ß92.16 thread (3) caveat (Constitutional Classifiers comparison stops being "structural analog" and becomes "empirical parity result"). **The substrate prerequisite is now CLOSED** ‚Äî the remaining work is corpus *scale* (‚â•3,000 inputs via wired LLM MutationProvider + AdversarialReviewer corpus-batch extension) and the empirical parity soak, not the generator itself.

### Slice 86 ‚Äî Adversarial Mutation Harness & Reflection Closure (2026-06-03)

**Honesty invariant:** This measures O+V's OWN threat model ‚Äî adversarial *code patches* attempting to escape the autonomous-dev sandbox (reflection / module-level side effects / dynamic execution). It is NOT a reproduction of Anthropic's Constitutional Classifiers chatbot-jailbreak benchmark (arXiv:2501.18837, 86%‚Üí4.4%); that result is cited as inspiration only, and the threat models differ. No external number is claimed as O+V's.

**Harness:** `governance/graduation/adversarial_sweep.py` (+ CLI `scripts/security/run_adversarial_sweep.py`) composes `build_corpus()` (39 entries: 32 adversarial + 7 clean controls) √ó the 8 deterministic `MutationStrategy` operators through the existing `adversarial_cage.evaluate_entry` ‚Äî no new evaluator, no reimplemented operators. Single-operator-per-seed. The report card attributes the cage's escape rate by category / gate (AST rule, SemanticGuardian) / mutation strategy, tracks mutation-induced escapes, and counts clean-control false positives (using the distinct `clean_passed` verdict, never conflated with `passed_through`).

**Result:** raw adversarial escape rate **7/32 = 21.9% ‚Üí 6/32 = 18.8%** after closing `chr_constructed_attr` with a bounded static constant-folder (chr-of-int / literal concat / "".join / best-effort .format), scoped to banned-name-into-banned-sink, fail-open, with zero clean-control false positives. The prior Apr-2026 31.6% figure was stale: the AST validator (`meta/ast_phase_runner_validator.py`) was already a NodeVisitor with 5 of the 6 original static gaps closed before this slice; the harness establishes the reproducible measurement that proves it.

**Static/runtime boundary (spec criteria for ¬ß43 Arc 5 ‚Äî netns + seccomp):** the residual 6 escapes are runtime-defense gaps, architecturally out of scope for static analysis ‚Äî `run_body_popen`, `run_body_shell_exec`, `run_body_eval`, `run_body_dynamic_compile`, `run_body_importlib`, `multi_step_string_assembly`. A `subprocess.Popen` in a function body cannot be statically proven malicious; these define the requirements for the OS-containment layer.

**Cage known-gap drift ‚Äî RESOLVED (2026-06-04 hygiene follow-up).** The earlier drift (the corpus's `known_gap` flags + `RESULTS.md` not refreshed after the validator hardening closed alias / `__mro__` / vars / dir / globals, plus chr by this slice) is reconciled: all 6 static-shape vectors now carry `known_gap=False` and the cage suite asserts they are BLOCKED; the residual documented known gaps are exactly the 6 runtime-defense gaps; `RESULTS.md` regenerated (39 entries, 6 pass-through = the runtime gaps, 0 open vectors). A new anti-drift guard (`test_known_gap_flags_match_cage_reality_no_stale_pins`) asserts every corpus entry's `known_gap` flag matches the cage's actual verdict, so a future validator hardening that closes a gap can no longer leave a stale "known gap" pin behind.

**Deferred (named follow-ups):** LLM MutationProvider + 3,000-input corpus (¬ß41.11.2 parity-scale), operator chains, the runtime-containment layer itself, and the ¬ß43.6.2 Chaos Monkey daemon (the harness engine is importable by it).

##### ¬ß41.11.2-cal ‚Äî Generative Fuzzing Calibration Baseline (2026-06-05, FIRST live run)

First-ever live execution of the ¬ß40.7.4 self-immunization loop ‚Äî *generate jailbreak mutations to harden the cage* ‚Äî with a real frontier model. Telemetry artifact: `docs/security/telemetry_calibration_v1.json`.

**Honest framing (load-bearing).** This is **calibration, not the parity gate**, and an **analog, not a reproduction**. It measures O+V's own *code-patch sandbox-escape* threat model as a structural analog of Anthropic's Constitutional Classifiers (arXiv:2501.18837); it does **not** reproduce their 86%‚Üí4.4% chatbot-jailbreak result, and **does not yet meet the ‚â•3,000-input ¬ß41.11.2 parity gate** (graduation milestone still open).

**Measured (real data, single run):**
- **236 mutations** (‚âà216 deterministic + ‚âà20 live Claude-generated).
- **Cost: $0.067 total, $0.000283/mutation** ‚Üí full 3,000-input parity run projects to **‚âà$0.85**. The economic barrier to the parity gate is effectively removed. Cost figure is valid; the toolchain artifact affects escape counts, not cost.

**INTEGRITY RESET ‚Äî Slice 95b (2026-06-05).** The original report said "4 escapes = 2.11%." That figure is **not trustworthy and must not be cited as a real bypass rate.** The 4 apparent escapes (`alias_defeats_resolver`, `ctx_mutation_assign`, `dir_introspection`, `module_level_for_with_call`) are **toolchain artifacts**, not real cage bypasses: `adversarial_cage.evaluate_entry` natively blocks all 4 via `blocked_ast` rules (`alias_defeat` / `ctx_mutation` / `introspection_builtin_call` / `module_level_side_effect`), and identity-source == raw-source for each. The cage rules are correct. What happened: the standalone calibration script ran in an execution context where the AST validator flag was not propagated ‚Äî AST-only-defended seeds were not caught by that run. The 2.11% figure is over-counted, measured with an incompletely-active cage. A trustworthy escape rate requires a canary-attested re-run (Slice 95b adds the preflight canary).

**Slice 95b Phase 1 fix ‚Äî cage SG layer was offline.** `adversarial_cage._invoke_semantic_guardian` was reading `Detection.pattern_name` / `Detection.name` (both `None` on all `Detection` objects ‚Äî the attribute is `.pattern`, `semantic_guardian.py:84`). This made `sg_blocked` permanently `False` ‚Äî the SemanticGuardian layer was offline for ALL `evaluate_entry` calls. Fixed in `adversarial_cage.py`: `_invoke_semantic_guardian` now reads `getattr(f, "pattern", None)`. Empirically confirmed: `_invoke_semantic_guardian("sk-ant-api03-...")` returned `()` before, returns `("credential_shape_introduced",)` after. **SG-activation delta:** 4 entries changed `blocked_ast ‚Üí blocked_both` (getattr_string_banned / module_level_eval / module_level_dynamic_import / multi_step_string_assembly); 3 previously-`known_gap` / `passed_through` entries (run_body_eval / run_body_dynamic_compile / run_body_importlib) are now `blocked_semantic_guard` ‚Äî gaps CLOSED. Corpus known-gap count drops from 5 to 2 (only run_body_popen + run_body_shell_exec remain). Clean controls: **0 false positives**. Adversarial escape rate drops from 15.6% to **6.25%** (2/32 seeds). This is a real defense-in-depth improvement: the SG layer was always present in the governance path for live mutations, but the cage's own stress harness was not correctly reading its findings.

**Slice 95b Phase 2 ‚Äî canary routes through the TRUE cage path.** The initial Slice 95b staged work (pre-operator-review) used a direct `SemanticGuardian.inspect()` call to check the SG canary ‚Äî bypassing `_invoke_semantic_guardian` (the workaround for the `.pattern` mismatch). With Phase 1 fixing the root cause, Phase 2 redesigns the canary: a single `_CANARY_COMBINED_SOURCE` trips BOTH cage layers simultaneously (introspection-escape ‚Üí AST Rule 7; credential-shape ‚Üí SG `credential_shape_introduced`). `evaluate_entry` MUST return `blocked_both`. `run_sandbox_integrity_preflight` now calls `evaluate_entry` exclusively ‚Äî no direct SG call. Verified: `--dry-run --max-mutations 4` with `JARVIS_ANTIVENOM_SELF_IMMUNIZATION_ENABLED=true` outputs `[Slice 95b] Preflight PASSED ‚Äî cage stack confirmed active.` and exits `[PASS]`.

**`SandboxIntegrityPanic`** (a `RuntimeError` subclass in `self_immunization.py`) is raised and the campaign is aborted if `evaluate_entry` on the combined canary does not return `blocked_both`. The panic message is `[CRITICAL SECURITY FAULT] Campaign aborted ‚Äî defense-in-depth canary not intercepted (AST validator and/or SemanticGuardian inactive in this context). Escape metrics would be untrustworthy.` The panic includes a diagnosis of which layer is at fault.

**What it proves / doesn't.** Proves: the generative cage-hardening pipeline (Slice 93 engine #69268 + Slice 94 hardening #69269) works end-to-end live, is cheap to scale, and the SG layer is now correctly wired into the cage's stress harness. Does **not** prove: parity-scale robustness, any equivalence to Anthropic's number, Aegis-routed generation, or a trustworthy escape rate for the original calibration run (that requires a fresh canary-attested re-run).

**Architectural note (Aegis leak).** The run used the explicit `--allow-direct` bypass (app-capped at $5) because the Aegis-routed path has a **budget-session gap**: `--bootstrap-aegis` spawns the daemon but never establishes a spend session, so Aegis rejected the lease at $0. Aegis-routed generation through the credential/budget proxy (¬ß43) is a named follow-up ‚Äî generation must not fall back to direct host billing.

**Open to reach the actual ¬ß41.11.2 gate:** (1) Aegis session-lease so generation routes through the proxy; (2) re-run with the Slice 95b canary-attested preflight to obtain a trustworthy escape rate; (3) scale to ‚â•3,000 mutations (~$0.85) for the parity-scale result.

##### ¬ß41.11.2-95c ‚Äî First Aegis-routed scale-attempt + honest dual-metric ledger (2026-06-05)

Closes open items (1) and (2) above; surfaces an honest FAIL on (3). Ledger artifact: `docs/security/production_parity_ledger_v1.json`.

**Open item (1) CLOSED ‚Äî Aegis routing works with real spend.** The ¬ß41.11.2-cal "budget-session gap" (the `--allow-direct` leak) is fixed by **Slice 95a-3** (#69275): `--bootstrap-aegis` now authorizes the daemon's fail-closed (0.0) per-route + session + hourly caps from `--budget-usd` *before* the daemon snapshots them, and `AegisLeaseError` propagates honestly through all three caller layers (was swallowed ‚Üí misdiagnosed as config-starvation). The run below routed **30 generations through the Aegis budget session for $0.919635 real metered spend** ‚Äî no `--allow-direct`, no direct host billing. Canary-attested (Slice 95b preflight `blocked_both` PASSED), so escape metrics are trustworthy.

**Open item (3) ‚Äî honest scale shortfall.** Requested `--max-mutations 3000`; the campaign issues exactly **one `mutate()` call per seed** (30 calls), and a single `max_tokens=2048` response holds ~14 variants ‚Üí **418 LLM mutations, not 3,000** (658 total incl. deterministic). The fix is a per-seed async multi-call batching loop (Slice 95d), not a budget bump.

**Result (658 mutations, canary-attested, Aegis-routed): `6.42%` gross escape ‚Üí FAIL (> 4.4% gate).** The 0.92% seen in a 29-sample probe was a small-sample artifact; 658 is the honest baseline.

**Dual metric (fully computable):** deterministic-operator escape = **0.0%** (the 8 mechanical operators are 100% caged); **all 39 escapes were novel LLM `identity`-strategy variants** ‚Üí novel-LLM-variant escape ‚âà **10.6%**. The cage is impervious to mechanical mutation but is defeated by creative surface-form variation.

**Load-bearing finding ‚Äî these are bypasses of *rule-backed* constructs, not just the 6 runtime gaps.** All 15 escaping families have EXISTING static rules (Rule 8 module-level dangerous calls: shell_exec/subprocess_run/marshal/deserialization_rce; Rule 8 Pattern 2: if/for-with-call; Rule 4: ctx_mutation; AST introspection bans: dir/vars/globals/direct_class/getattr_string; SemanticGuardian.dynamic_import_chain: dynamic_compile/importlib). Three (`alias_defeats_resolver`, `run_body_dynamic_compile`, `run_body_importlib`) are explicitly tagged `known_gap=False` / CLOSED ‚Äî so the LLM variants are **regressions of supposedly-closed gaps**. This corrects an earlier name-based estimate that mislabeled 6 families as architectural "runtime-defense gaps"; reading the corpus falsified it. **Implication:** the deterministic-only 0/38 and 18.8%‚Üí6.25% figures were measured against *literal* corpus forms; an LLM defeats the same rules with surface variation.

**Honest limitation.** The corpus cache persists only metadata (`mutated_source_bytes`, `strategy`, `verdict`) ‚Äî not the escaped source ‚Äî so a rigorous *runtime-vs-static-fixable* per-escape split is not computable from this run. Slice 95d adds escaped-source capture; Slice 95e publishes the split + a true-scale (‚â•3,000) `production_parity_ledger_v2.json`; Slice 95f+ hardens the static-catchable surface forms via dynamic AST tree-walking (no hardcoded string matches), re-measuring after each.

##### ¬ß41.11.2-95e ‚Äî True-scale batched run + honest dual-metric ledger v2 (2026-06-05)

Supersedes the ¬ß41.11.2-95c v1 baseline. Ledger artifact: `docs/security/production_parity_ledger_v2.json`. Batching engine (Slice 95d) + escape-source capture both on.

**Result (1,566 mutations, Aegis-routed $5.27, canary-attested): `8.91%` gross escape ‚Üí FAIL (> 4.4%).** The escape rate ROSE vs v1 (6.42% over 658) ‚Äî a larger, more diverse LLM sample finds more bypasses, so **v1 underestimated**; v2 is the more trustworthy baseline. Dual metric: deterministic-operator escape **0.0%** (the 8 mechanical operators never escaped); all 135 escapes were novel LLM `identity`-strategy variants (~10.6% LLM-only). Budget bound the run at 1,566 (literal ‚â•3,000 ‚âà $10); a transient daemon cold-start `TimeoutError` on the first attempt was non-deterministic (retry succeeded ‚Äî recorded for honesty).

**Rigorous classification (derived by READING the 135 captured escaped sources, not guessing).** The bypasses are dominated by a small set of evasion TECHNIQUES that cut across all 19 escaping families ‚Äî which is why per-family hardcoding would be wrong:
1. **Indirection/aliasing** (defeats name-matching): `[os.system][0]('ls')`, `lambda o,n: getattr(o,n)`, helper-fn wrapping, `setattr(self,'_x',eval)`, aliased-import `_pkl.loads(...)`. Fix: light intra-procedural taint of banned callables/names through assignments/containers/lambdas/setattr/module-aliases into call sinks. *Static-fixable.*
2. **Synonym constructs** (defeats node-type/builtin-name enumeration): list-comprehension vs `for`/`if` statement (Rule 8 P2 only walks statements ‚Äî `module_level_for_with_call`=32 escapes), `inspect.getmembers` vs `dir`, `setattr(ctx,‚Ä¶)` vs `ctx.x=` (Rule 4), `__getattribute__` vs `getattr`. Fix: walk comprehension/genexp nodes; expand introspection-sink + ctx-mutation node sets. *Static-fixable.*
3. **Constant-in-variable** (defeats string-literal matching): `lookup='__subclasses__'; ‚Ä¶__getattribute__(o,lookup)()`. Fix: bounded constant propagation (the Slice 86 folder is the precedent). *Static-fixable.*
4. **Runtime-primitive residual**: eval/deserialize of a runtime-constructed value once the reference is dynamically built ‚Äî the irreducible core is OS-containment (¬ß43 Arc 5 netns+seccomp), not static analysis. *Small residual.*

**Honest conclusion.** The large majority of escapes are static-fixable via three composable AST-tree-walking techniques (no hardcoded matches); a small residual defines the OS-containment boundary. The 135 full-source escapes are captured at `.jarvis/antivenom_escapes.jsonl` (gitignored) for Phase-4 hardening. Roadmap: 95f (synonym constructs ‚Äî lowest-risk, highest-count) ‚Üí 95g (indirection taint) ‚Üí 95h (constant propagation), re-measuring the escape-rate descent after each.

##### ¬ß41.11.2-95e2 ‚Äî LOCKED baseline via cross-run convergence + the honest ">=3,000" correction (2026-06-05)

Ledger: `docs/security/production_parity_ledger_v3.json`. A second true-scale run (llm_per_seed=100, **$12 budget authorized to hit the literal ‚â•3,000 graduation criterion**) produced **1,472 mutations / 8.73% gross escape** while spending only **$5.09 ‚Äî leaving $6.91 (58%) of the budget UNSPENT**.

**The ‚â•3,000 criterion is diversity-bound, not budget-bound.** The binding constraint is LLM surface-variant diversity per seed (~40-50 distinct variants before the model repeats) combined with the Slice 95d AST-hash dedup that correctly rejects repeats. More dollars do NOT buy more unique mutations from a fixed 30-seed corpus; a literal 3,000 needs **corpus expansion (~60-100 seeds)**, an authoring task orthogonal to hardening. Recorded honestly rather than faking a 3,000 figure.

**Baseline now LOCKED by convergence (stronger than one larger run):** v2 (per-seed 50, budget-bound) = 1566 mut / **8.91%**; v3 (per-seed 100, diversity-bound) = 1472 mut / **8.73%**. Two independent samples agree ‚Üí converged gross escape ‚âà **8.8%**, ~2√ó the 4.4% gate ‚Üí FAIL. Both: deterministic operators 100% caged; all escapes novel LLM variants. Saturation point for 30 seeds ‚âà 1470-1570 evaluable mutations. The evasion-technique taxonomy (¬ß41.11.2-95e) is unchanged and confirmed. Hardening proceeds against this locked baseline (95f synonym-constructs ‚Üí 95g indirection-taint ‚Üí 95h constant-propagation).

#### ¬ß41.11.3 ASL-4 recursion-bound stability under sustained autonomy stress

**The claim that needs proving.** Under sustained self-modification activity (i.e., ¬ß41.11.1 graduated and firing regularly), the ¬ß1 Boundary recursion-depth gate (¬ß40.1 #1) + hash-cap on self-modification (¬ß40.1 #2) **hold for ‚â•7 cumulative days of unattended-apply autonomy** without any boundary violation logged.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- ‚úÖ Recursion-depth gate is implemented in `boundary_recursion_gate.py` (per ¬ß40 Wave 2 #5 closure, 2026-05-10).
- ‚úÖ Hash-cap on self-modification proposals exists structurally (¬ß40.1 #2).
- ‚úÖ Unit-tested invariants: gate refuses recursion-depth > N; hash-cap refuses identical-hash mutations within window.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ‚ùå Stability *under stress* ‚Äî gate must hold during sustained autonomous self-modification, not just during synthetic test pressure.
- ‚ùå 7-day continuous operation under autonomy (longest unattended soak to date is hours, not days).
- ‚ùå Recovery behavior when gate fires under real autonomy (synthetic recovery tested; live-fire recovery has never happened because the gate has never fired in production).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).**

- **‚â•7 days cumulative** of unattended-apply autonomy with ¬ß41.11.1 firing (so the gate has actual self-modification activity to constrain, not just idle uptime).
- **Zero ¬ß1 Boundary violations** logged in the audit ledger across the 7-day span.
- **At least one synthetic stress-test** where the gate's recursion-depth threshold is artificially lowered to fire under load, and the recovery path (operator notification + safe-state retreat) runs end-to-end.

**Prerequisites.**

- ¬ß41.11.1 must graduate first (no stress test until self-mods are happening ‚Äî the gate must have actual pressure to demonstrate stability against)
- 7-day unattended-apply session feasibility (current battle-test harness wall-clock cap is hours; needs longer-soak infrastructure or a sequence of stitched-together soaks with state-preservation)
- Synthetic stress-test fixture (one-shot, gated by a dev-only env flag, fires gate intentionally and verifies recovery)

**Tier placement.** ¬ß41.8 Phase 2 candidate (months 12‚Äì18, after ¬ß41.11.1 and concurrent with ¬ß41.11.2). Closing this ticket retires the ¬ß92.16 thread (4) caveat (ASL-4 framework alignment stops being "structural mapping" and becomes "empirical stability result").

#### ¬ß41.11.4 AutoCommitter graduation-evidence gate for unattended apply

**PREMISE CORRECTION (2026-05-14 ‚Äî a ¬ß92.16-class self-audit catch).** The original framing of this ticket said *"AutoCommitter's `JARVIS_AUTO_COMMIT_ENABLED` flag graduates from `default-FALSE` (current) to `default-TRUE`."* **That premise was false** and was an unverified claim authored into this PRD by the same hand that authored ¬ß92.16. `auto_committer.py` line 83 reads `os.environ.get("JARVIS_AUTO_COMMIT_ENABLED", "true")` ‚Äî **the flag is and was already `default-TRUE`.** "Flip the flag" was never the work. This is exactly the class of overclaim ¬ß41.11 exists to retire, caught here in the PRD itself by the same forensic discipline (`git`/source verification, not assertion).

**The claim that actually needs proving (reframed).** The generic `GraduationLedger.is_eligible()` proves *"soaks were clean"* ‚Äî it does **not** prove *"AutoCommitter actually fired on a real Yellow-tier (`NOTIFY_APPLY`) op during those clean soaks."* A soak is clean even if every op was `SAFE_AUTO`, or no op fired, or all commits were human-authored. Graduating AutoCommitter (or asserting ¬ß41.11.1's SICA prerequisite is met) on clean-soak-count alone is a ¬ß92.16-class overclaim. What needs proving is **per-soak-window evidence** that AutoCommitter exercised the unattended-apply path.

**SHIPPED 2026-05-14 ‚Äî the AutoCommitter graduation-evidence gate** (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/auto_commit_graduation_gate.py`, ~700 LOC + 33-test spine + 6 AST pins + 4 FlagRegistry seeds). Closed taxonomies `CommitEvidenceKind` (3) + `AutoCommitEvidenceVerdict` (5: READY / LEDGER_NOT_ELIGIBLE / EVIDENCE_INSUFFICIENT / NO_GIT_HISTORY / MASTER_OFF). **Composes** the canonical `GraduationLedger` clean-soak counter + `_read_all` row reader (never re-implements it) and **adds** the missing evidence layer: genuine per-soak-window `git log` attribution (consecutive clean-`SessionRecord.recorded_at_epoch` define each window; oldest soak bounded by `JARVIS_AUTOCOMMIT_GRAD_LOOKBACK_DAYS`, caveat stated in the diagnostic ‚Äî *claim exactly what the evidence supports*). A soak has evidence iff ‚â•1 commit in its window carries **both** the canonical O+V signature (derived from `auto_committer.ov_signature_substring()` ‚Äî not hardcoded) **and** the Yellow marker (derived as `f"Risk: {RiskTier.NOTIFY_APPLY.name}"` ‚Äî not hardcoded). `READY` only when the generic ledger is eligible **and** every counted clean soak has window evidence; otherwise `EVIDENCE_INSUFFICIENT` with the bare soaks named. git access via `asyncio.create_subprocess_exec` argv-list (no shell, repo standard, AST-pinned). Master `JARVIS_AUTOCOMMIT_GRADUATION_GATE_ENABLED` ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE. Authority-asymmetry AST-pinned (no orchestrator/iron_gate/policy_engine/change_engine/candidate_generator). The gate **NEVER flips the flag** ‚Äî it makes the operator's evidence-driven decision auditable; `evidence_summary().meets_evidence_gate` is the single boolean a REPL/SSE surface reads.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- ‚úÖ `JARVIS_AUTO_COMMIT_ENABLED` is already `default-TRUE` (verified `auto_committer.py:83`).
- ‚úÖ AutoCommitter primitive end-to-end (commit signature, conventional format, risk-tier metadata, protected-branch push prevention).
- ‚úÖ The graduation-evidence gate substrate (this ship) ‚Äî deterministic, fail-closed, 33 tests + 6 AST pins green.

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated.**

- ‚ùå The empirical *evidence* itself ‚Äî i.e. the gate returning `READY` against a real ledger + real git history (requires actual Phase 9 clean soaks where AutoCommitter fired on Yellow-tier ops). The gate is the *instrument*; the soak is the *measurement*. Fabricating the measurement would be the ¬ß92.16 mistake ‚Äî not done.
- ‚ùå Unattended-apply on governance ops (still requires `notify_apply` floor or stricter; that floor relaxation is a separate operator decision the gate informs but does not make).

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** `evaluate_graduation_evidence()` returns `AutoCommitEvidenceVerdict.READY` against the live ledger + live git history ‚Äî meaning generic clean-soak eligibility **and** ‚â•1 Yellow-tier O+V commit in every counted clean-soak window. That verdict (not clean-soak-count, not operator judgment) is the evidence-driven gate for any unattended-apply scope decision.

**Prerequisites.**

- Phase 9 cadence runs producing clean soaks where AutoCommitter fires on real Yellow-tier ops (the measurement the instrument reads ‚Äî operator-paced, cannot be fabricated).
- ¬ß41.11.2 Anti-Venom parity-scale eval *should ideally close first* (soft prerequisite ‚Äî don't widen unattended autonomy before the cage is parity-verified).

**Tier placement.** ¬ß41.8 Phase 1 candidate (months 6‚Äì12). The **substrate prerequisite is now CLOSED** ‚Äî ¬ß41.11.1's SICA demonstration can no longer be claimed on clean-soak-count alone; it must pass this gate's `READY` verdict, which structurally prevents the ¬ß92.16-class overclaim at the graduation boundary.

#### ¬ß41.11.5 Reverse Russian Doll bounded-RSI formal proof (dissertation cross-reference)

**The claim that needs proving.** The Reverse Russian Doll architecture (¬ß3, ¬ß50) is a *sufficient condition* for bounded recursive self-improvement, formalizable as a thermodynamic stability theorem on the (*C*, *S<sub>n</sub>*) system under the Wang RSI formulation (*arXiv:1805.06610*) extended to non-stationary, multi-repository, memory-augmented settings.

**What is empirically demonstrated today.**

- ‚úÖ Conjecture sketch (research paper ¬ß92.17, claims a/b/c).
- ‚úÖ Three Wang RSI extensions identified (non-stationary kernel / coupled multi-chain state / memory-augmented capability score).
- ‚úÖ Thermodynamic-entropy formulation sketched (shell as deterministic-entropy reservoir; core as thermal source; AST-pin invariants as lower bound on shell entropy).

**What is *not* empirically demonstrated (and is not engineering work ‚Äî this is dissertation work).**

- ‚ùå Formal proof of (a) monotone non-decreasing deterministic-entropy of *S<sub>n</sub>*.
- ‚ùå Formal proof of (b) monotone non-increasing conditional semantic-entropy of *C* under *S<sub>n</sub>*.
- ‚ùå Formal proof of (c) net agentic entropy bounded above by a finite constant.
- ‚ùå Empirical validation of the proof against O+V's actual Phase 9 graduation-cadence data.

**Graduation criterion (concrete).** Peer-reviewed publication (or arXiv pre-print + faculty endorsement) of the **bounded-RSI theorem** under the working dissertation title ‚Äî *"Thermodynamic Containment of Agentic Entropy: Formalizing the Reverse Russian Doll Architecture for Bounded Recursive Self-Improvement"*. Acceptance criteria match standard ML-conference / safety-research review (NeurIPS / ICLR / SaTML / Anthropic-aligned venue).

**Prerequisites.**

- Penn MSE-AI program enrollment (in-progress; UPenn application submitted 2026-05-13).
- Dissertation work under Eric Wong (certifiably robust networks) / Aaron Roth (trustworthy-learning) / PRECISE Center lineage.
- ¬ß41.11.1 ‚Äî ¬ß41.11.4 empirical closures provide the *substrate evidence* the proof validates against.

**Tier placement.** ¬ß41.8 Phase 3 (months 18‚Äì30) ‚Äî dissertation-track, not engineering-track. This ticket exists in the PRD as a **placeholder + cross-reference**, not an engineering deliverable. The engineering arc closes at ¬ß41.11.1 ‚Äî ¬ß41.11.4; the formal arc opens with ¬ß41.11.5 and is the dissertation work proper.

### ¬ß43 Resource-Zero Watchdog -- Severing the Kill Path From the Guarded System *(NEW 2026-05-16 -- postmortem bt-2026-05-17-024509)*

**Root cause.** The wall-clock hard-deadline thread (`_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread._watch` in `harness.py`) was a watchdog that *shared resources with the system it guarded*. Session `bt-2026-05-17-024509` proved both legacy escalation layers poisonable:

- **Old Layer 3** sent `SIGTERM` to itself and trusted the harness asyncio signal handler to write a partial summary. Signal delivery was queued behind the same starved interpreter, so the *external* SIGTERM was swallowed -- proven wedgeable.
- **Old Layer 4** emitted a `logging`-module diagnostic and called `_atexit_fallback_write` before `os._exit(75)`. Both acquire the logging lock. A wedged producer held that lock, so the watchdog's own kill path blocked on lock acquisition and never reached the exit. The logging lock *was* the poison.

A watchdog that shares the signal path **and** the logging lock with the system it guards is not a watchdog.

**The fix -- resource-zero collapse.** Layers 3 and 4 are *deleted, not tuned*. Composed within the existing Layer-7 dual-clock skeleton (monotonic + wall anchors untouched), the kill path now touches **nothing a poisoned process can hold**:

1. A raw `os.dup(2)` panic fd captured at *arm time* (pre-wedge), stored as `self._wd_panic_fd`.
2. `os.write` to that fd for every diagnostic -- the startup announce too, never `logging` (a poisoned lock at arm time would deadlock the thread *before* its tripwire is established; `try/except` cannot rescue a blocked acquire).
3. `os.kill(os.getpid(), signal.SIGKILL)` -- never SIGTERM, never the asyncio signal handler, never `call_soon_threadsafe`. `signal` is the module-level import (resident in `sys.modules` before the thread ran) so not even the import lock is touched on the last line of defense.
4. `os._exit(137)` (128 + SIGKILL) as the absolute backstop.

The hard-kill tripwire (`time.monotonic() >= hard_kill_monotonic or time.time() >= hard_kill_wall`) is the **first statement** in the watchdog while-loop, before any poisonable call. The bounded grace still gives the clean asyncio `summary.json` path its full window; only a wedge past the extra margin trips the unblockable SIGKILL. `debug.log` remains the canonical session record per CLAUDE.md -- a partial `summary.json` is not worth a poisonable blocker on the last line of defense.

**Retired config.** `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_ESCALATION_SIGTERM_S` / `..._EXIT_S` tuned the deleted poison path and are retired (named only in a provenance comment -- a no-op knob misleads operators). The resource-zero grace stays operator-tunable and bounded via `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_HARD_KILL_MARGIN_S` (floor 5s / ceiling 300s).

**Spine.** `tests/battle_test/test_phase_d_resource_zero_watchdog.py` -- 8 AST pins enforce the structural invariants (panic fd dup'd at arm time / no `logging` anywhere on `_watch` / no SIGTERM-or-signal-handler / no `_atexit_fallback_write` / raw `write -> kill -> exit` triplet ordered / tripwire-first / dead knobs retired / margin env-bounded) plus a subprocess behavioral proof that the shipped function under a wedged loop terminates with `returncode -9` and emits its `RESOURCE-ZERO HARD KILL` diagnostic on the pre-dup'd fd. The AST pins are load-bearing: a future edit re-introducing `logging`/`SIGTERM`/`_atexit_fallback_write` onto the path would silently re-open the exact poison vector. Postmortem follow-up: a headless soak with `OUROBOROS_BATTLE_MAX_WALL_SECONDS` set proves the kill path under real wedge conditions.

But the resulting history row classified as `outcome=infra` because the partial summary stamped `session_outcome=incomplete_kill` (cause‚Üíoutcome adapter conflated harness-intended terminations with external interruptions) AND the soak classifier's `_SHUTDOWN_NOISE_STOP_REASONS` set included `wall_clock_cap`. **Root cause** ‚Äî TWO compounding design errors: (1) `termination_hook_default_adapters._CAUSE_TO_SESSION_OUTCOME` mapped WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDED ‚Üí `incomplete_kill` (the original Slice 3 design rationale from 2026-05-07 said "every cause that was previously classified as incomplete_kill MUST still map to incomplete_kill" ‚Äî but that pre-dated CLAUDE.md's clean-bar-equivalence footnote which states wall_clock_cap is equivalent to idle_timeout); (2) Slice 7c (2026-05-07) added `wall_clock_cap` to the soak classifier's noise set as a defensive move BEFORE v2.88 Layer 6 atexit fallback shipped ‚Äî post-v2.88, the composite `wall_clock_cap+atexit_fallback` is a graceful-shutdown signature, NOT an external-boundary-cut-us-off signature. The adapter contradicted CLAUDE.md; the classifier conflated harness-intended with external. **Architectural insight**: there are TWO distinct termination classes ‚Äî **external-signal causes** (SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP ‚Äî the harness did NOT intend this termination ‚Üí INFRA correct) and **harness-intended causes** (WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDED ‚Äî the harness's own watchdogs/gates fired per design ‚Üí CLEAN correct). Pre-Layer-8 conflated both under "incomplete_kill" + noise. Layer 8 separates them at the source. **Structural fix ‚Äî 2 seams at source**: (1) `termination_hook_default_adapters.py:153-163` ‚Äî WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDED now stamp `"complete"` (was `"incomplete_kill"`); external-signal causes unchanged; NORMAL_EXIT still None; UNKNOWN still incomplete_kill (safe default). (2) `live_fire_soak.py:380-400` ‚Äî `wall_clock_cap` REMOVED from `_SHUTDOWN_NOISE_STOP_REASONS` (was 5 entries; now 4 entries ‚Äî sigterm/sighup/sigint/harness_idle_timeout; symmetric with idle_timeout which has always been clean per CLAUDE.md). **Why source-discrimination (not downstream filtering)**: alternative considered (special-case branch in classify_outcome for `wall_clock_cap+atexit_fallback`) ‚Äî rejected because it treats a symptom not the root cause, AND because LastSessionSummary parser + audit tooling + future graduation arcs would each need the same filter (duplication). Source-discrimination fixes the marker once; every downstream consumer sees the canonical value. **16 new regression tests** in `tests/battle_test/test_layer8_atexit_marker_discrimination.py`: adapter mapping (6 ‚Äî WALL_CLOCK_CAP/IDLE_TIMEOUT/BUDGET_EXCEEDED‚Üícomplete; SIGTERM/SIGINT/SIGHUP‚Üíincomplete_kill preserved; NORMAL_EXIT‚ÜíNone; UNKNOWN‚Üíincomplete_kill safe default) + classifier noise set (2 ‚Äî wall_clock_cap NOT in set; external signals still in) + forward-fix integration (1 ‚Äî bt-2026-05-10-221432 post-Layer-8 signature classifies CLEAN) + backward-compat (1 ‚Äî legacy incomplete_kill data still routes infra; Layer 8 fixes source not predicate) + **4 AST pins** (adapter cites Layer 8 + v2.96 / classifier cites Layer 8 + v2.96 / WALL_CLOCK_CAP‚Üí"complete" bytes-pinned via ast.Dict walk / noise set size=4). **Existing tests updated** (no regressions): `test_termination_hook_slice3_wiring.py` (24 tests ‚Äî 3 expectation flips + commentary updates); `test_phase_9_slice_7c_composite_stop_reason.py` (32 tests ‚Äî 2 parametrize flips + canonical set assertion update + 2 new tests added: `_layer8_routes_clean` + `_uncomposed_wall_clock_cap_routes_clean`; renamed legacy test to `_legacy_incomplete_kill_routes_infra` to preserve backward-compat semantics). **100 broader regression green** across Layer 6 + Layer 7 + Layer 8 + Slice 3 wiring + Slice 7c composite. **Zero regression** on the cadence-arc spine. **Cadence arc state ‚Äî Layers 1-8 ALL CLOSED**: v2.86 Layers 1-4 (env / wrapper / sentinel / socksio) / v2.87 Layer 5 (modality ledger always loads) / v2.88 Layer 6 (Layer 4 escape-hatch writes partial summary) / v2.92 Layer 7 (dual-clock authority ‚Äî sleep/suspend immunity) / **v2.96 Layer 8 (atexit-fallback marker discrimination)**. The watchdog + atexit fallback + soak classifier now correctly classify wall_clock_cap as a clean harness-intended termination under ALL paths (clean shutdown completes / clean shutdown wedges + atexit fallback fires). `GET /observability/flags` IDE endpoint includes both specs in its bounded projection; Levenshtein typo detection (e.g. `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCIVE_ENABLED` ‚Üí suggests canonical name) + `flag_typo_detected` SSE event; **Version (prior)**: 2.93 (2026-05-10 ‚Äî **Venom V2 Slice 4 SHIPPED ‚Äî IDE GET `/observability/tool-permissions` endpoint suite**: Forward-additive on v2.89 substrate + v2.90 REPL + v2.91 SSE. Completes the ¬ß8 absolute-observability triad for Venom permission decisions: **ring (history) + REPL (operator query) + SSE (real-time push) + GET (browseable HTTP surface)**. **Three new routes** registered on the canonical `IDEObservabilityRouter`: `GET /observability/tool-permissions[?limit=N]` (recent N decisions, default 20 max 200) / `GET /observability/tool-permissions/by-tool/{tool_name}[?limit=N]` (exact-match filter) / `GET /observability/tool-permissions/{op_id}[?limit=N]` (exact-match filter, default 100). **Route-order discipline (AST-pinned via byte-index comparison)** ‚Äî `/by-tool/{tool_name}` MUST register BEFORE `/{op_id}`; aiohttp matches in registration order and the generic op_id pattern would otherwise capture the literal string `by-tool` as an op_id value. The pin `test_route_order_specific_before_parameterized` asserts source-code positional invariant via `src.index(by_tool_path) < src.index(by_op_path)`. **Dual master-flag gating** (no parallel knob): `JARVIS_IDE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED` (graduated default-TRUE 2026-04-20) ‚Üí 403 when off (port-scanner discipline); `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_ENABLED` (default-FALSE per ¬ß33.1) ‚Üí 403 when off. Composed via new `_permission_archive_master_enabled()` static helper that defers to canonical `permission_archive_enabled()` ‚Äî mirrors `_posture_master_enabled()` pattern from v2.84. **Handler shapes mirror canonical posture-health handler** ‚Äî 403 on either master-off, 429 on rate limit, 400 on missing path parameter, 503 on substrate import failure (graceful degradation), 200 + canonical projection on happy path. **Query-param parsing** ‚Äî new `_parse_limit(request, default, ceiling)` static helper extracts `?limit=N` with bounds clamping (floor 1, env-tunable ceiling 200). Falls through to defaults on parse failure. NEVER raises. **Payload schema composes canonical projection** ‚Äî recent route returns `{schema_version, count, snapshot: {capacity/size/next_seq/utilization/schema_version}, records: [{ref, op_id, tool_name, decision_value, decision: {AggregatePermissionDecision projection with schema_version=tool_permission.1}, inserted_at, schema_version=permission_decision_archive.v1}]}`. IDE consumers correlate the event to `/expand p-N` retrieval via the canonical ref. **Surface field** in `/observability/health` extended with `tool-permissions` ‚Äî IDE clients feature-detect against comma-separated list. **Authority asymmetry preserved (AST-pinned)** ‚Äî IDE module imports ONLY `get_default_archive` + `permission_archive_enabled` from substrate. Does NOT import `BoundedDecisionArchive` (ring constructor), `DecisionRecord` (frozen dataclass), or `ArchiveSnapshot` (projection). Composes only `.to_dict()` projection contract ‚Äî preserves ¬ß33.5 frozen-artifact discipline + forward-compat with future schema bumps. AST walker test verifies via `ast.ImportFrom` inspection (not source-string grep ‚Äî docstring mentions are fine). **15 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_ide_observability_tool_permissions.py`: route registration (3 ‚Äî all 3 paths registered / route-order specific-before-parameterized AST-pinned / surface field advertises tool-permissions) + gate enforcement (4 ‚Äî 403 when IDE off / 403 when archive off / 400 on missing tool_name / 400 on missing op_id) + happy path (6 ‚Äî empty archive 200+empty records / recent newest-first / limit query param respected / limit clamped to max / by_tool exact match / by_op exact match) + authority asymmetry (2 ‚Äî read-only contract handlers do not mutate archive / does not import policy substrate types). **Read-only contract** (load-bearing) ‚Äî `test_handlers_do_not_mutate_archive` records baseline `snapshot().to_dict()`, hits all 3 routes, asserts archive's snapshot is byte-identical post-call. Drift = operator-visible signal that someone added a mutating side effect. **129 broader regression green** across all Venom V2 slices + IDE observability. **Cumulative Venom V2 arc state (4 slices)**: v2.89 substrate (31 tests) / v2.90 REPL (31 tests) / v2.91 SSE (11 tests) / v2.93 GET (15 tests) = 88 new tests + 19 AST pins. **Concurrent soak #3** (`bt-2026-05-10-221432`) running while this slice shipped ‚Äî first soak under post-Layer-7 dual-clock watchdog (v2.92). When clean row lands, `JARVIS_EXECUTION_MONITOR_BRIDGE_ENABLED` graduates default-FALSE ‚Üí default-TRUE (first Phase 9 flag graduation). **Files**: `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/ide_observability.py` (3 substantive blocks: register_routes / surface field / new helpers + handlers); `tests/governance/test_ide_observability_tool_permissions.py` (NEW, 15 tests + 2 AST pins). **Why this matters for RSI**: when O+V's proactive curiosity loop scales (e.g., the post-arc soak's "code_gen::.py import_error" intent firing real ops, not just BG-route no-ops), every ALLOW/DENY/ASK/DEFER decision becomes browseable in real time via the IDE extension. Operators can answer "what is the system choosing not to do" without log archaeology. This is the load-bearing pre-requisite for unattended autonomous-development cycles where the operator reviews Venom V2 decisions asynchronously. **NEXT** (operator-paced, each ~30min): Slice 5 FlagRegistry seed for `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_SIZE` env vars; Slice 6 PRD ¬ß37 Tier 2 #6 row flip + ¬ß3.6.3 row üü° ‚Üí ‚úÖ. See v2.92 below for Layer 7 dual-clock authority details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.92 (2026-05-10 ‚Äî **Layer 7 closure ‚Äî WallClockWatchdog dual-clock authority (sleep/suspend immunity) + Soak #2 evidence row landed**: Closes Layer 7 root cause exposed by live-fire soak `bt-2026-05-10-093428`. **Soak #2 result (2nd Phase 9 evidence row)**: `outcome=clean ops=16 cost=$0.0000 stop_reason=idle_timeout duration_s=39838 (~11h actual wall-clock ‚Äî see Layer 7 below)`. **Phase 9 ladder progress**: `JARVIS_EXECUTION_MONITOR_BRIDGE_ENABLED` now at **2 of 3 clean rows**. One more soak ‚Üí flag graduates default-FALSE ‚Üí default-TRUE. **v2.86‚Äìv2.88 cadence-arc verified under second live-fire run**: Layer 1-4 (env / wrapper / sentinel / socksio) held; Layer 5 (modality ledger) held; Layer 6 (watchdog Layer 4 escape-hatch summary write) held ‚Äî `summary.json` written cleanly before `ShutdownWatchdog os._exit(75)` fired at terminal; `session_id` linked correctly in history row; soak harness produced parseable evidence. v2.89-v2.91 Venom V2 stack not exercised during BG-only soak (no STANDARD route ops escalated to permission evaluator). **Layer 7 root cause** (newly diagnosed during forensics): the soak ran 11h instead of its 40-min cap because `time.monotonic()` on macOS is backed by `mach_absolute_time()`, which **pauses while the CPU is halted** (host sleep/suspend). The laptop slept ~10.5h between 02:55 PDT and 13:36 PDT; monotonic advanced 1892s (~31.5 min); wall-clock advanced 39798s (~11h 03min); the cap (2400s monotonic) was never reached. Heartbeat trail: tick=48 at 10:59 PDT showed elapsed=1603s; tick=60 at 13:36 PDT showed elapsed=1790s ‚Äî only 187s monotonic advance over 2h 36min wall-clock = 2% clock efficiency. The hard-deadline thread (Slice B safety net from 2026-05-03 Defect #1 fix) uses the same `time.monotonic()` so it was paused identically. The watchdog's stated contract is "wall-clock cap" (cf. `_monitor_wall_clock` docstring: "Opaque ceiling on total session duration ... wall-clock elapsed time") ‚Äî monotonic-only enforcement violates that contract whenever the host suspends. **Structural fix at `harness.py`**: composes BOTH `time.monotonic()` AND `time.time()` anchors at task/thread entry; takes `max(elapsed_monotonic, elapsed_wall)` as effective elapsed. **NTP-rollback-safety preserved** (wall jumps backward ‚Üí wall < monotonic ‚Üí max picks monotonic). **Forward NTP jump handled** (max picks wall ‚Äî fires cap earlier than intended; acceptable for soak semantics where operator wanted "kill after N seconds of real time"). **Sleep/suspend handled** (monotonic paused ‚Üí max picks wall ‚Üí cap fires correctly on wake or earlier if wall already exceeded cap during sleep). Both `_monitor_wall_clock` (asyncio path) AND `_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread` (Slice B thread path) modified; thread uses `min(remaining_monotonic, remaining_wall)` so whichever clock approaches the deadline first wins. Diagnostic log line in the thread's fire path includes `won_clock` field for operator-visible forensics. **Env-tunable skew warning** `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_SKEW_WARN_THRESHOLD_S` (default 60s, floor 5s, ceiling 3600s) ‚Äî when `elapsed_wall - elapsed_monotonic` exceeds threshold, log a debounced warning ("clock skew detected: monotonic=Ns wall=Ms skew=Ks (host sleep/suspend OR forward NTP jump). Treating wall-clock as cap-authoritative."). Debounce ensures sustained sleep doesn't spam the log every tick; re-warns only after another threshold-worth of additional drift. Cancel-path diagnostic upgraded: when async monitor cancelled (peer waiter wins FIRST_COMPLETED race), "NEVER fired" log line now reports BOTH monotonic and wall elapsed ‚Äî load-bearing for retrospective sleep-event forensics. **Canonical AST invariant updated** (`register_shipped_invariants`, harness.py:5194-5310): added `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_SKEW_WARN_THRESHOLD_S` to `REQUIRED_LITERALS` ‚Äî guarantees the skew-warn path doesn't regress to monotonic-only enforcement. AST walker now also detects `time.monotonic()` AND `time.time()` Attribute calls inside `_monitor_wall_clock` body ‚Äî violations raised if either is missing. **10 regression tests** in `tests/battle_test/test_layer7_dual_clock_watchdog.py`: 3 monitor-side pins (calls time.monotonic / calls time.time / composes via max()) + 2 thread-side pins (anchors both clocks + computes both deadlines / uses min() over remainings) + 1 env-knob pin (skew threshold referenced) + 1 canonical-validator green + **3 provenance pins** (cites v2.92 / cites bt-2026-05-10-093428 / cites mach_absolute_time). **Why no mocked functional test**: mocking `time.monotonic` AND `time.time` simultaneously interferes with `asyncio.sleep`'s internal scheduling (which uses `time.monotonic` for delay accounting). The event loop hangs. AST pins are the load-bearing guarantee ‚Äî drift in dual-clock composition is structurally detected; the `max(elapsed_monotonic, elapsed_wall)` literal is byte-pinned in source. **Test results**: 10 Layer 7 tests + 6 Layer 6 tests + 63 broader watchdog/wall-clock/partial-shutdown/termination-hook tests = **79 green, zero regression**. **Cadence arc state (Layers 1‚Äì7 all CLOSED)**: v2.86 Layers 1-4 (env / wrapper / sentinel / socksio); v2.87 Layer 5 (modality ledger always loads); v2.88 Layer 6 (Layer 4 escape-hatch writes partial summary); **v2.92 Layer 7 (dual-clock authority ‚Äî sleep/suspend immunity)**. The watchdog now honors its "wall-clock cap" contract under ALL clock-skew scenarios (NTP step forward, NTP step backward, NTP slew, host sleep/suspend, host hibernate, virtualization-induced clock drift). **Why this matters for RSI**: graduation soaks run under operator-paced cadence. If the operator's laptop sleeps mid-soak, the watchdog MUST still enforce the cap so the soak doesn't run 11 hours and burn budget on idle ops. Layer 7 makes the cadence robust to host-side environmental conditions outside the harness's direct control ‚Äî the load-bearing pre-requisite for unattended Phase 9 graduation cycles. Operator binding 2026-05-10 satisfied verbatim: solved root problem directly (composed both clocks at the seam where elapsed is computed; preserved monotonic's NTP-safety AND added wall's sleep-detection); no workarounds (did NOT add a "wake detector" that retroactively adjusts elapsed; did NOT switch entirely to wall-clock ‚Äî would lose NTP safety; composition via max() is the canonical resolution); no shortcuts (6 AST pins + 3 provenance pins + 1 canonical validator update; drift to single-clock structurally caught); composes existing canonical paths (existing periodic-check loop preserved from Defect #1 2026-05-03; existing hard-deadline thread structure preserved; existing skew-warn env-knob pattern mirrors `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_CHECK_INTERVAL_S` and `JARVIS_WALL_CLOCK_HARD_DEADLINE_GRACE_S`); no hardcoding (skew threshold env-tunable; existing knobs unchanged); no duplication (single seam at `_monitor_wall_clock` + single seam at `_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread`; thread mirrors asyncio path exactly). **Files modified**: `backend/core/ouroboros/battle_test/harness.py` (3 substantive blocks: `_monitor_wall_clock` ~line 4561 dual-clock anchors + max-based effective_elapsed + skew warning + dual-clock diagnostics; `_start_wall_clock_hard_deadline_thread` ~line 4347 dual-clock anchors + dual deadlines + min(remaining) + dual-clock fire log; `register_shipped_invariants` ~line 5194 skew-threshold required-literal + dual-clock AST validation); `tests/battle_test/test_layer7_dual_clock_watchdog.py` (NEW, 10 regression tests). **NEXT** (operator-paced): (a) run `--once` for soak #3 ‚Äî final clean row to graduate `JARVIS_EXECUTION_MONITOR_BRIDGE_ENABLED` (Phase 9 first flag graduation under post-Layer-7 watchdog); (b) Venom V2 Slice 4 IDE GET `/observability/tool-permissions` (~1.5h); (c) Venom V2 Slice 5 FlagRegistry seed (~30min); (d) Venom V2 Slice 6 PRD ¬ß37 Tier 2 #6 row flip (~30min). See v2.91 below for Venom V2 Slice 3 (SSE event) details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.91 (2026-05-10 ‚Äî **Venom V2 Slice 3 SHIPPED ‚Äî SSE event `permission_decision_recorded` + producer-bridge wiring**: Forward-additive on v2.89 substrate + v2.90 REPL surfaces. **Slice scope** ‚Äî SSE producer-bridge for the canonical IDE event stream; IDE GET endpoint + FlagRegistry seed + PRD ¬ß37 Tier 2 #6 row flip deferred to Slices 4-6. **Event-type registration** in `ide_observability_stream.py` (the canonical broker substrate from Gap #6 Slice 2 + v2.84 sibling pattern): new `EVENT_TYPE_PERMISSION_DECISION_RECORDED = "permission_decision_recorded"` constant defined alongside existing event-type constants; **added to the `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset** ‚Äî load-bearing because the broker's pre-publish validation rejects any event type NOT in this set (mirrors the v2.84 fix where `POSTURE_OBSERVER_DEGRADED` was defined locally but missing from the frozenset ‚Üí silent publish-rejection failure mode). Comment block cites Slice 3 + PRD v2.91 + master-flag interaction. **Producer-bridge ¬ß33.2** in `permission_decision_archive.maybe_record_decision`: after `archive.record()` returns a non-None record, attempts to publish the canonical `record.to_dict()` projection via the existing `publish_task_event(event_type, op_id, payload)` bridge ‚Äî composes the existing canonical bridge (ZERO parallel publish path, ZERO direct broker-coupling). **Positional invariant (AST-pinned)**: the publish call MUST come AFTER `archive.record()` ‚Äî load-bearing because publishing before would emit a stale ref / phantom record. **Defensive try/except (AST-pinned)**: broker exceptions MUST NOT propagate into the policy substrate; the policy path is fail-silent by contract. **Dual-master-flag composition (no parallel knob)**: archive master flag `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_ENABLED` (default-FALSE per ¬ß33.1) gates `record()` first ‚Üí archive-disabled means no record + no publish; stream master flag `JARVIS_IDE_STREAM_ENABLED` (graduated default-TRUE 2026-04-20 Gap #6 Slice 2) gates `publish_task_event` second ‚Üí stream-disabled means record happens but publish is no-op. Both flags compose deterministically: `record_attempted = archive_enabled`, `publish_attempted = (archive_enabled AND record_succeeded)`, `publish_observed = (publish_attempted AND stream_enabled)`. **Payload schema** composes the canonical `DecisionRecord.to_dict()` projection: `ref` (`p-N`), `op_id` / `tool_name` / `decision_value` (allow/deny/ask/defer), `decision` (full `AggregatePermissionDecision.to_dict()` projection ‚Äî schema_version `tool_permission.1`, detail, deny_callbacks, ask_callbacks, total_callbacks), `inserted_at` / `schema_version` (`permission_decision_archive.v1`). IDE consumers can correlate the SSE event to a `/expand p-N` retrieval via the canonical ref. **11 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_permission_decision_sse.py`: 2 event-type registration pins (canonical literal value + presence in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset) + 4 producer-bridge contract tests (publish fires when both gates ON / silenced when stream OFF / silenced when archive OFF / payload carries canonical projection with deny + detail + schema_version) + 1 best-effort contract test (broker exception via mocked `publish_task_event.side_effect` MUST NOT propagate; archive write still succeeds) + **4 AST pins** (`EVENT_TYPE_PERMISSION_DECISION_RECORDED` constant present at module scope with canonical literal / constant appears in the `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset literal ‚Äî drift here is the same silent-drop failure mode v2.84 fixed / `maybe_record_decision` invokes `publish_task_event` AFTER `archive.record()` (positional invariant) / publish call wrapped in try/except (best-effort contract enforced structurally)). **137 broader regression green** across 5 files (`test_ide_observability_stream.py` + `test_section_37_tier1_2_posture_health_wiring.py` + `test_permission_decision_archive.py` Slice 1 + `test_tool_permissions_repl.py` Slice 2 + `test_permission_decision_sse.py` Slice 3) ‚Äî zero regression on the canonical broker substrate or on v2.84's POSTURE_OBSERVER_DEGRADED path (which is the canonical pattern v2.91 follows). **Cumulative Venom V2 arc state** (3 slices shipped tonight): Slice 1 (v2.89) substrate ring + producer-bridge + tool_executor seam wiring (31 tests); Slice 2 (v2.90) REPL verb auto-discovered + /expand p-N 5th cross-substrate prefix (31 tests); Slice 3 (v2.91) SSE event registration + producer-bridge to broker (11 tests); total 73 new regression tests + 16 AST pins across the 3 slices. **Why this matters for RSI**: the SSE event makes Venom permission decisions **observable in real time** ‚Äî when O+V's proactive curiosity loop fires tool calls at scale (e.g., the post-arc soak `bt-2026-05-10-082106`'s "code_gen::.py import_error" intent that fired 2026-05-10 02:43), operators see every ALLOW/DENY/ASK/DEFER event in the IDE stream as it lands. Without this, the policy boundary between proactive ops and operator authority is invisible at runtime. The combination of v2.89's ring (history) + v2.90's REPL (operator query) + v2.91's SSE (real-time push) gives the full observability stack ‚Äî exactly the ¬ß8 absolute-observability mandate from the manifesto. **Files**: `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/ide_observability_stream.py` (+18 LOC: constant + comment block + frozenset entry); `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/permission_decision_archive.py` (+22 LOC inside existing `maybe_record_decision`: defensive try/except wrapping `publish_task_event` call after the archive write succeeds); `tests/governance/test_permission_decision_sse.py` (NEW, 11 tests + 4 AST pins). **Version (prior)**: 2.89 (2026-05-10 ‚Äî **Venom V2 observability substrate ‚Äî `permission_decision_archive` Slice 1 SHIPPED**: First post-cadence-arc forward-progress slice. Closes the ¬ß37 v10 brutal review's "tool_permission.py substrate-unwired" gap with a thin composition layer. **Pre-build audit** (Explore agent verification of ¬ß37 Tier 2 catalog before writing any code): 6 of 7 Tier 2 items already SHIPPED (`--rerun-from`+`/replay` v2.40, session search via `session_browser`+`/history`, op-graph via `op_fanout_tree`+`/fanout`, per-tool confidence via `tool_confidence_*` graduation contract, Operation Modes via `operation_mode`+`/mode`, Pattern C via `component_tool_scope`+`/scope`); only genuine gap was Venom V2 ‚Äî `tool_permission.py` substrate exists + composed by `tool_executor._maybe_evaluate_tool_permission` but no operator-facing query surface. **Critical pre-build collision-avoidance**: confirmed `inline_permission_observability.py` (default-TRUE, `/observability/permissions/{prompts,grants}`) is a DIFFERENT concern (mid-prompt human-in-the-loop interactive permissions with prompt queues + remembered allows) ‚Äî Venom V2's `tool_permission.py` is the per-tool-callback policy chain that runs BEFORE V1 PRE_TOOL_USE hook fires. Naming-collision avoided via `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_*` (not `JARVIS_PERMISSION_*`) + `p-N` ref scheme + `/tool-permissions` REPL verb (deferred to Slice 2). **Slice 1 substrate** ‚Äî new `governance/permission_decision_archive.py` (~440 LOC pure-stdlib) mirrors the canonical `BoundedBodyStore` pattern from `tool_render_store.py` (Gap #2 Slice 3): closed `DecisionRecord` (frozen ¬ß33.5 with symmetric `to_dict`) + `ArchiveSnapshot` (capacity / size / next_seq / utilization). `BoundedDecisionArchive` thread-safe FIFO with `threading.RLock` + `OrderedDict` + drop-oldest eviction at capacity + monotonic `p-N` refs (counter NEVER rewinds ‚Äî load-bearing safety: a printed ref always resolves to the same decision OR None, never a different decision). Filtering APIs: `recent(limit)` newest-first / `by_tool(name)` exact-match / `by_op(op_id)` exact-match. `permission_archive_enabled()` master flag `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_ENABLED` default-FALSE per ¬ß33.1 graduation contract; `JARVIS_PERMISSION_ARCHIVE_SIZE` env knob (default 50, bounds [1, 10000]). Singleton via `get_default_archive()` + `reset_default_archive_for_tests()`. **Producer-bridge ¬ß33.2** ‚Äî `maybe_record_decision(*, op_id, tool_name, decision)` single-call composition wrapper; master-flag-gated; NEVER raises into the policy path; defensive try/except swallows exceptions. **Wired at single seam** in `tool_executor.py:1218‚Äì1232` AFTER `evaluate_tool_permission` returns the decision ‚Äî composes the existing `_maybe_evaluate_tool_permission` wrapper rather than creating a parallel evaluation path; defensive try/except so a fallback bug never breaks tool dispatch. **Authority asymmetry preserved (AST-pinned)** ‚Äî archive module does NOT import `compute_permission_decision` / `PermissionRegistry` / `evaluate_tool_permission` / `ToolPermissionCallback`; archive composes `AggregatePermissionDecision.to_dict()` projection via duck-typing ‚Äî preserves the ¬ß33.5 frozen-artifact contract without coupling. Decision-value extracted via `getattr(decision.decision, 'value', ...)` first-match-wins; foreign shapes fall through to safe DEFER default (zero-raise contract). **31 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_permission_decision_archive.py`: master-flag contract (default-False / canonical truthy alternation `("1","true","yes","on")` / no-op when off / producer-bridge no-op when off ‚Äî 13 tests including parametrized) + ring contract (p- prefix / drop-oldest eviction / monotonic refs never rewind even after `clear()` ‚Äî 3 tests) + filtering API (recent newest-first / by_tool exact-match / by_op exact-match ‚Äî 3 tests) + projection contract (DecisionRecord.to_dict carries full inner projection / ArchiveSnapshot.to_dict shape ‚Äî 2 tests) + fail-closed contract (invalid lookup returns None / malformed decision shape doesn't raise / producer-bridge swallows exceptions ‚Äî 3 tests) + singleton lifecycle (same instance / reset drops singleton / capacity env var respected ‚Äî 3 tests) + **4 AST pins** (REF_PREFIX canonical literal `"p-"` / master-flag default-false structural via AST sweep + canonical 4-tuple alternation / no-policy-imports authority asymmetry / `record()` first-statement-master-flag-shortcircuit) + **1 tool-executor wiring pin** (load-bearing seam: `_maybe_evaluate_tool_permission` MUST invoke `maybe_record_decision` AND import from canonical `permission_decision_archive` module ‚Äî drift would silently disable the observability surface). **301 broader permission-related governance tests still green** ‚Äî zero regression on existing `test_venom_v2_slice_1_tool_permission.py` (the substrate that v2.89 hooks into). **Why ship substrate-only first**: per CLAUDE.md "Don't add features beyond what the task requires" + operator binding "fully leverage existing files; avoid duplication". Slice 1 is a complete, parseable, testable closure on its own. When the system DOES start acting on its own curiosity signals at scale, `tool_permission.py` becomes the policy boundary between proactive ops and operator authority. Without observability into that boundary's decisions, operator has no signal of what the system is choosing not to do.
