See v2.88 below for cadence-arc Layer 6 closure details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.88 (2026-05-10 ‚Äî **Layer 6 closure ‚Äî wall-clock watchdog Layer 4 escape-hatch now writes partial summary.json before os._exit(75)**: Closes the final cadence-arc layer. **Diagnosis**: when the asyncio cleanup path wedged after wall-clock cap fires (provider streams stuck, slow `_shutdown_components`, etc.), the wall-clock watchdog escalated through 4 layers ending with `os._exit(75)` (line 4476). `os._exit` bypasses `atexit`, so Wave 3 v2.79's "partial-shutdown insurance" never fired ‚Üí session dir contained only `debug.log` ‚Üí `_read_most_recent_session` linkage failed ‚Üí harness wrote `outcome=infra session=unknown` for every wall-clock-cap-induced soak (the operator-visible cadence pattern: 281/379 historical sessions had summary.json but the recent failure cohort had ZERO). Per-session audit confirmed: clean exits via SIGTERM (Layer 3) DID get atexit-fired summaries; only Layer 4-escalated exits had the gap. **Root cause**: the watchdog thread runs OUTSIDE the asyncio loop (specifically because the loop is wedged), so it has the unique capability to write sync I/O even when the rest of the harness is frozen. But it didn't take the opportunity. **Structural fix at `harness.py:4475-4504`** (just before `os._exit(75)` at line 4506): invoke the existing canonical `_atexit_fallback_write(session_outcome="incomplete_kill_layer4")` synchronously from the watchdog thread. The fallback is already designed for partial-shutdown contexts (Wave 3 v2.79: "this writer is pure-sync and defensive ... we can't trust the event loop or many imports") so it composes cleanly. New `incomplete_kill_layer4` outcome marker (distinct from Wave 3's `incomplete_kill` from Ticket B's signal-driven path) for operator-greppable audit. **Defensive try/except** wraps the fallback call so a fallback bug NEVER blocks the load-bearing escape hatch from firing ‚Äî `os._exit(75)` always runs even if `_atexit_fallback_write` raises. **Composes existing canonical primitives**: zero parallel summary writer, zero new state machinery, zero new env knob. The watchdog thread is already writing log lines via `_wd_log` and reading `self.<state>`; adding a sync method invocation is structurally additive. **6 regression tests** in `tests/battle_test/test_layer6_watchdog_layer4_summary.py`: (1) **AST positional pin** ‚Äî `_atexit_fallback_write` is invoked WITHIN the Layer 4 block AND BEFORE the `os._exit(75)` statement (load-bearing ‚Äî writing AFTER the kill is unreachable); regex anchored on `# ‚îÄ‚îÄ Layer 4: os._exit` code marker (not the log-message string `"LAYER 4 ESCALATION..."`) + `try:\\s*os\\._exit\\(75\\)` actual statement; uses `block.rindex("os._exit(75)")` to find the executable call (not the substring inside the log message above the fallback). (2) **outcome marker pin** ‚Äî `incomplete_kill_layer4` literal must appear in the block (operator audit). (3) **defensive wrapping pin** ‚Äî fallback call must be inside try/except (a fallback bug must not block the escape hatch). (4) **functional integration** ‚Äî invoke `_atexit_fallback_write(session_outcome="incomplete_kill_layer4")` directly + assert summary.json gets written with the load-bearing `session_id` field populated (without it, soak-harness linkage falls back to `"unknown"` literal string). (5) **idempotence** ‚Äî when clean path already wrote summary, fallback is no-op (Layer 3 SIGTERM may trigger atexit which writes; Layer 4 must NOT clobber). (6) **provenance pin** ‚Äî Layer 4 block cites `Layer 6` + `v2.88` so future readers find the design doc. **Test results**: 6/6 + 25 wall-clock-cap + partial-shutdown tests still green (no regression). Operator binding 2026-05-10 satisfied verbatim: solved root problem directly (the missing summary.json IS the link-failure cause, fixed at the source), no workarounds (we did NOT add a parallel summary path or move `os._exit` ‚Äî preserved the canonical escape hatch + composed the canonical fallback writer), no shortcuts (load-bearing positional invariant pinned via AST), composes existing `_atexit_fallback_write` substrate (Wave 3 v2.79), preserves operator-override discipline (no new env knob beyond what already exists). **Cadence arc COMPLETE**: with v2.86 + v2.87 + v2.88 in place, the live-fire soak harness produces parseable evidence even from wall-clock-cap-induced exits + the modality ledger correctly informs catalog ranking + the wrapper carries proxy/sentinel/.env semantics across all cadence paths. Next soak should produce its first cost-positive `outcome=clean` row when the operator chooses to run it. **Files modified in this slice**: `backend/core/ouroboros/battle_test/harness.py` (Layer 4 escape-hatch + fallback invocation, lines 4475-4504); `tests/battle_test/test_layer6_watchdog_layer4_summary.py` (NEW, 6 regression tests). See v2.87 below for Layer 5 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.87 (2026-05-10 ‚Äî **Layer 5 closure ‚Äî modality ledger now always loads (separated from verification probing)**: Closes the dominant cause of the 75% topology-blanket-block pattern observed in v2.86's verification soak. **Diagnosis** (deeper than v2.86): the topology sentinel's `lightonai/LightOnOCR-2-1B-bbox-soup` + `allenai/olmOCR-2-7B-1025-FP8` were correctly in TERMINAL_OPEN (403-deny + timeout terminal verdicts) but were nonetheless leading the BG/STANDARD candidate list at the sentinel dispatch site ‚Äî exhausted before the canonical `Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8` (CLOSED, healthy, accessible) could be tried. **Root cause**: `dw_discovery_runner._get_or_create_modality_ledger` at lines 592-611 conflated TWO orthogonal concerns under a single env flag (`JARVIS_DW_MODALITY_VERIFICATION_ENABLED`, default-FALSE): (a) loading the disk ledger of pre-classified verdicts (cheap pure-I/O ‚Äî should always be safe), and (b) probing new models live (expensive HTTP ‚Äî gated correctly). With the master flag off (default), the disk ledger's 22 pre-existing verdicts (14 CHAT_CAPABLE including Qwen3.5-397B + 8 UNKNOWN ‚Äî populated by prior verification cycles + persisted at `.jarvis/dw_modality_ledger.json`) were silently ignored. The classifier ran in legacy mode that ranked TERMINAL_OPEN models at the top. Sentinel dispatch exhausted them before reaching healthy candidates. **Structural fix** at `dw_discovery_runner.py:592-616`: separated the two concerns. Ledger loading is now unconditional (only short-circuited by explicit `JARVIS_DW_MODALITY_LEDGER_DISABLE=1` escape hatch for diagnostic isolation). Probing remains gated by the existing `modality_verification_enabled()` check at the canonical call site (`run_discovery` Step 1.5 lines 202-205) ‚Äî that gate is unchanged, so the expensive HTTP work still requires explicit operator opt-in. Verified via direct repro: `_get_or_create_modality_ledger()` returns a populated ledger with `has_record('Qwen/Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8') == True`. **149 governance modality+discovery+catalog_classifier tests pass** with the fix in place ‚Äî no regression. Operator binding 2026-05-10 satisfied verbatim: composes existing canonical primitives (zero parallel ledger machinery), separates conflated concerns (zero new env knob beyond an additive escape hatch), preserves operator-override discipline (explicit disable still works). **What v2.87 does NOT close** (Layer 6, pending v2.88): 0-cost session-id linkage failure. When the battle-test subprocess exits via wall-clock cap, `_read_most_recent_session`'s mtime-sort finds the session dir but `summary.json` isn't always written before SIGTERM/SIGKILL ‚Äî harness writes terminal row as `outcome=infra session=unknown`. The partial-shutdown insurance from Wave 3 v2.79 should cover this but apparently isn't firing in the wall-clock-cap exit path. Multi-hour investigation requiring instrumentation of atexit ordering + signal handler write paths + battle-test's own exit hook chain. **NEXT** (cadence arc remaining): Layer 6 deep-dive (atexit + signal handler write paths under wall-clock-cap exit) ‚Äî fresh-eyes session required. Once Layer 6 closes, the cadence should produce clean evidence at the first soak. See v2.86 below for the original 4-layer connectivity arc.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.86 (2026-05-10 ‚Äî **Cadence connectivity discipline ‚Äî 4 structural fixes for live-fire soak harness**: Closes the operator-paced cadence's silent-zero-cost / DW-blocked-by-topology runner-failure pattern that bit cron #1 on 2026-05-06 and every manual `--once` invocation since. **Discovery sequence** (5+ hour debug arc): operator launched first verification `--once` 2026-05-09 21:35 PDT; soak ran 30s then idle through wall-clock cap producing 0 ops + $0 cost (RUNNER classification). Iterative diagnosis peeled back **4 distinct structural layers**, each requiring its own fix; tonight's PRD entry captures the substrate work so the architecture is robust regardless of operator path (cron / launchd / `--once` / manual) and parent-process environment (Claude Code agent / IDE / terminal / CI). **Layer 1 ‚Äî `.env` not loading into harness subprocess**: canonical providers (`doubleword_provider.py:43-44`, `providers.py`) read `os.environ` at module load with no `load_dotenv()` call; the wrapper's env-block boundary is the canonical source-of-truth per the install script's design comment ("single source of truth for the env block") but it didn't source `.env`. **Fix at `scripts/run_live_fire_graduation_soak.sh:24-77`**: bash 3.2-portable `.env` loader using `case`/glob (not bash 4 regex ‚Äî macOS frozen at 3.2.57; `[!chars]` is literal in 3.2 so we use `[^chars]` POSIX form) + `eval "x=\${$key:-}"` indirect lookup (avoids the bash 4.2+ `${!var:-default}` combinator); validates KEY shape via two-stage `case` glob (`[A-Z_]*` head + `*[^A-Z0-9_]*` body negation). Operator-override discipline preserved: explicit shell-set values WIN over `.env`. **Layer 2 ‚Äî `--once` bypasses the wrapper's env-block**: `install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh::run_once` inlined a duplicate of the wrapper's env block, so the launchd path used the wrapper but `--once` did not. **Fix at `install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh:255-280`**: `run_once` now delegates to `WRAPPER_SCRIPT` (which the launchd plist already references at line 323) ‚Äî single env-block source of truth honored across all cadence paths; one inline duplicate eliminated. Cadence-kind enum bug caught (`once` ‚Üí `adhoc` matching the wrapper's own preflight default). **Layer 3 ‚Äî `JARVIS_TOPOLOGY_SENTINEL_ENABLED` defaults to FALSE**: the YAML `brain_selection_policy.yaml` has `dw_allowed: false` for every route (intentional ‚Äî Phase 12 Slice E graduation purged YAML routing in favor of dynamic catalog from `dw_catalog_classifier`). The v2 dynamic-catalog path through `provider_topology.is_dw_blocked_for_route` is gated by THIS flag; without it, the v1 path reads YAML's `dw_allowed: false` and blocks every DW op. Catalog discovery completes correctly (`routes_assigned=['background','complex','speculative','standard']`) but the orchestrator never consults it because the sentinel flag is off ‚Üí falls through to v1 yaml ‚Üí all DW routes blocked. **Fix at `scripts/run_live_fire_graduation_soak.sh:104` (post-`.env`-load)**: `export JARVIS_TOPOLOGY_SENTINEL_ENABLED="${JARVIS_TOPOLOGY_SENTINEL_ENABLED:-true}"` ‚Äî operator-override discipline preserved (`=false` shell-set still wins). Verified end-to-end: `[CandidateGenerator] Phase 10 sentinel preflight: healthy=True schema=topology.2 routes_with_dw_models=['standard','complex','background','speculative']`. **Layer 4 ‚Äî `socksio` not installed ‚Üí httpx 0.28+ ImportError on every Claude API call**: when the harness runs under a parent process whose network tunnel is `socks5h://localhost:N` (Claude Code agent sandboxing ‚Äî surfaced as `ALL_PROXY=socks5h://...` in the spawned subprocess env), httpx 0.28+ raises `Using SOCKS proxy, but the 'socksio' package is not installed. Make sure to install httpx using \`pip install httpx[socks]\`.` on every ClaudeProvider call ‚Üí fallback path fails ‚Üí ops stall waiting for either DW or Claude to recover. **First fix attempt (rejected as workaround)**: stripped proxy env vars in the wrapper. Caused `ClientConnectorDNSError: Cannot connect to host api.doubleword.ai:443 [nodename nor servname provided, or not known]` because the SOCKS proxy IS the network path under sandboxed agents ‚Äî stripping it broke DNS resolution. **Correct structural fix** ships in v2.86: `socksio==1.0.0` pinned in `requirements.txt:158` (next to `httpx==0.28.1` with provenance comment citing the sandbox-tunnel rationale). Wrapper preserves the inherited proxy block; httpx 0.28 now accepts the `socks5h://` scheme without raising. Verified end-to-end: `[ClaudeProvider] ‚Üí stream model=claude-sonnet-4-6 timeout=196.0s max_tokens=16384 temp=1.0 thinking=on` ‚Äî Claude streams flow through the SOCKS5h tunnel cleanly. **What still doesn't work** (deeper than v2.86 closes ‚Äî pending v2.87 investigation arc): topology sentinel still **blanket-blocks DW under load** even with all 4 fixes ‚Äî when models hit transient failures (`/v1/files` ConnectionTimeoutError, heavy probe ConnectorTimeout on `moonshotai/Kimi-K2.6` / `deepseek-ai/DeepSeek-OCR-2`), the sentinel marks DW unhealthy and routes subsequent BG/STANDARD ops as `background_dw_blocked_by_topology` despite Qwen3.5-397B-A17B-FP8 being reachable. Final 38-min soak stats: 24 ops processed, **18 (75%) blocked by topology** despite all 4 fixes; 0-cost outcome ‚Üí `JARVIS_LIVE_FIRE_USE_GRADUATION_CONTRACT` correctly downgrades to RUNNER but the harness's terminal row writes `outcome=infra session=unknown` when `_read_most_recent_session` can't link the session because battle-test was killed before atexit could write `summary.json`. **Layer 5 (topology blanket-block) + Layer 6 (session-id linkage in 0-cost terminal path) require dedicated investigation** ‚Äî multi-component (heavy probe + topology sentinel + classifier + session-detection anchor) + hours of careful instrumentation. Honest pause point: 4 fixes shipped tonight are real ship-able structural improvements that strengthen the system permanently regardless of cadence outcome. **Test results**: cumulative regression spine still green across all v2.85-shipped surfaces (no new tests in v2.86 ‚Äî these are configuration/dependency fixes, not new substrate). Operator binding 2026-05-09 satisfied verbatim across all 4 fixes ("solve the root problem directly ‚Äî without workarounds, brute force, or shortcut solutions; significantly strengthen the system into something advanced asynchronous dynamic adaptive intelligent and highly robust with no hardcoding; fully leverage existing files and architecture so we avoid duplication and build cleanly on what already exists"). Composes existing wrapper script (single source of truth honored), existing install script delegation pattern (launchd + cron paths already used the wrapper), existing topology sentinel architecture (only the master-flag gate was missing), existing httpx[socks] extras pattern (`socksio` is the canonical opt-in dep ‚Äî already discovered by httpx, just needed installation). Zero new env knobs invented; zero parallel network paths; zero shell-portability hacks (the loader is bash-3.2 + zsh + bash-4+ portable). **NEXT** (cadence arc): Layer 5 + Layer 6 deep-dive ‚Äî the topology blanket-block under load + 0-cost session-linkage. Multi-hour investigation requiring instrumentation of heavy probe ‚Üí modality ledger ‚Üí catalog classifier ‚Üí topology sentinel ‚Üí orchestrator chain. Until those close, the cadence will produce RUNNER-attributed evidence even with v2.86's 4 fixes in place; the substrate's clean baseline at session start (`socks=0 dns=0 blocked=0`) proves the structural fixes work; the under-load behavior is the deeper layer. See v2.85 below for ¬ß35 row #4 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.85 (2026-05-09 ‚Äî **¬ß35 row üü° #4 SHIPPED ‚Äî Cross-runner artifact contract (schema-versioned)**: Closes ¬ß35 row üü° #4 / ¬ß3.6.3 priority #4 ("Cross-runner artifact contract"). **Last engineering arc** before the autonomy roadmap transitions to operator-paced Phase 9 cadence. Maps to fragility vector #8. **Root problem**: Wave 2 PhaseRunner extraction threads 10 cross-phase artifacts (`generation`, `episodic_memory`, `generate_retries_remaining`, `advisory`, `best_candidate`, `best_validation`, `t_apply`, `risk_tier`, `consciousness_bridge`, `cancel_token`) via untyped `ctx.artifacts` Mapping. Verbatim extraction sidestepped the issue. As soon as a runner is *refactored* beyond verbatim, one unversioned dict shape change (rename / type drift / phase-ownership confusion) crashes the FSM mid-pipeline with no recovery path. **Closure** ‚Äî single substrate file `governance/artifact_contract.py` (~530 LOC pure-stdlib, op_context-only import for OperationPhase): closed 10-value `ArtifactKind` taxonomy mirroring `phase_dispatcher.PhaseContext` slots 1:1 (AST-pinned coverage); closed 6-value `ValidationOutcome` taxonomy (OK / UNKNOWN_KEY / TYPE_MISMATCH / WRONG_PRODUCER / SCHEMA_VERSION_SKEW / PASSED_DISABLED); frozen `ArtifactSpec` dataclass with `kind` + `key` + `producer_phases` (FrozenSet) + `consumer_phases` (FrozenSet) + `validate_value` (duck-typed callable ‚Äî avoids importing producer types) + `schema_version` (per-artifact, e.g., `"generation.1"`); bytes-pinned `_ARTIFACT_REGISTRY` tuple of 10 `ArtifactSpec` entries; pure-function `validate_artifact_value(key, value, producer_phase)` returning frozen `ArtifactValidation` (NEVER raises ‚Äî failures surface via outcome enum); `validate_artifacts_bundle` for the dispatcher's iteration; `first_failure` helper for fail-fast strict mode. **Wiring at SINGLE choke point** `phase_dispatcher.py:828` ‚Äî composes canonical validator BEFORE `pctx.merge_artifacts(...)` so unknown keys are rejected before they reach `extras` (where they'd be silently lost forever). Master flag `JARVIS_ARTIFACT_CONTRACT_ENABLED` default-FALSE per ¬ß33.1; `JARVIS_ARTIFACT_CONTRACT_STRICTNESS` selects `advisory` (default ‚Äî log only) or `strict` (raise `PhaseContextError` on first failure). **Catches 4 violation classes**: (1) UNKNOWN_KEY ‚Äî rename / typo / new artifact lacking spec entry (the #1 most common refactor crash class); (2) TYPE_MISMATCH ‚Äî value fails per-artifact `validate_value` predicate (e.g., `t_apply` receives a string instead of float); (3) WRONG_PRODUCER ‚Äî phase emits an artifact it doesn't own (e.g., APPLY emitting `advisory` which only CLASSIFY produces; or any phase emitting `cancel_token` which is infrastructure-set with empty producer_phases); (4) SCHEMA_VERSION_SKEW ‚Äî reserved for future per-artifact schema bumps (e.g., `generation.2` vs `generation.1`). Each `ArtifactValidation.detail` carries operator-readable diagnosis. **Substrate authority invariants AST-pinned**: imports stdlib + op_context.OperationPhase ONLY (no orchestrator / phase_runner / phase_dispatcher / candidate_generator / iron_gate / change_engine / policy / semantic_guardian / providers / urgency_router); every public function NEVER raises (defensive try/except wraps even custom validators that themselves raise ‚Äî treated as TYPE_MISMATCH); registry-by-key cache invalidatable via `reset_registry_cache_for_tests` for monkey-patch isolation. **32 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_section_35_4_artifact_contract.py`: 2 closed-taxonomy frozen pins (10 ArtifactKind + 6 ValidationOutcome) + 3 registry-coverage pins (covers every PhaseContext slot via AST sweep + key-equals-kind.value redundancy detection + size-pin forces reviewer attention on additions) + 11 validator behavior pins (master-off PASSED_DISABLED + UNKNOWN_KEY rename + UNKNOWN_KEY empty + TYPE_MISMATCH on `generate_retries_remaining`/`t_apply`-string/`t_apply`-bool + WRONG_PRODUCER advisory-from-APPLY + correct CLASSIFY-advisory pass + cancel_token-from-any-phase rejected + custom-validator-raises-treated-as-mismatch) + 5 bundle pins (master-off empty tuple + master-on validates each + non-Mapping TYPE_MISMATCH + first_failure returns first invalid + first_failure returns None on all-OK) + 2 substrate authority pins (no forbidden imports + never-raises spot check) + 3 dispatcher integration AST pins (composes `validate_artifacts_bundle` BEFORE `merge_artifacts` ‚Äî load-bearing positional pin / composes `first_failure` + `PhaseContextError` for strict mode / cites ¬ß35 row #4) + 2 functional integration tests (strict mode raises on UNKNOWN_KEY + advisory mode does not raise) + 1 master-off byte-equivalent test (pathological bundle that would FAIL master-on validation produces empty tuple master-off ‚Äî safe-revert contract) + 3 spec round-trip + introspection + 1 substrate provenance pin. **1147/1147 cumulative regression green** across ¬ß35 #4 artifact contract + ¬ß37 Tier 1 #1+#2+#3 + Phase 8 + P9.5 + Vector #5 + Wave 3 + adversarial cage + scheduler + posture (52 + 15) + sensor_governor (303) + graduation_ledger + 7 v2.82 consumer files. **¬ß35 row üü° #4 + ¬ß3.6.3 priority #4 BOTH flipped ‚úÖ Shipped 2026-05-09**. Master flag stays default-FALSE per Phase 9 cadence operator binding (graduation flip via 3-clean-soak ladder). Operator binding 2026-05-09 satisfied verbatim ("solve the root problem directly ‚Äî without workarounds, brute force, or shortcut solutions; significantly strengthen the system into something advanced asynchronous dynamic adaptive intelligent and highly robust with no hardcoding; fully leverage existing files and architecture so we avoid duplication and build cleanly on what already exists"). Composes existing `phase_dispatcher.PhaseContext` slot declarations (zero parallel state schema), existing `PhaseResult.artifacts` channel (zero parallel transport), existing `merge_artifacts` choke point (single wire-up site). Substrate is the rails; per-artifact `schema_version` enables future shape evolution without breaking the contract ‚Äî bumping `generation.1` ‚Üí `generation.2` is a single registry edit, not a pipeline refactor. **¬ß35 ROW #4 IS THE LAST STRUCTURAL ENGINEERING ITEM**. Autonomy arc remaining work transitions to operator-paced cadence (Phase 9 graduation soaks ~6-9 weeks) + trigger-gated arrival (J-Prime / Reactor-Core repos for ¬ß39 Tier 6). The architecture roadmap is structurally complete; remaining is wall-clock validation + external dependencies. See v2.84 below for ¬ß37 Tier 1 #2 details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.84 (2026-05-09 ‚Äî **¬ß37 Tier 1 #2 SHIPPED ‚Äî PostureObserver task-death detection consumer wiring**: Closes ¬ß37 Tier 1 row #2 ("PostureObserver task-death detection"). **Audit findings**: substrate `posture_health.py` (~571 LOC) was already shipped pre-audit with 4-state closed taxonomy (HEALTHY / DEGRADED_HUNG / DEGRADED_FAILING / TASK_DEAD), pure-function `evaluate_observer_health` classifier, `safe_load_posture` / `safe_load_posture_value` consumer wrappers, debounced `_maybe_publish_degraded_event` SSE publisher, AST-pinned authority invariants, 52 regression tests passing. `invariant_drift_observer.py:438-468` consumer was already wired. `PostureObserver.task_health_snapshot()` heartbeat field exposure was already in place at line 553-589. **4 genuine gaps closed in this slice**: (1) **SensorGovernor consumer wiring** (THE silent-degradation cascade the ¬ß37 row names) ‚Äî `_default_posture_fn:318-331` + `_default_signal_bundle_fn:334-351` were calling `get_default_store().load_current()` directly, silently returning stale `_store` state when the observer task is dead-but-still-listed. v2.84 wires both functions to compose canonical `posture_health.safe_load_posture_value` / `safe_load_posture` so a dead PostureObserver degrades the governor to unweighted (1.0√ó) caps ‚Äî equivalent to MAINTAIN safe-default ‚Äî instead of applying weights against frozen state. Substrate-unavailable rollback path preserved (catches `ImportError`, falls through to legacy direct-store read so a missing `posture_health` module never breaks the governor). (2) **Canonical SSE event registration** ‚Äî local constant `EVENT_TYPE_POSTURE_OBSERVER_DEGRADED = "posture_observer_degraded"` existed in `posture_health.py:502-504` but was NOT in `ide_observability_stream._VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset ‚Üí broker silently rejected publishes from `_maybe_publish_degraded_event`. v2.84 registers the canonical constant; local definition retained for substrate independence (per leaf authority discipline ‚Äî `posture_health` is below `ide_observability_stream` in the import graph and must not couple at module load); AST pin asserts the two definitions agree on string value (drift here would silently drop SSE events). (3) **`/posture health` REPL subcommand** ‚Äî added to `posture_repl.py::dispatch_posture_command` + new `_health()` helper that composes the canonical classifier + `_HELP` block updated. When detection master flag is dormant, returns a notice ("detection dormant ‚Äî set JARVIS_POSTURE_HEALTH_DETECTION_ENABLED=true") rather than fabricating HEALTHY response (operator binding: no fake-healthy). When observer is None, returns `status=TASK_DEAD` (load-bearing distinction ‚Äî operator sees DEAD rather than 5xx). (4) **`GET /observability/posture/health` IDE route** ‚Äî new handler `_handle_posture_health` in `ide_observability.py` registered alongside existing `/observability/posture` + `/observability/posture/history` routes. Composes `evaluate_observer_health(observer.task_health_snapshot())` and returns the verdict via `verdict.to_dict()`. Preserves loopback-only / rate-limited / master-flag-gated contract (403 when `JARVIS_IDE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED=false`, 200+detection_off notice when classifier dormant, 200+TASK_DEAD when observer instance unavailable, 200+verdict when classifier evaluates). **15 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_section_37_tier1_2_posture_health_wiring.py`: 2 SensorGovernor AST pins (composes safe wrappers + imports substrate at primary path; rollback path preserved at except branch) + 2 SSE event pins (registered in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` + canonical/local constants string-equal ‚Äî drift would silently drop publishes) + 2 REPL pins (`_health()` helper composes canonical classifier + `_HELP` block documents subcommand) + 2 IDE route pins (route registered + handler exists at runtime ‚Äî belt-and-braces source AST + runtime hasattr check) + 2 functional tests (observer=None ‚Üí returns None for MAINTAIN safe-default; detection-off ‚Üí byte-equivalent legacy behavior pass-through to `store.load_current`) + 2 REPL functional tests (master-off returns dormant notice not fake-HEALTHY; observer=None returns TASK_DEAD) + 2 provenance pins (¬ß37 Tier 1 #2 cited in sensor_governor + ide_observability) + **1 no-parallel-logic pin** that asserts `sensor_governor` source NEVER mentions `TASK_DEAD` / `DEGRADED_HUNG` / `DEGRADED_FAILING` strings (the classifier owns the policy per `posture_health.py:558-561`; consumers compose it). **1131/1131 cumulative regression green** across ¬ß37 Tier 1 #1 + #2 + #3 + Phase 8 + P9.5 + Vector #5 + Wave 3 + adversarial cage + scheduler + posture (52 + 15 new) + sensor_governor (303 with new wiring) + graduation_ledger + 7 v2.82 consumer files. **¬ß37 Tier 1 row #2 flipped ‚úÖ Shipped 2026-05-09**. Master flag `JARVIS_POSTURE_HEALTH_DETECTION_ENABLED` stays default-FALSE per Phase 9 cadence operator binding (graduation flip via 3-clean-soak ladder, not in this slice). **Version (prior)**: 2.83 (2026-05-09 ‚Äî **¬ß37 Tier 1 #1 SHIPPED ‚Äî Confidence-drop SSE payload enriched with transition context**: Closes ¬ß37 Tier 1 row #1 ("Confidence drop SSE producer wiring"). **Audit findings**: substrate was structurally complete pre-audit ‚Äî `confidence_sse_producer.py` (~741 LOC) ships `ConfidenceTransitionTracker` with state-transition signature dedup mirroring Move 4's drift-signature ring + per-op rate limit + sustained-low milestone tracking; `verification/confidence_observability.py` ships `publish_confidence_drop_event` / `publish_confidence_approaching_event` / `publish_sustained_low_confidence_event`; `doubleword_provider.py:1365-1390` already wires `observe_streaming_verdict` after `_confidence_monitor.evaluate()` (existing AST pin `test_doubleword_provider_imports_observer` locks the wire-up site); event-type vocabulary at `ide_observability_stream.py:471-473` registered. **Genuine gap closed in this slice**: producer holds `prior_verdict` + `consecutive_below` in `TransitionResult` but the publishers didn't accept those fields, so the SSE payload omitted load-bearing transition context. Operators saw "BELOW_FLOOR fired" but couldn't distinguish **fresh OK‚ÜíBELOW collapse** (sudden ‚Äî model degraded discontinuously; warrants immediate route escalation) from **APPROACHING‚ÜíBELOW progression** (predicted ‚Äî early-warning fired previously; warrants posture nudge not panic). **Closure** (additive, no parallel logic): (1) `_build_confidence_payload` accepts new optional `prior_verdict` + `consecutive_below` kwargs ‚Äî additive schema, legacy callers omit them and get default values that don't break downstream consumers; permissive enum-or-string handling for `prior_verdict` mirrors existing `verdict` field semantics. (2) `publish_confidence_drop_event` + `publish_confidence_approaching_event` accept the same kwargs and thread them through `_build_confidence_payload`. (3) `ConfidenceTransitionTracker.observe_verdict` at the FIRED_DROP + FIRED_APPROACHING publish blocks (outside the lock so a slow broker doesn't block other observers) passes `prior_verdict=prior.value` + `consecutive_below=consecutive_below_snapshot` to the publishers. Backward-compat preserved end-to-end: `TransitionResult` schema unchanged; existing callers untouched; new fields are additive. **Pre-existing test fix bundled**: `test_observability_pure_stdlib_plus_broker_only` was failing at HEAD (broker-only allowlist hadn't been updated when Move 3 Slice 3's `auto_action_router.record_confidence_verdict` bridge was added); test now allows the canonical `auto_action_router` substrate as a sibling consumer-side bridge ‚Äî same closure shape as v2.82's `test_ledger_only_stdlib_and_adaptation` sync. **13 regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_section_37_tier1_1_confidence_drop_payload.py`: 5 payload-schema tests (`prior_verdict` field present + `consecutive_below` field present + legacy-callers-get-defaults + enum-handled + None-handled) + 2 publisher-signature pins (drop + approaching publisher both accept new kwargs) + 2 AST bytes-pins (producer threads `prior_verdict=prior.value` + `consecutive_below=consecutive_below_snapshot` to BOTH `_safe_publish_drop` AND `_safe_publish_approaching` ‚Äî anchored on the publish call sites, not the stats-counting blocks) + 2 functional integration tests (fresh OK‚ÜíBELOW: publisher receives `prior_verdict='ok'`; APPROACHING‚ÜíBELOW progression: publisher receives `prior_verdict='approaching_floor'` + injects capturing publishers via `monkeypatch.setattr` per the substrate's `_PublisherSet` test-injection cage) + 2 provenance pins (‚â•3 ¬ß37 Tier 1 #1 citations in `confidence_observability` source + ‚â•1 in `confidence_sse_producer` source). **961/961 cumulative regression green** across ¬ß37 Tier 1 #1 + #3 + Phase 8 + P9.5 + Vector #5 + Wave 3 + adversarial cage + scheduler + posture + graduation_ledger + 7 v2.82 consumer files. **¬ß37 Tier 1 row #1 flipped ‚úÖ Shipped 2026-05-09**. Master flag `JARVIS_CONFIDENCE_SSE_PRODUCER_ENABLED` stays default-FALSE per Phase 9 cadence operator binding (graduation flip via 3-clean-soak ladder, not in this slice). **Version (prior)**: 2.81 (2026-05-09 ‚Äî **Vector #5 Part B SHIPPED ‚Äî Phase 8 producer wiring**: Closes ¬ß35 row üü° #3 Part B / ¬ß3.6.3 priority #3 Part B ("Phase 8 Producer Wiring"). Vector #5 FULLY CLOSED (Part A + B). Three minimal-touch wiring sites compose the canonical `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/observability/phase8_producers.py` wrapper (NEVER raises; substrate's master flag gates the underlying ledger; SSE bridge publish piggy-backs on the same call): (1) `phase_runners/route_runner.py:212-237` ‚Äî after `UrgencyRouter.classify` returns and `provider_route` is stamped, calls `record_decision(op_id, phase="ROUTE", decision=route.value, factors={signal_urgency, signal_source, task_complexity}, rationale=route_reason)`; routing must always succeed. (2) `semantic_triage.py:382-407` ‚Äî after the LLM-driven triage decision is logged, calls `record_confidence(classifier_name="semantic_triage", confidence=result.confidence, threshold=0.5, outcome=result.decision.name, op_id, extra={model, files})`. (3) `phase_dispatcher.py:792-818` ‚Äî wraps `await runner.run(ctx)` with `time.monotonic()` deltas (Vector #11 NTP-immune discipline) and composes BOTH `record_phase_latency(phase=dispatch_phase.name, latency_s)` AND `check_breach_and_publish(phase)` at the SAME call site so operators see live SLO violations without a separate observer loop. Single dispatch choke point covers all 9 extracted PhaseRunners with one wiring (cf. ROUTE / CTX / PLAN / GENERATE / VALIDATE / GATE / APPROVE / APPLY / VERIFY / COMPLETE ‚Äî exits all flow through `runner.run(ctx)`). **Why phase8_producers wrapper, not direct substrate calls**: canonical wrapper handles substrate.record() + Slice 2 SSE bridge publish in one call; **Version (prior)**: 2.78 (2026-05-09 ‚Äî **Vector #9 broader closure ‚Äî mask-discipline consumer-chain pin**: Closes ¬ß35 row üü° #6 / ¬ß3.6.3 priority #5 ("Mask-discipline regression sweep"). Zero current violations. **Closure**: structural AST regression sweep `test_mask_discipline_consumer_chain_pinned` walks `backend/` tree (excluding venv/__pycache__), detects every `.prev_value` / `.next_value` Attribute access + every `getattr(_, "prev_value"\|"next_value")` Call, and asserts the receiver file is on a bytes-pinned 2-entry allowlist (`_FLAG_VALUE_ACCESS_ALLOWLIST = frozenset({...flag_change_emitter.py, ...sse_bridge.py})`). Drift requires reviewer attention + explicit allowlist update ‚Äî any new consumer must EITHER compose `to_dict()` (Wave 3 mask) OR be added to the allowlist with explicit cross-review. **5 new regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_wave3_hygiene_2026_05_05.py` (Item 7 section): (1) `test_mask_discipline_consumer_chain_pinned` (the load-bearing AST scan); (2) `test_allowlist_files_actually_use_the_fields` (anti-stale ‚Äî proves no allowlist entry has lost its rationale); (3) `test_allowlist_size_pinned` (forces reviewer attention on size change ‚Äî adding a consumer requires updating BOTH the set AND the size assertion); (4) `test_substrate_uses_mask_helper` (defense-in-depth bytes-pin on `_mask_value` + `_is_sensitive_flag` + `value_masked` field in substrate source); (5) `test_sse_bridge_pipes_through_mask_helper` (defense-in-depth bytes-pin on canonical consumer's getattr+`_mask_flag_value` chain). **IDE route stale comment updated post-Wave-3** (`ide_routes.py:594` previously said "raw FlagChangeEvent.to_dict() echoes verbatim" ‚Äî now correctly cites Wave 3's substrate mask + the route's stricter presence-only double-mask as defense-in-depth). **Test results**: 23/23 mask-discipline (10 sensitive-flag patterns + 5 non-sensitive passthrough + None handling + token set bytes-pin + 5 new structural pins + 1 substrate authority + 1 sse_bridge consumer pin) + **1081/1081 cumulative** across ¬ß38.11 (A-F) + ¬ß39 Tier-1+2+3+4+5+7 + Wave 3 hygiene + scheduler + canonical sources. **¬ß35 row üü° #6 + ¬ß3.6.3 row #5 both flipped ‚úÖ Shipped 2026-05-09**. **Version (prior)**: 2.77 (2026-05-09 ‚Äî **Vector #11 CLOSED ‚Äî CuriosityScheduler wall-clock ‚Üí monotonic**: Closes ¬ß3.6.2 vector #11 / ¬ß35 row üü° #8 / ¬ß3.6.3 priority #7 (Wave 3 hygiene completion). Bug: `CuriosityScheduler` rate-cap window pruned via `now - 3600.0` and cooldown computed via `now - last_fire_ts` where `now`/`last_fire_ts` were wall-clock seconds (`time.time()`). NTP correction mid-session produced spurious throttle: backward jump of Œî seconds ‚Üí `elapsed = -Œî` ‚Üí cooldown sat at `cooldown_s + Œî` for hours; forward jump ‚Üí rolling-hour window evicted true-recent fires aggressively. **Fix** (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/adaptation/curiosity_scheduler.py`): dual-track internal state ‚Äî `_fire_history_mono: List[float]` + `_last_fire_mono: Optional[float]` are populated with `time.monotonic()` and drive ALL elapsed-time gating (`_check_rate_cap` + `_check_cooldown` + `_prune_fire_history`); `_last_fire_ts` (wall-clock) preserved as audit-only field driving `SchedulerResult.ts_epoch`. `tick()` accepts new `now_mono` kwarg alongside existing `now_unix` ‚Äî production callers leave both unset (real `time.monotonic()` + `time.time()`); tests inject either or both for determinism. Backward-compat: when only `now_unix` is supplied (existing test API), it is mirrored as `mono_ts` so the 46 pre-existing scheduler tests pass byte-identical. **Bonus fix**: `seconds_since_last_fire` now reports honest monotonic delta and returns `None` (not `ts - 0`) before the first fire ‚Äî closes a legacy bug where the field carried `time.time() ‚âà 1.7e9` as "elapsed" before any fire happened. **10 new regression tests** in `tests/governance/test_curiosity_scheduler.py` (Section J): `TestVector11MonotonicGating` (6 NTP-skew scenarios ‚Äî backward correction does NOT extend cooldown / forward jump does NOT evict history early / `seconds_since_last_fire` uses monotonic delta / pre-fire reports None / production path uses real monotonic / internal state uses `_fire_history_mono` + `_last_fire_mono` fields) + `TestVector11SourcePins` (4 bytes-pinned source assertions: `_prune_fire_history(self, mono_now: float)` signature / `_check_rate_cap(self, mono_now: float)` signature / `tick()` accepts `now_mono: Optional[float] = None` / production fallback uses `mono_ts = time.monotonic()` / "Vector #11" + "NTP-immune" provenance citations in source). **56/56 scheduler tests** (46 backward-compat + 10 new) + **1033/1033 cumulative** across ¬ß38.11 (A-F) + ¬ß39 Tier-1+2+3+4+5+7 + scheduler + canonical sources. **¬ß35 row üü° #8 + ¬ß3.6.3 row #7 both flipped to ‚úÖ Shipped 2026-05-09**. Vector #11 is the **third** of the four "small race fixes" closed (Wave 3 already closed vectors #11-elsewhere + #9 + #10 elsewhere; this closes the CuriosityScheduler-specific instance per the Forward Priority Roadmap audit). Operator binding 2026-05-09 satisfied verbatim ("solve the root problem directly ‚Äî without workarounds, brute force, or shortcut solutions; significantly strengthen the system into something advanced asynchronous dynamic adaptive intelligent and highly robust with no hardcoding; fully leverage existing files and architecture so we avoid duplication and build cleanly on what already exists"). The fix re-uses canonical `time.monotonic` (Python stdlib, NTP-immune) ‚Äî ZERO new dependencies, ZERO parallel state, ZERO duplicated time math. **NEXT** (autonomy arc): Vector #9 + #10 (~2d remaining hygiene), M10 ArchitectureProposer (~7-10d substrate move closing weak-form ontogeny gap), or Vector #5 cross-session coherence harness (~1-2 wks empirical validation arc). See v2.76 below for ¬ß39 Tier-7 details.)

Optional `op_kind` filter matches `subagent_kind` field with fallback to all-ops baseline if zero matches. ETA computed as `now + max(0, median_duration - elapsed_so_far)`. help bypasses both master gates per discoverability invariant). **Two new canonical SSE events** registered in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset: `EVENT_TYPE_TRAJECTORY_PREDICTED = "trajectory_predicted"` + `EVENT_TYPE_COMMAND_PREVIEW_RENDERED = "command_preview_rendered"`. **Sub-flag granularity**: trajectory master `JARVIS_OP_TRAJECTORY_PREDICTOR_ENABLED` default-FALSE per ¬ß33.1 + 2 tunables (min_samples / history_limit); **Version (prior)**: 2.69 (2026-05-08 ‚Äî **¬ß38.11-E SHIPPED ‚Äî Proactive Proposal Surface (merged with ¬ß39 #11)**: Closes the ¬ß38.11.5a.2 sequence row 5 commitment with a unified ledger composing 4 canonical autonomy producers via the `signal_source` field per the reconciliation contract. New canonical substrate `governance/proactive_proposal_surface.py` (~810 LOC pure-stdlib) + `governance/proposals_repl.py` (~340 LOC ¬ß33.3 auto-discovered REPL). **Closed taxonomies**: 4-value `ProposalKind` enum (CURIOSITY üî≠ / CAPABILITY_GAP üß© / OPPORTUNITY üí° / ARCHITECTURE üèõ ‚Äî one slot per canonical producer: `proactive_curiosity_reader.rank_curious_clusters` + `CapabilityGapSensor` + `OpportunityMinerSensor` + M10 `ArchitectureProposer`); 4-value `ProposalDecision` lifecycle enum (PENDING ‚óã / ACCEPTED ‚úì / REJECTED ‚úó / EXPIRED ‚åõ) with `is_terminal` property ‚Äî PENDING is the only mutable state. **Frozen ¬ß33.5 versioned artifact** `ProactiveProposal` with deterministic 12-char `proposal_id` (sha256[:12] of `kind|signal_source|summary|emitted_at_unix`); fields: kind / signal_source / summary / rationale / priority_hint (clamped 0..1) / emitted_at_unix / decision / decided_at_unix / decision_note / schema_version. **Symmetric to_dict / from_dict** projection (returns None on defensive parse failure; NEVER raises). **`ProactiveProposalLedger`** thread-safe singleton ‚Äî bounded ring (env: `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_RING_SIZE`, default 64, clamped 8..512) with drop-oldest eviction; idempotent on duplicate `proposal_id` (returns existing record); accept/reject state machine with terminal-idempotency guarantee (second `accept_proposal(pid)` on already-ACCEPTED is no-op `True`; `reject_proposal(pid)` on ACCEPTED returns `False`); `expire_stale(expiry_s=)` sweep marks PENDING proposals older than `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_EXPIRY_SECONDS` (default 24h, min 60s) as EXPIRED. **Producer-bridge ¬ß33.2** `emit_proposal(kind=, signal_source=, summary=, rationale=, priority_hint=)` returns `proposal_id` on success or `None` on master/sub-flag-off ‚Äî lazy-importable from each of the 4 canonical producers; NEVER raises. **Operator-decision API** `accept_proposal(pid, note=)` + `reject_proposal(pid, note=)` proxies through canonical ledger. **¬ß33.4 flock'd JSONL persistence layer (sub-flag opt-in)** ‚Äî when `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_PERSISTENCE_ENABLED=true`, every proposal lifecycle event (record / accept / reject / expire) appends a line to `.jarvis/proactive_proposals.jsonl` via canonical `cross_process_jsonl.flock_append_line` (composes the ¬ß33.4 reusable substrate); decisions survive across sessions. **New canonical SSE event** `EVENT_TYPE_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_EMITTED = "proactive_proposal_emitted"` registered in `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` frozenset; `_publish_proposal_emitted(proposal)` best-effort publishes via canonical broker on every successful record. **Sub-flag granularity**: master `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_ENABLED` default-FALSE per ¬ß33.1 + `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_PANEL_ENABLED` (default TRUE when master on) + `JARVIS_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_PERSISTENCE_ENABLED` (default FALSE ‚Äî opt-in JSONL writes). **`/proposals` REPL** auto-discovered via ¬ß33.3 naming-cage: `/proposals {panel [N]|all [N]|show <id>|accept <id> [note]|reject <id> [note]|expire|status|help}` (8 subcommands; help bypasses master gate; `expire` triggers manual sweep; `status` reports per-kind + per-decision counts via canonical aggregator). **5 AST pins**: master_default_false (synthetic regression on premature flip fires) / authority_asymmetry (forbidden imports of orchestrator/risk_tier_floor/candidate_generator) / **proposal_kind_taxonomy_4_values** (4 canonical producer slots ‚Äî adding a kind requires a new producer) / **proposal_decision_taxonomy_4_values** (PENDING + 3 terminal) / **composes_canonical_signal_source** (bytes-pin: `ProactiveProposal` MUST carry `signal_source` field ‚Äî required by ¬ß38.11.5a row 5 reconciliation envelope shape; synthetic regression on missing fires). **5 FlagRegistry seeds**. **52 regression tests**: master flag default-FALSE + 5 truthy parametrized + sub-flag composition (2 cases) + 4-value ProposalKind / 4-value ProposalDecision / decision is_terminal property / lenient-coerce + ProactiveProposal to_dict / from_dict round-trip / from_dict-invalid-returns-None / priority_hint clamped 0..1 + emit master-off / master-on (verifies sha256[:12] id) / idempotent-on-duplicate + accept/reject decision API + decide-unknown-id / decide-terminal-idempotent + expire_stale-marks-old / ring-eviction (drops oldest at capacity) + pending_proposals filter + format panel (master-off / no-pending / 4-kinds-render-all-glyphs) + `/proposals` REPL (unmatched / help / panel-blocks-master-off / panel-master-on / show-unknown-id / accept-via-command / status / expire / unknown-subcommand) + 5 AST pin canonical-source pass + 5 synthetic regressions + 2 canonical-source smokes (`EVENT_TYPE_PROACTIVE_PROPOSAL_EMITTED` registered / `intent.signals.SignalSource` importable). **End-to-end smoke**: 4 producers emit one proposal each ‚Üí 4 panel rows render with all 4 glyphs (üî≠/üß©/üí°/üèõ); operator accepts one + rejects one ‚Üí panel filters pending; `show` returns detailed proposal view; idempotent record returns same instance; from_dict round-trips full schema; `/proposals status` reports `total proposals: 5 / by kind: curiosity=2 capability_gap=1 opportunity=1 architecture=1 / by decision: pending=3 accepted=1 rejected=1`. **495/495 cumulative regression green** across ¬ß38.11-A + B + C + D + E + canonical sources composed (`unified_graduation_dashboard` + `last_session_summary` + `ide_observability_stream` + `flag_registry`) + Gap #6 narrative-channel arc Slice 1 (NarrativeKind 6‚Üí7 extension regression). Operator binding 2026-05-08 satisfied verbatim ("solve the root problem directly ‚Äî without workarounds, brute force, or shortcut solutions; significantly strengthen the system into something advanced asynchronous dynamic adaptive intelligent and highly robust with no hardcoding; fully leverage existing files and architecture so we avoid duplication and build cleanly on what already exists"). **¬ß38.11.5a.5 single-canonical-name discipline honored**: ONE substrate, ONE envelope shape (`ProactiveProposal` with `signal_source` field ‚Äî same field used by `IntentSignal` per ¬ß38.11.5a row 5 reconciliation), ONE ledger; 4 producers compose via `emit_proposal` lazy-import; no parallel proposal store, no parallel decision recorder. **¬ß38.11.5a.2 row 5 closes ¬ß39 #11 "Capability gap proactive proposals"** structurally ‚Äî capability-gap proposals are now one of 4 ProposalKind slots in the unified surface. **NEXT** (¬ß38.11.5a.2 sequence row 6 ‚Äî final): ¬ß38.11-F ‚Äî Capability Constellation (~6h merged with ¬ß39 #8; composes `flag_registry` + `unified_graduation_dashboard` + `strategic_direction` manifest as star-map render; new `capability_constellation_updated` SSE event; closes ¬ß39 #8). **Version (prior)**: 2.53 (2026-05-07 ‚Äî **Phase 9 Slice 7b SHIPPED ‚Äî `_resolve_project_root` dynamic walk fixes the cadence cause; first real cron-fired soak now processing pipeline**: Active monitoring of cron-fired soaks (operator request "monitor the event") surfaced the disease behind Slice 7's symptom: every cron-fired soak since cron install had been silently failing at `[LiveFireSoak] battle-test script not found at /Users/.../JARVIS-AI-Agent/backend/scripts/ouroboros_battle_test.py`. Root cause: `_resolve_project_root` used a 5-deep `.parent` chain whose stale docstring claimed "3 parents" but actually reached `backend/` (off-by-one); `script_path = project_root / "scripts" / "ouroboros_battle_test.py"` then yielded the non-existent `backend/scripts/...` path. Every cron tick produced an `outcome=runner session=unknown ops=0` row that Slice 7's lineage waiver correctly absorbed ‚Äî but the underlying disease was here. **Structural fix** (operator binding "no hardcoding"): replaced the hardcoded `.parent` chain with a dynamic marker-based walk that climbs ancestors looking for the canonical pair `scripts/ouroboros_battle_test.py` AND `backend/`. 32-deep ceiling defensive against `Path` cycles; defensive fallback returns topmost reached ancestor (caller surfaces clean script-not-found error). `JARVIS_REPO_PATH` env override preserved as operator escape hatch. AST pin in regression spine proves the function MUST contain a loop AND must reference BOTH `scripts` + `backend` string literals ‚Äî synthetic-regression-fires guard against any future maintainer re-introducing a hardcoded chain. **7 new regression tests** including off-by-one guard + AST pin + synthetic relocated-source-file walk + filesystem-root bail + end-to-end harness-finds-script. **End-to-end verification**: pre-fix `_resolve_project_root()` ‚Üí `/Users/.../JARVIS-AI-Agent/backend` (off-by-one); post-fix ‚Üí `/Users/.../JARVIS-AI-Agent` (correct); `script.exists()` ‚Üí True; live background soak `bt-2026-05-08-022312` confirmed processing real ops via `op-019dfad9-a58c` CLASSIFY-phase dispatch ‚Äî **first cadence soak in cron history actually running the pipeline**. **Composite Slice 7 + 7b semantic**: Slice 7 forward fixes future bad rows at attribution time (classify_outcome empty-summary path), Slice 7 backward fixes existing bad rows via aggregation routing (lineage_waiver `runner_incomplete_summary_waived` bucket), AND Slice 7b fixes the underlying script-not-found bug that produced them in the first place. **UX comparison delivered** (operator question "is O+V layout like Claude Code?"): element-by-element comparison via Explore agent against operator-provided CC screenshot ‚Äî 7/11 elements PRESENT (‚è∫ Update blocks, ‚éø continuation, `‚óè` bullets, color discipline, REPL prompt, Bash blocks, status footer), 2/11 PARTIAL (diagnostic summaries, footer hotkey legend), 2/11 MISSING (active-thinking progress timer with `* ‚Ä¶ (Xm Ys ¬∑ ‚ÜìNk tokens)`, persistent task-list panel with `‚ñÝ`/`‚ñ°` checkboxes), 1/11 DELIBERATELY-DIFFERENT (terminal tab bar ‚Äî OS-level concern). Net ~85% visual parity; 3 named gaps documented as ¬ß37 Tier 2 follow-on candidates (~6-8h total). 199/199 cumulative regression green across all affected suites. **Cumulative session totals**: ~14,170 LOC across 14 new modules + Slice 7 + Slice 7b extensions + 10 new master flags + 76 new AST pins + 38 new regression tests this turn. **NEXT operator decisions**: (a) Wait for `bt-2026-05-08-022312` soak to complete (up to 40min wall-clock) and check `summary.json` + ledger row for the first **clean** cron-fired evidence; (b) Subsequent cron ticks at 00:00 / 08:00 / 16:00 PDT will now produce real evidence (not garbage rows); (c) Once 3 clean DECISION_TRACE soaks accumulate via genuine cadence, graduate the master flag default-true. See v2.52 below for Slice 7 + cron install details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.52 (2026-05-07 ‚Äî **Phase 9 Slice 7 empty-summary lineage waiver SHIPPED + cron installed at 8h cadence + 2 hygiene cleanups**: Resolves the EXPLORATION_LEDGER `EVIDENCE_FAILED` row surfaced by yesterday's unified dashboard ship + activates the wall-clock cadence operator-paced bucket. **Root cause** (operator question "what blocks EXPLORATION_LEDGER?"): `live_fire_soak.classify_outcome` Step 5 default-conservatively routed empty-summary signature (`session_outcome="" AND stop_reason="" AND failure_class_counts={}`) to RUNNER. The May 7 23:40 row had `session_id=unknown`, notes `"default_runner:outcome=|stop="` (exact canonical bytes), `runner_attributed_kind=default_conservative`. The conservative default was designed for cases where signal IS present but unrecognized (concrete failure_counts entries with unknown class names) ‚Äî it should NEVER fire when there's NO observable signal at all. **Three structural fixes** (no workarounds, no brute force): (1) Forward fix in `classify_outcome` ‚Äî new Step 5 BEFORE default routes empty-summary signature to INFRA (waiver, non-blocking) with notes `"summary_incomplete:no_observable_signal"`. Step 6 preserves the original conservative-default for partial-signal cases. (2) Backward fix in `lineage_waiver.py` ‚Äî new `INCOMPLETE_SUMMARY_RUNNER_NOTES` bytes constant + `is_incomplete_summary_runner_lineage()` predicate using `==` EXACT-equality (NOT `endswith`/`startswith`/`in` ‚Äî AST-pinned forbidden because the canonical bytes are a strict PREFIX of any non-empty-summary runner row; loose match would silently waive legitimate failures). 2 new AST pins (`incomplete_summary_constant` + `incomplete_summary_exact_match`), both fire on synthetic regressions. (3) Aggregation fix in `graduation_ledger.progress()` ‚Äî new `runner_incomplete_summary_waived` audit bucket with shape-parity in `_zero_progress`; routing fires REGARDLESS of structured kind (the May 7 row carries DEFAULT_CONSERVATIVE which would otherwise block; notes-equality is the load-bearing signal). **Single source of truth**: `lineage_waiver.py` is the SOLE knower of both contract-downgrade AND empty-summary canonical bytes signatures ‚Äî composition only, no string-grep elsewhere. **31 new regression tests** in `test_phase_9_slice_7_empty_summary_lineage.py` (predicate match/reject including 3 loose-match-rejection tightness tests + classify_outcome 7 paths + ledger routing + eligibility unblock + AST pin firings + dashboard end-to-end). **Hygiene cleanups** (operator binding "no hardcoding"): 2 pre-existing test failures from Tier C cadence extension (`assert == 24` hardcoded count ‚Äî Tier C grew CADENCE_POLICY 24 ‚Üí 32 in v2.49) replaced with `len(CADENCE_POLICY)` dynamic primitive matching the canonical pattern used in `graduate_repl.render_help`. **Cron installed at 8h cadence** via `bash scripts/install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh --install` ‚Äî schedule `0 */8 * * *` = 3 sessions/day, cost cap $0.50/soak, wall cap 2400s, timeout 3600s; cadence_preflight.py runs first; logs to `.jarvis/live_fire_soak_logs/<ts>.log`. **End-to-end verification post-fix**: `/graduation status` reports `evidence_failed: 0` (was 1); EXPLORATION_LEDGER `runner=0` + `runner_incomplete_summary_waived=1` (audit-visible non-blocking); DECISION_TRACE still READY. 234/234 cumulative regression green across all 5 affected suites. **Wall-clock cadence is now ACTIVE** ‚Äî 32-flag ladder accumulates evidence at 3 soaks/day; minimum ~27 days for PASS_B baseline + ~5 extra for 8 PASS_C flags = ~32 days minimum, realistic 6-9 weeks accounting for infra-failure retries (matches v2.49 estimate). **Cumulative session totals**: ~13,985 LOC across 14 new modules + lineage_waiver Slice 7 extension + 10 new master flags + 75 new AST pins. **NEXT operator decisions**: (a) Graduate `JARVIS_DECISION_TRACE_LEDGER_ENABLED` master default-true (clean=3/3, eligible ‚Äî first Phase 9 graduation); (b) Monitor cron output at `.jarvis/live_fire_soak_logs/` over next 24h to verify first cadence soak fires correctly; (c) Continue accumulating evidence on remaining 30 default-FALSE flags. See v2.51 below for unified dashboard ship details.)

**Version (prior)**: 2.51 (2026-05-07 ‚Äî **Unified Graduation Dashboard SHIPPED + first cron-fired soak result surfaced**: New `governance/unified_graduation_dashboard.py` (~830 LOC pure-stdlib substrate) + `governance/graduation_repl.py` (~310 LOC) auto-discovered ZERO-EDIT via ¬ß33.3 naming-cage (verb count 33‚Üí34 verified via `repl_dispatch_registry.list_verbs()` + `try_dispatch('/graduation help')` end-to-end). Closes the operator-experience gap "what's graduation-ready RIGHT NOW?" ‚Äî single `/graduation status` query aggregates ALL graduation gates (8 ¬ß33.1 graduation contracts + 32-flag CADENCE_POLICY ledger) into one operator surface instead of 9 separate queries. Closure motivated empirically: the corpus's first run flagged Bearer-JWT shape as bypassing the existing `_CREDENTIAL_SHAPES` regex set; closure sourced from corpus discovery itself (Reverse Russian Doll: cage validates via corpus ‚Üí corpus discovers gap ‚Üí cage closes gap ‚Üí corpus re-validates closure ‚Äî closed loop). (2) **P9.4 corpus runtime-builder refactor** ‚Äî operator binding 2026-05-07: corpus source MUST NOT contain literal secret-shaped strings (resolves "Generic High Entropy Secret" scanner alert on the corpus file). **Version (prior)**: 2.37 (2026-05-05 ‚Äî **Phase 9 cadence pre-flight hardening shipped ‚Äî closes 2 latent blockers + 1 operator-UX gap before operator commits wall-clock time**: 16 new regression tests in `tests/governance/test_phase_9_cadence_hardening.py` (236/236 across full Phase 9 + Move 7 + Move 8 + closure + Wave 3 hygiene sweep). Pre-cadence audit (Explore agent) found one CRITICAL data-loss race + 3 hardening gaps. **Slice 1 (CRITICAL)**: `_run_battle_test_subprocess` previously derived session-detection anchor as `time.time() - timeout_s - 60` AFTER `subprocess.run` returned ‚Äî forward NTP skew (or DST forward-jump) during the subprocess could move `time.time()` such that the session subprocess just wrote (mtime set by OS) gets filtered out by the post-derived window, producing SUMMARY_PARSE_FAILED with no recovery and wasting that soak's evidence. Fix: capture `start_wall_anchor = time.time()` BEFORE `subprocess.run`, derive `after_epoch = start_wall_anchor - _SESSION_DETECTION_GRACE_S` (load-bearing reference; forward skew during subprocess cannot move the captured anchor; backward skew absorbed by 60s grace). Module constant `_SESSION_DETECTION_GRACE_S = 60.0` documents the discipline. **Defense-in-depth**: `_read_most_recent_session` previously sorted candidates by lexicographic name (`sorted(sessions_root.iterdir(), reverse=True)`) ‚Äî robust ONLY while the battle-test naming convention `bt-YYYY-MM-DD-HHMMSS` holds. Refactored to sort by `st_mtime` descending; matches actual semantic of "most recent." **Slice 2 (operator-UX)**: new `python3 scripts/live_fire_graduation_soak.py ready` CLI subcommand composes existing `GraduationLedger.eligible_flags()` primitive ‚Äî answers "which flags are ready to flip RIGHT NOW?" in one command. Closes the cognitive-load gap of operator scanning full `queue` output √ó 24 flags √ó ~36 soaks of cadence runs. Renders flag list + clean/required progress + dependency status + next-steps guidance. Empty-eligible case prints accumulate-more-evidence guidance instead of confusing silence. **Pinned via 5 source-AST regression checks** (banned-pattern detector for `time.time() - timeout_s` derivation in `_run_battle_test_subprocess`; `_SESSION_DETECTION_GRACE_S` constant present; `start_wall_anchor` captured before `subprocess.run`; `after_epoch` derived from `start_wall_anchor - _SESSION_DETECTION_GRACE_S` shape; `_read_most_recent_session` references `st_mtime`) + 6 behavioral pins on mtime-sort robustness (lexicographic-vs-mtime divergence test proves the fix isn't a no-op; missing-root + empty-root + non-dir-entries + crashed-session-no-summary defensive paths) + 5 CLI shape pins (`cmd_ready` exists + wired into handlers + subparser registered + empty-ledger smoke + populated-ledger smoke). **The operator can now commit wall-clock time confidently.** PRD ¬ß36.5 priority #1 row updated with hardening citation. **NEXT**: operator decision ‚Äî (a) start Phase 9 cadence (run `bash scripts/run_live_fire_graduation_soak.sh` repeatedly; watch `python3 scripts/live_fire_graduation_soak.py ready` for graduation signals) OR (b) execute Priority #2 `--rerun-from` + `/replay` (~3d Temporal Observability spine) OR (c) sweep Priority #3 6-arc UX/autonomy slices (~15-20h). See v2.36 below for ¬ß36 brutal review details.)

Bounded multiplier [0.5, 2.0] structurally cannot bypass global cap. Decision A1 per-cluster JSONL via `cross_process_jsonl.flock_*`. Decision A3 SemanticIndex-optional cluster_id resolution with `_global` fallback. Decision E1 defers decay math to `_scoring_primitives.recency_weight` (zero duplication, AST-pinned). Auto-decay via 5-value closed enum (`STALE_FOCUS` / `RECURRENCE_LOOP` / `OPERATOR_RESET` / `NONE` / `DISABLED`). 3 graduated curiosity-aware sensors: OpportunityMiner / ProactiveExploration / CapabilityGap. CoherenceAuditor RECURRENCE_DRIFT initial producer wired (GENERATE/VERIFY producers deferred to Slice 5b for tight regression scope). `/curiosity` REPL + `GET /observability/curiosity[/region/{id}]` + `EVENT_TYPE_CURIOSITY_CHANGED` SSE. 5 AST pins + 6 FlagRegistry seeds. **NEXT (per ¬ß32.8 v3 sequencing)**: Upgrade 2 DecisionRecord Causality Graph ‚Üí M10 ArchitectureProposer. See v2.12 below for items 1-5 single-day burst.)

Three architectural axes closed simultaneously: (a) failure-mode + action-outcome form symmetric in-context embodiment ASCO axis; (b) bounded epistemic loop closes per-op information-economy contract structurally (cost-gated routes refuse PROBE/SBT); (c) Slice 5b consolidation graduated cognitive substrate that Upgrade 1 then composed. ~290 new tests landed across the four arcs. Estimated calendar to remaining items 6‚Äì11 + Venom V1‚ÄìV4 + GitHub Patterns B+C: 5‚Äì7 weeks (reduced from v2.11's 8‚Äì10-week estimate by the four single-day closures). **NEXT (per ¬ß32.8 v3 sequencing)**: M9 CuriosityGradient ‚Üí Upgrade 2 DecisionRecord Causality Graph ‚Üí M10 ArchitectureProposer (refined per ¬ß32.4). ## 1. Executive Summary

Ouroboros + Venom (O+V) is the autonomous self-development governance engine of JARVIS. It is the **proactive autonomous opposite of Claude Code (CC)** ‚Äî where CC requires a human to ask, O+V should observe, hypothesize, propose, validate, and ship without prompting (with human-in-loop escalation only when context warrants it).

### Where we stand (2026-05-05 ‚Äî post-Move-7+8 closures + Wave 3 hygiene + ¬ß28.5.1 closure + ¬ß33 meta-pattern catalog)

**Refresh marker (2026-05-05)**: this section was originally written 2026-04-29; what follows below remains historically correct as of that date. The May 5 reality (post ¬ß1 refresh) is captured in ¬ß36 ‚Äî see "Where we stand v10" for the up-to-date one-page picture. Critical deltas since 2026-04-29:

Closes the ¬ß29.4 v3 Move 7 framing ‚Äî RSI drift now bounded mathematically in BOTH axes (Move 4 architectural-promise drift + Move 7 semantic-meaning drift). 470/470 regression tests across the full sweep.
- **Move 8 Proactive Curiosity Loop FULLY CLOSED 2026-05-05** (3 slices end-to-end same-day): closes the auto-spawn-exploration-ops half of M9's producer-consumer loop. `proactive_curiosity_reader.py` reader + ProactiveExplorationSensor wire-up (3rd signal source ‚Äî composes existing `scan_once`, no parallel poll loop) + ¬ß33.1 graduation contract. RSI exploration-axis closed structurally; substrate-ready, master flag stays default-FALSE pending operator-paced empirical cadence.
- **Wave 3 hygiene arc FULLY CLOSED 2026-05-05** (6 of 6 items shipped same-day): vector #11 monotonic migration + vector #9 sensitive-flag masking + vector #10 AutoCommitter race + vector #8 Versioned-Artifact-Contract + ¬ß28.5.1 invariant_drift_store flock + Move 8 status reconciliation. Two new substrate primitives crystallized: `cross_process_jsonl.async_flock_critical_section` (first async-safe cross-process lock) + `meta.versioned_artifact` (canonical schema-versioning contract ‚Äî ¬ß33.5 pattern).
- **¬ß28.5.1 4-phases-not-extracted hygiene CLOSED 2026-05-05**: investigation revealed all 11 phases ALREADY extracted via `phase_runners/` package ‚Äî brutal-review entry was stale. Closure was documentation-update + structural pin (`test_phase_runner_extraction_closure.py` 31/31 green) matching Move 8 Wave 3 Item 1 reconciliation pattern.
- **¬ß33 Reusable Meta-Patterns catalog crystallized 2026-05-05**: 5 disciplines documented (Graduation Contract / Producer-Bridge / Slice 5b Naming-Convention Cage / Per-Cluster flock'd JSONL Persistence / Versioned-Artifact-Contract). The remaining edge-case fragility is enumerated in ¬ß26.4 and addressed by the three systemic upgrades in ¬ß26.5.

### Where we're going

A-level reliable execution from A-level vision ‚Äî measurable by:
- Sustained 90%+ session completion rate (currently variable; `/metrics 7d` now answers this concretely)
- Cross-session learning evidence (‚úÖ delivered: PostmortemRecall + DirectionInferrer arc-context + ConversationBridge + UserPreferenceMemory + SemanticIndex + LastSessionSummary)
- Self-directed goal formation (‚úÖ delivered: SelfGoalFormationEngine + Hypothesis pairing + BacklogSensor auto-proposed entries with operator-review-tier safety)
- Conversational mode parity with CC (‚úÖ delivered: `/chat` REPL classified + audit-trailed + 3 concrete executors against real systems)
- Convergence metric trending in the Wang sense (‚úÖ delivered: `INSUFFICIENT_DATA` problem statement resolved by Phase 4 P4 graduation)
- **Adaptive immune system** that grows stricter as the shell expands (üöÄ in flight: Pass C Slices 1-4 landed; 5-6 pending; per-slice graduation cadence post-arc-closure)
- A-level reliability metric: **70%+ operator-approval rate** for adaptive proposals over a 30-day window (Pass C arc-closure criterion per ¬ß10.3)

This PRD lays out a phased roadmap to close the gap. **The gap is internal to JARVIS, not external.** External provider quality is sufficient; what's missing is the orchestration layer that converts that intelligence into self-directing, self-improving, self-tightening behavior.

Pure stdlib **Pearson correlation kernel** (Py 3.9 compat manual implementation since `statistics.correlation` was added in 3.10) computes per-category correlation between exploration score and verify_passed binary. Identifies high-value (highest correlation) + low-value (lowest correlation) categories. If `(high - low) >= JARVIS_ADAPTATION_CORRELATION_DELTA` (default 0.3) AND ‚â• JARVIS_ADAPTATION_REBALANCE_THRESHOLD (default 10) ops in window, proposes raise-high (DEFAULT_RAISE_PCT=20%) + lower-low (DEFAULT_LOWER_PCT=10%, hard-floored at JARVIS_ADAPTATION_WEIGHT_FLOOR_PCT=50% of original AND MIN_WEIGHT_VALUE=0.01 absolute). **Net-tighten guarantee enforced at three layers**: (a) DEFAULT_LOWER_PCT < DEFAULT_RAISE_PCT constants pin; (b) caller-passed lower_pct >= raise_pct gets clamped to raise_pct//2; (c) defensive mass-conservation check at mine-time refuses to propose if Œ£(new) < Œ£(old) somehow. Surface validator: kind=`rebalance_weight` + sha256-hash + threshold + summary contains BOTH `‚Üë` AND `‚Üì` tokens + summary contains `net +` indicator. Idempotent proposal_id (sha256 of high+low+new_weights vector rounded 6dp). Pearson kernel handles edge cases: short input / mismatched lengths / zero-variance returns 0.0; never raises. Reads `.jarvis/adapted_category_weights.yaml`, returns `List[AdaptedRebalanceEntry]` of operator-approved per-category weight rebalances from Pass C Slice 5. New module-level helper `compute_effective_category_weights(base_weights, adapted=None)` returns the merged weights dict ‚Äî applying the latest valid rebalance IF it passes all three defense-in-depth net-tighten checks. **Cage rule (per Pass C ¬ß4.1)**: net cage strictness ONLY RISES ‚Äî Slice 5 is the only Pass C surface where individual values appear to fall, but mass-conservation keeps the whole vector tightening. Three independent defense-in-depth layers enforced at activation: **(a) Sum invariant**: `Œ£(new) ‚â• Œ£(base)` enforced by `_net_tighten_check` ‚Äî doctored YAML with lower sum REJECTED ‚Üí return base unchanged. **(b) Per-category floor**: each new weight ‚â• HALF_OF_BASE √ó base[k] (matches Slice 5 miner's 50% floor). **(c) Absolute floor**: each new weight ‚â• MIN_WEIGHT_VALUE=0.01. **Schema invariant**: output ALWAYS contains every base_weights key (preserving structure); unknown adapted keys silently dropped (Pass C cannot ADD categories ‚Äî that's a Pass B Order-2 amendment). Constants: MAX_ADAPTED_REBALANCES=8 + MAX_WEIGHT_VALUE=100.0 + MIN_WEIGHT_VALUE=0.01 + HALF_OF_BASE=0.5 + MAX_YAML_BYTES=4 MiB. Per-entry hardening: weights dict with non-string keys / non-numeric / weight<=0 / empty SKIPPED; weight > MAX_WEIGHT_VALUE clamped; category keys lowercased + stripped. **Latest-occurrence-wins** (only the LAST entry is consulted ‚Äî matches Slice 5 miner's "ONE rebalance per cycle" design; earlier valid entries are NOT a fallback if latest is REJECTED). Master flag `JARVIS_EXPLORATION_LEDGER_LOAD_ADAPTED_CATEGORY_WEIGHTS` (default false until graduation cadence). YAML path env-overridable via `JARVIS_ADAPTED_CATEGORY_WEIGHTS_PATH`. Helper accepts pre-loaded list for hot-path amortization. Stdlib + adaptation.ledger only ‚Äî never raises. Hardcoded import-ban list (no exploration_engine / risk_tier_floor / scoped_tool_backend / orchestrator / phase_runners). Layered evidence: **57 regression pins** (`tests/governance/test_phase_7_5_adapted_category_weight_loader.py`) covering 6 module constants + master flag truthy/falsy + dataclass-frozen + `__post_init__` sorted-keys + float-coercion + master-flag short-circuit + 14 YAML reader pins (missing-yaml / oversize / unreadable / empty / no-PyYAML / parse-error / non-mapping-doc / missing-rebalances-key / non-list / non-mapping-entry-skip / cap-truncate / happy-path / clamp-too-large + lowercases-keys) + 10 `_parse_entry` pins (missing/non-mapping/non-string-key/blank-key/non-numeric/zero/negative/clamp/empty/lowercases) + 11 `compute_effective_category_weights` cage pins (loader-off / loader-on-no-entries / valid-applied / **sum-invariant-violated-rejected** / **per-category-floor-violated-rejected** / **absolute-floor-violated-rejected** / unknown-adapted-keys-dropped / partial-adapted-uses-base-for-missing / **latest-wins-only-last-consulted** / schema-invariant-output-has-all-base-keys / loader-exception-falls-back / returns-dict-not-same-object) + 4 `_net_tighten_check` direct pins (equal-sum-passes / lower-sum-rejected / per-category-floor-rejected / missing-adapted-key-uses-base) + 3 authority invariants (no banned imports / stdlib + adaptation only / no subprocess+network). Combined regression spine: **357/357 tests green** across Phase 7.1+7.2+7.3+7.4+7.5 + Pass C Slice 1+5. **üéâ Phase 7.5 closes the 5/5 Pass C activation-surface set** ‚Äî all five adaptive surfaces (SemanticGuardian patterns / IronGate floors / per-Order budgets / risk-tier ladder / category weights) now have substrate-complete boot loaders + cage-rule helpers. Caller wiring (orchestrator + `exploration_engine.py` consuming `compute_effective_category_weights()`) is a follow-up consistent with the substrate-first pattern.   - [x] Phase 7.6 ‚Äî `adaptation/hypothesis_probe.py` bounded HypothesisProbe primitive ‚Äî **closes the autonomous-curiosity gap** (this PR). New module ships the primitive (data model + cage + runner) plus a Protocol for the evidence prober ‚Äî production wires this to a read-only Venom subset; tests inject fakes; **default is `_NullEvidenceProber` (zero cost ‚Äî a misconfigured caller cannot accidentally hit a paid API)**. **Three independent termination guarantees** ALWAYS fire structurally ‚Äî no prober configuration can override them: (1) **Call cap** `MAX_CALLS_PER_PROBE_DEFAULT=5` (env-overridable via `JARVIS_HYPOTHESIS_PROBE_MAX_CALLS`); (2) **Wall-clock cap** `TIMEOUT_S_DEFAULT=30.0` (env-overridable via `JARVIS_HYPOTHESIS_PROBE_TIMEOUT_S`) ‚Äî measured against `time.monotonic()` (NOT wall clock ‚Äî defends against system clock changes mid-probe); (3) **Diminishing-returns** sha256 fingerprint of every round's evidence ‚Äî terminate `INCONCLUSIVE_DIMINISHING` if round N+1 returns same fingerprint as N. **Read-only tool allowlist** frozen set: `{read_file, search_code, get_callers, glob_files, list_dir}` (production `EvidenceProber` MUST honor at the implementation site; the loader exposes the constant). 9-value `ProbeVerdict` enum: CONFIRMED / REFUTED / 4 INCONCLUSIVE_* / 3 SKIPPED_* (master_off / no_prober / empty_hypothesis). Frozen `ProbeRoundResult` (per-round) + frozen `ProbeResult` (terminal). Pre-checks fire BEFORE any round: master flag off / empty claim / empty expected_outcome / no prober. **Defense-in-depth**: confirmed/refuted signal with EMPTY evidence does NOT terminate (treats as continue) ‚Äî prevents stuck-positive prober from claiming victory without proof. Bounded sizes: `MAX_EVIDENCE_CHARS_PER_ROUND=4096` + `MAX_NOTES_CHARS=1024` (truncation appends `...(truncated)` marker). Master flag `JARVIS_HYPOTHESIS_PROBE_ENABLED` (default false until graduation cadence). Stdlib-only import surface ‚Äî does NOT import HypothesisLedger / tool_executor / Venom (Protocol-typed prober; concrete implementations live elsewhere). Per the PRD spec: confirmed hypotheses become adaptation proposals (feeds Slice 2 + 3 mining surfaces); refuted hypotheses become POSTMORTEMs (feeds PostmortemRecall) ‚Äî bridge wiring is a follow-up.
