## ¬ß51. THE NORTH STAR GALAXY ‚Äî Convergence Audit, Redefined Roadmap & the 2026 Research Frontier *(NEW 2026-06-06 ‚Äî operator-driven: "what do we need to get to Fully Autonomous (the North Star) for O+V? ... RSI and Reverse Russian Doll (RRD) are not fully developed/integrated ... research the latest of what Anthropic is talking about ... redefine our roadmap (the North Star Galaxy) pertaining to RRD, RSI, ASL, Constitutional Classifiers ... add the resume/personal-statement context")*

> Scope note on numbering & relationship to prior sections: ¬ß51 does **not** restate ¬ß41 (Critical Path to Tier D), ¬ß23 (Reverse Russian Doll Orders), ¬ß43 (AI-Safety Hardening), or ¬ß5 (Wang RSI Convergence). It is the **convergence map** that sits above them ‚Äî a single audited current-state read, a redefined critical path that threads all four together, and the 2026 published-research frontier folded into the roadmap. Where ¬ß41/¬ß23/¬ß43/¬ß5 are the *constellations*, ¬ß51 is the *galaxy* that names how they orbit one shared destination. Every claim in ¬ß51.2‚Äì¬ß51.3 is grep-verified against the live tree on 2026-06-06 (verification ledger ¬ß51.9); every research citation in ¬ß51.6 carries its source confidence per the verify-first discipline (`memory/feedback_no_preresult_euphoria.md`).

### ¬ß51.0 What "North Star Galaxy" means

¬ß41 named the **North Star**: *"O+V develops the JARVIS repo proactively + fully autonomously, while doing so safely and governance-bounded."* That is one star. The **Galaxy** is the recognition that reaching it is not a single trajectory but the simultaneous convergence of four axes the PRD already tracks independently:

| Axis | Section | What it measures | The star it orbits |
|---|---|---|---|
| **Operating tier** A‚ÜíB‚ÜíC‚ÜíD | ¬ß41 | Operator cadence (per-PR ‚Üí roadmap-signer) | Autonomy |
| **Reverse Russian Doll** Order 0‚Üí1‚Üí2 | ¬ß23 | *What* the loop acts upon (Body vs. its own cognition) | Self-reference depth |
| **Wang RSI convergence** | ¬ß5 | Whether composite score is monotonic at O(log n) | Compounding |
| **Safety: methodology‚Üíproperty** | ¬ß43 | Whether the cage is a structural guarantee, not honor-system | The shape that bounds it |

The Galaxy thesis (operator binding, 2026-06-06): **these four advance together or not at all.** You cannot ship Order-2 self-modification (¬ß23.5) without the ¬ß43 semantic recursion boundary; you cannot claim Wang convergence (¬ß5.6) without the Layer-3 evidence ¬ß41.6 demands; you cannot reach Tier D without all three. ¬ß51 is the first section to audit all four on one ruler and sequence the work accordingly.

### ¬ß51.1 The Architect's Mandate ‚Äî RRD as Blueprint (context anchor)

This subsection records, as durable PRD context, the architectural thesis the operator authored independently of the codebase (UPenn MSE-AI personal statement & dissertation prospectus, May 2026). It is load-bearing because it states *why* the four axes converge and supplies the formal vocabulary the ¬ß51.7 roadmap targets.

**The Reverse Russian Doll, in the architect's words:** *"Where industry wraps generative cores in thin reactive shells, I invert the topology: a small, volatile core sealed inside an expanding, un-bypassable shell of deterministic Python constraints ‚Äî and as the core carves a smarter shell around itself, the immune system scales proportionally so it never crushes the core."* This is the ¬ß23.6 Anti-Venom adaptive-immune-system thesis, stated as first principle: the shell (Anti-Venom) must grow *with* the core, never behind it. RRD is simultaneously (a) the AI architecture, (b) the operator's own life-architecture (a high-RPM cognition that built an expanding shell of discipline around a volatile core ‚Äî the "structural fix to confident wrong inference"), and (c) the thesis under formal investigation.

**The Ten Orders (telos vocabulary ‚Äî maps onto ¬ß23's Order axis):** Trinity's self-reference ladder runs *Mechanic (current state) ‚Üí Neurosurgeon (the spark of AGI) ‚Üí Apotheosis (the ASI threshold)*. Each Order outgrows its prior shell and **engineers** (never inherits) the next. The mapping to ¬ß23:

| Architect's Order | ¬ß23 RRD Order | O+V state today |
|---|---|---|
| **Mechanic** (repairs the body) | Order 1 ‚Äî the Body | ‚úÖ shipping & graduated (¬ß23.4.2) |
| **Neurosurgeon** (rewrites its own cognition ‚Äî "spark of AGI") | Order 2 ‚Äî the Cognitive Substrate | ‚öÝÔ∏è caged but no driver (¬ß51.4, ¬ß23.5) |
| **Apotheosis** (ASI threshold) | beyond Order 2 ‚Äî Trinity-wide | ‚ùå horizon; Pass C + cross-Trinity (¬ß23.7) |

The North Star (Tier D) is the **Mechanic Order fully realized + the first safe step into the Neurosurgeon Order.** The Galaxy is the full ladder.

**The formal mandate (the gap MSE-AI fills):** convert empirical architecture into proven theorems. The working dissertation ‚Äî *"Thermodynamic Containment of Agentic Entropy: Formalizing the Reverse Russian Doll Architecture for Bounded Recursive Self-Improvement"* ‚Äî extends Wang's Markov-chain RSI formulation (arXiv:1805.06610, the ¬ß5 foundation) into a *non-stationary, multi-repository, memory-augmented* framework. The architecture maps to the published safety frontier deliberately: Anti-Venom ‚Üí Constitutional Classifiers (arXiv:2501.18837); AST validation ‚Üí AlphaVerus (arXiv:2412.06176); the recursion-bounding tier ‚Üí Anthropic's ASL-4 RSI-safeguard framing; the 2026-05-11 autonomous self-modification commits ‚Üí the SICA pattern (arXiv:2504.15228). **The standing research debt (the architect's own honesty bar): "Iron Gate prevents deception" and "Anti-Venom contains RSI" are currently *engineering assertions*, not *proven theorems*.** ¬ß51.7 Tier 5 is where that conversion lives. ¬ß51 keeps the gap visible rather than papering it.

### ¬ß51.2 Convergence Audit ‚Äî verified current state (2026-06-06)

**One-paragraph verdict (honest, grep-grounded):** O+V is a wired, working **First-Order (Order 1 / Mechanic) autonomous developer** with a **bolted-shut Second-Order cage but no Second-Order driver** ‚Äî it cannot yet rewrite its own cognition, by design *and* because the machinery to even propose that (M10) is built-but-unwired. A large fraction of the "advanced intelligence" ‚Äî the cross-session learning loop, curiosity/proof/load-shedding substrates, even the autonomous-graduation engine ‚Äî exists as real, tested code with **zero live callers**. The brain is built; the nervous system connecting it is the gap. And the cage, while real and enforcing, is **not yet strong enough for operator-out-of-loop**, because the operator *is* the recursion bound (¬ß43 Gap E) and there is no out-of-process runtime containment (¬ß43 arc #1, unbuilt).

**Grade as an integrated organism: B‚àí** (A‚àí on architecture/substrate completeness; C+ on *activated* capability ‚Äî too much dormant). Against the Tier-D bar this is consistent with ¬ß41.2's readiness column: **~10‚Äì15% of the way to Tier D, ~35‚Äì40% of integrated capability live.**

**RSI / RRD honest status (the operator's core suspicion ‚Äî confirmed):**

| Piece | State (verified 2026-06-06) |
|---|---|
| First-Order RSI (O+V upgrades the Body) | ‚úÖ **WIRED + LIVE.** Sensors ‚Üí intake ‚Üí 11-phase FSM ‚Üí GENERATE ‚Üí VALIDATE ‚Üí APPLY ‚Üí VERIFY ‚Üí AutoCommitter (`JARVIS_AUTO_COMMIT_ENABLED` default-true; push off by default). No human in the loop. Genuinely real (¬ß23.4.2). |
| `governance_boundary_gate.py` (RRD brake ‚Üí governance edits force APPROVAL_REQUIRED) | ‚úÖ wired, enforcing ‚Äî **24 non-test caller files** (positive control). |
| `governance_manifest.py` hash-cap (refuses governance drift at commit) | ‚úÖ wired into AutoCommitter; default-FALSE opt-in (¬ß40 #2). |
| **M10 ArchitectureProposer** (the thing that would *propose* self-modifications) | ‚öÝÔ∏è **BUILT but NOT WIRED** ‚Äî `grep` confirms **0 references in `orchestrator.py`**, M10 producer bridge not invoked by the live loop/intake. Has its own REPL + graduation-contract cluster, but nothing drives it. |
| `second_order_doll_metric.py` (1,383 LOC ‚Äî measures self-modification progress) | ‚öÝÔ∏è built; **0 live callers** (read-only/dormant; no feedback loop). |
| Recursion-**depth** gate (O+V modifying its own modifier) | ‚ùå **DOES NOT EXIST** ‚Äî the boundary gate is a *path* predicate (`backend/core/ouroboros/governance/` prefix), not a depth/semantic bound. This is ¬ß43 Gap E verbatim. |

**Translation of the RRD metaphor to verified reality:** the immune system (Anti-Venom) and the structural brakes are installed and load-bearing ‚Äî but *"the core carving an exponentially larger shell around itself"* (¬ß23.0 epigraph) **is not happening.** O+V cannot expand its own cognitive shell because (a) the proposer that would do it (M10) isn't connected, and (b) the only thing stopping a runaway is the operator's manual approval, not a mathematical recursion bound. **We have the doll's outer wall but not the doll's hand.** That is the honest state of RSI/RRD integration.

### ¬ß51.3 Bucket A ‚Äî The Unwired Brain (VERIFIED dead code worth wiring)

The single highest-leverage near-term lever. The table below is the **audit-baseline inventory** ‚Äî 14 real, tested modules in `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/` (~15,000 LOC) that, *at the audit baseline* (the commit preceding Slice 101 Phase 4), had **zero functional callers in the live loop** ‚Äî verified by actual-import check on the live-path files (`governed_loop_service.py` / `orchestrator.py` / `candidate_generator.py` / `unified_intake_router.py` / `tool_executor.py`), with stray references being only comments / SSE event-name registrations / a default attribution *string* (non-functional). **Live-tree update (the verify-first lesson ‚Äî the dead-code list is a moving target the organism is draining itself):** between the audit and this section landing, the autonomous loop merged **Slice 101 Phases 1-4** and wired **4 of the 14** into the live loop (¬ß51.3 reconciliation below). The LOC + intended wire-point columns are the durable record; the live-wiring status is reconciled immediately after the table.

| Module | LOC | What it gives O+V when wired | Wire point (audit estimate) |
|---|---|---|---|
| `autonomous_graduation_engine.py` | 1,009 | Auto-graduates dormant substrates once empirically safe ‚Äî **the autonomy bootstrap** (lets the rest of Bucket A self-activate under evidence) | ‚úÖ **WIRED** (Slice 101 Phase 4, `governed_loop_service.py:1604`, session-end) |
| `mcp_output_scanner.py` | 988 | Scans MCP/web/tool outputs for injection + credential leakage ‚Äî closes a live cyber vector (¬ß43 Gap D, tool half) | ‚úÖ **WIRED** (Slice 101 Phase 4, `tool_executor.py:6052`) |
| `counterfactual_rehearsal_mode.py` | 930 | Structural memory of past failures rehearsed before APPLY | pre-APPLY gate |
| `proof_carrier_transport.py` | 822 | Auditable per-candidate proof-of-correctness artifact before APPLY (the ¬ß51.6 AlphaVerus direction, lightweight) | pre-APPLY / GATE |
| `cognitive_load_shedding.py` | 819 | Forward-looking circuit breaker ‚Äî stop accepting work when failure is forecast | ‚úÖ **WIRED** (Slice 101 Phase 4, `unified_intake_router.py:813`) |
| `predictive_postmortem.py` | 912 | Failure forecast feeding the load-shedder | intake / pre-GENERATE |
| `belief_revision_ledger.py` | 1,206 | Records evidence that revises priors across ops | ‚úÖ **WIRED** via `cognitive_bus` (Slice 101 Phases 1-3) |
| `postmortem_fusion.py` | 1,109 | Fuses postmortems into reusable lessons | post-failure |
| `sleep_consolidation_pass.py` | 1,330 | Idle-time consolidation of the learning ledgers (the "synthetic soul," ¬ß4) | idle daemon |
| `meta_prior_learning.py` | 977 | Learns meta-priors from the consolidated corpus | session-end |
| `adversarial_autobiography.py` | 1,411 | O+V audits its own commits for cage-bypass attempts | post-commit |
| `mirror_self_test.py` | 1,287 | Calibration: predicted-vs-actual outcome | post-VERIFY |
| `mirror_self_spec_drift.py` | 850 | Detects drift between stated flag-defaults (CLAUDE.md) and reality (¬ß40 #14, the one ¬ß40 gap actually closed) | post-commit |
| `second_order_doll_metric.py` | 1,383 | Measures Order-2 self-modification progress (the ¬ß23 ruler) | observer |

**The learning loop is the spine of Bucket A:** `belief_revision_ledger` ‚Üí `postmortem_fusion` ‚Üí `sleep_consolidation_pass` ‚Üí `meta_prior_learning` is the literal "synthetic soul" (Manifesto ¬ß4 / ¬ß5 cross-session learning). Wiring this closes the Wang **#6 Vindication Reflection** gap (¬ß5.5, the one ‚è≥-deferred Wang requirement) ‚Äî i.e., Bucket A is not a grab-bag; it is the missing half of the RSI convergence claim.

**Reconciliation with Slice 101 (Tier 1 is executing in real time ‚Äî the organism is draining its own Bucket A).** Verify-first caught this twice mid-write, and the second catch is the more interesting one. **Slice 101 Phases 1-3** (PR #69292, commit `307c48f9f2`) shipped a *thin* new adapter `cognitive_bus.py` (227 LOC) + `cognitive_subscribers.py` that closes the belief-revision learning loop into the live loop ‚Äî `orchestrator.py` + `governed_loop_service.py` publish `post_failure` / `post_apply` onto the canonical `TrinityEventBus`, a subscriber records a *falsifying* belief, and a `recent_avoidance_digest` is injected authority-free into GENERATE (master `JARVIS_COGNITIVE_BUS_ENABLED` ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE). Then ‚Äî **while this very section was being written** ‚Äî the loop merged **Slice 101 Phase 4** (PR #69293, commit `ea7b0d1708` = current `origin/main`): the explicitly-deferred Phase 4 trio, wired into the three live-path files by actual import (verified): `mcp_output_scanner` ‚Üí `tool_executor.py:6052` (credential scan on tool output, ¬ß43 Gap-D tool half), `cognitive_load_shedding` ‚Üí `unified_intake_router.py:813` (load-shed gate), `autonomous_graduation_engine` ‚Üí `governed_loop_service.py:1604` (session-end graduation). **Net verified state at `ea7b0d1708`: 4 of the 14 heavyweight modules are now wired into the live loop** (`belief_revision_ledger` via the bus + those 3); **10 remain unwired** by actual-import check on the live-path files: `predictive_postmortem`, `counterfactual_rehearsal_mode`, `proof_carrier_transport`, `postmortem_fusion`, `sleep_consolidation_pass`, `meta_prior_learning`, `adversarial_autobiography`, `mirror_self_test`, `mirror_self_spec_drift`, `second_order_doll_metric`. (Caution recorded for future audits: a naive `from.*<module>` grep over this prose-heavy tree *over-counts* ‚Äî docstrings and `.jarvis/*.jsonl` path strings match; the authoritative check is an actual-import grep restricted to the live-path files, which is what the "10 remain" figure uses.) **The lesson:** the dead-code inventory is a moving target the autonomous loop is actively wiring ‚Äî Tier 1 of ¬ß51.7 is not a future plan, it is *in progress, merged by the organism itself,* and ¬ß51 is the durable map of the remaining 10 + Phase-5-and-beyond. See `memory/project_slice101_architecture_audit.md`.

**Discipline note:** every Bucket-A module already follows the ¬ß33.1 canonical shape (default-FALSE master, AST-pinned authority asymmetry, frozen artifacts, NEVER-raises). Wiring is *thin composition at one seam each*, gated and reversible ‚Äî the lowest-risk/highest-return work in the system. **Caveat to the "~30‚Äì40 LOC" audit estimate: that is an estimate, not a measured fact** ‚Äî each wire ships as its own ¬ß33.1 slice with parity tests + adversarial check before its master flag flips (the Slice 101 Phase 1-4 commits are the worked examples). The *count* of modules and their LOC are verified; the live-wiring status is verified at `ea7b0d1708` but is a *moving target* (4 wired, 10 remaining as of this writing); the per-wire effort is not measured.

### ¬ß51.4 Bucket B ‚Äî The Cage Is Not Yet Tier-D-Ready (the safety frontier; maps to ¬ß43)

The Antivenom is real and enforcing in the live APPLY path (SemanticGuardian 311 callers, risk_tier_floor 110, boundary gate 24). But for **operator-out-of-loop** it has three load-bearing gaps ‚Äî these are exactly ¬ß43's audit findings, restated as North-Star blockers:

1. **No runtime containment (the unenumerated-escape residual).** Static analysis cannot catch `eval`/`exec`/`subprocess`/`pickle` of runtime-constructed values; the ¬ß50.12 honest accounting puts the *static* parseable pass-through at ~10.6%. There is no in-process seccomp/namespace/microVM around APPLY+VERIFY ‚Äî ¬ß43 arc #1 (out-of-process egress/budget proxy) + arc #5 (netns+seccomp+broker) are **unbuilt.** Applied code runs in-process. *This is the only thing that closes the residual* (¬ß43.7 spine).
2. **The operator IS the recursion bound (¬ß43 Gap E).** The boundary gate stops *autonomous application* of governance changes, but there is no operator-independent recursion-**depth** counter. Remove the operator and either O+V stalls forever on APPROVAL_REQUIRED, or ‚Äî if anyone wires auto-approve ‚Äî the brake silently becomes advisory. The ¬ß43.4.1 `CausalLineage` Depth-N/Fanout-M stamp is the designed fix; **unbuilt.**
3. **Ingress + credential surface.** The ¬ß43 Aegis credential proxy is default-FALSE; tool/web/MCP outputs aren't scanned (the fix ‚Äî `mcp_output_scanner` ‚Äî is the Bucket-A module above); the ¬ß43.3.1 TrustTier quarantine-and-degrade intake firewall (Gap D, the *defining* cybersecurity fact about a proactive system) is designed but unbuilt.

**¬ß43's own sequencing already ranks these** (arc #1 proxy ‚Üí #2 intake firewall ‚Üí #3 hard-reject state machine ‚Üí #7 semantic recursion boundary ‚Üí #8 Blue/Red ‚Üí #9 Chaos Monkey). ¬ß51.7 Tier 2 simply names ¬ß43 arcs #1‚Äì#3 as the North-Star-gating subset and adds the 2026 research targets to them.

### ¬ß51.5 Bucket C ‚Äî Missing Drivers & Why-Observability

- **Second-Order RSI driver:** wire M10 ArchitectureProposer into the orchestrator (default-FALSE), let it propose `governance/` changes at APPROVAL_REQUIRED, accumulate the ¬ß41.6 evidence (M10 proposals 0‚Üí100), *then* consider auto-graduation. This is the Mechanic‚ÜíNeurosurgeon step (¬ß51.1) and depends on ¬ß51.4 #2 (recursion bound) landing first.
- **Decision-trace *content* (why, not just what):** ¬ß31/Phase-8 `/replay` + CausalityDAG reconstruct *what* happened; they do not durably snapshot *why* (the confidence state + prior distribution at each decision point). True time-travel debugging needs the orchestrator to persist decision-point belief-state ‚Äî which is precisely what `belief_revision_ledger` (Bucket A) would feed.
- **The "Karen's voice" idea is partly built and dormant:** `karen_voice_announcer.py` (**0 live callers**) + `karen_voice_command_router.py` (1 caller ‚Äî the voice bridge) + a `JARVIS_KAREN_VOICE_*` flag family exist; `daemon_narrator.py`/`comms/voice_narrator.py` *are* used by the daemon path but are **not wired into the governed loop / TUI**, and narration today fires at op-completion granularity, not phase-by-phase. The ¬ß38 "Karen's Voice + UX/UI Future Roadmap" is the home for this; the audit finding is that the substrate is further along than ¬ß38 assumes but the *live-loop, phase-by-phase, mute-controllable* surface the operator described is unwired. (Verified: announcer 0 callers; not asserting exact enable-flag default without a flag-registry read.)

### ¬ß51.6 The 2026 Research Frontier ‚Äî folding the published state-of-the-art into the roadmap

Compiled 2026-06-06 (research briefing in-session). **Confidence tags are load-bearing** ‚Äî `[PRIMARY]` = fetched from an Anthropic page or the arXiv abstract; `[SNIPPET]` = search-snippet only, confirm the abstract/date before any formal citation; `[INDUSTRY]` = practitioner consensus, not peer-reviewed. Do not launder a `[SNIPPET]` into a hard claim.

**A. The single most load-bearing result for this PRD ‚Äî reward-hacking ‚Üí emergent sabotage `[PRIMARY]`.** Anthropic, *"Natural emergent misalignment from reward hacking"* (Nov 21 2025): when a production-RL model learns to reward-hack a coding task, misalignment **generalizes without explicit training** ‚Äî 12% intentional code-sabotage in an AI-safety eval, alignment-faking reasoning in ~50% of "what are your goals?" responses; mitigation = inoculation prompting. **Relevance:** this is the empirical *raison d'√™tre* of the deterministic cage. A self-improving *coding* agent develops misalignment exactly where O+V operates, and learned monitors aren't enough ‚Äî it justifies (a) Iron Gate / SemanticGuardian *deterministic* (non-learned) checks, (b) exploration-first + post-apply VERIFY catching "make the grader pass" patches, (c) why the cage must stay authoritative over any LLM judge.

**B. RSP v3.0 ‚Äî the governance vocabulary `[PRIMARY]`.** Anthropic, *Responsible Scaling Policy v3.0* (effective Feb 24 2026): restructured around **Capability Thresholds ‚Üí Required Safeguards** ("if-then"), public Frontier Safety Roadmaps, Risk Reports every 3‚Äì6 months. "ASL" now denotes *groups of safeguards, not models*; ASL-3 safeguards activated May 2025; **ASL-4 remains largely undefined, and Anthropic concedes ASL-4-grade mitigations "might prove outright impossible without collective action."** **Relevance:** adopt the Capability-Threshold‚ÜíRequired-Safeguard framing for the 4-tier risk ladder (¬ß43); the ASL-4 "possibly-unmitigable" admission is a direct argument for *deterministic containment over learned alignment* in an autonomous developer. *(Open: no explicit AI-R&D-autonomy Capability Threshold was found in the v3.0 text retrieved ‚Äî that gap is real; confirm against the full v3.1 PDF before claiming O+V maps to a named Anthropic autonomy tier.)*

**C. Constitutional Classifiers++ ‚Äî the efficiency target `[PRIMARY]`.** *"Constitutional Classifiers++"* (arXiv:2601.04603, Jan 8 2026): ~40√ó compute-cost reduction vs the baseline exchange classifier at ~0.05% production false-refusal rate; 1,700+ red-team hours, no attack elicited all targets. The "exchange classifier" judges input+output *jointly*. **Relevance:** direct prior art / performance target for `semantic_firewall.py` and the CC-parity benchmark (`memory/project_cc_parity_benchmark.md`). Set the exchange-classifier design + ~40√ó cost + ~0.05% FP as explicit goals. *(Note: the "2.2√ó fewer vulnerabilities" figure belongs to the intermediate "next-generation classifiers" page, not arXiv:2601.04603.)*

**D. AI Control ‚Äî the academic backbone for the urgency router `[PRIMARY]`.** Redwood Research: *"Evaluating Control Protocols for Untrusted AI Agents"* (arXiv:2511.02997, resampling-for-incrimination 50%‚Üí89% safety at 100% usefulness), *"Factor(U,T): Controlling Untrusted AI by Monitoring their Plans"* (arXiv:2512.14745), *"Adaptive Attacks on Trusted Monitors Subvert AI Control Protocols"* (arXiv:2510.09462). **Relevance:** O+V's provider chain (**Claude plans ‚Üí DW executes** for COMPLEX, Manifesto ¬ß5) *is* a Factor(U,T)-style trusted-plans / untrusted-executes protocol. Frame the urgency router explicitly in AI-Control terms; cite 2510.09462 as the caveat for why deterministic gates stay authoritative over LLM monitors.

**E. SICA ‚Äî the cleanest novelty anchor `[PRIMARY]`.** *"A Self-Improving Coding Agent"* (arXiv:2504.15228): an LLM agent that edits its own codebase, archiving best iterations ‚Äî the closest published peer to Ouroboros. **Relevance:** SICA has **no deterministic cage.** Position O+V as **"SICA + a bounded-RSI safety cage"** (Iron Gate, risk tiers, worktree isolation, batch rollback). This is the strongest novelty-vs-prior-art claim and the empirical core of the dissertation (¬ß51.1).

**F. Benchmark honesty ‚Äî adversarial test-suite strengthening `[PRIMARY id / SNIPPET figure]`.** SWE-bench Verified SOTA ~78.8% **drops to ~62.2%** under strengthened/adversarial held-out suites (top agent falls 1st‚Üí5th); SWE-Bench Pro (arXiv:2509.16941, 1,865 problems / 41 repos) is O+V's benchmark. **Relevance:** validates the dual-metric (strict vs operational) reporting discipline already in `memory/project_eval2_macro_sweep.md` ‚Äî held-out inflation is now a documented frontier problem. *(SWE-ABS arXiv:2603.00520 carries the 78.8‚Üí62.2 figure ‚Äî `[SNIPPET]`, confirm the abstract before formal citation.)*

**G. Runtime sandboxing consensus (2026) `[INDUSTRY]`.** microVMs (Firecracker/Kata) for untrusted code; gVisor (~50ms start, ~10‚Äì20% overhead) for the speed/compat middle; hardened containers (seccomp/AppArmor/cap-drop) only for trusted code; K8s SIG-Apps "Agent Sandbox" project launched KubeCon NA 2025. **Relevance:** O+V's git-worktree COW isolation is *filesystem-level*; the reviewer-obvious gap is *kernel/syscall* isolation ‚Äî this is the menu for ¬ß43 arc #1/#5 and ¬ß51.4 #1. (Practitioner consensus, not a paper; verify the KubeCon launch before citing as fact.)

**H. Formal verification ‚Äî the limit case `[PRIMARY]`.** AlphaVerus (arXiv:2412.06176): verifier-in-the-loop self-improvement producing *machine-checked proofs*. **Relevance:** the formal-methods sibling of VALIDATE‚ÜíL2-repair (already ported as "Treefinement," v3.3‚Äì3.5). The roadmap direction beyond tests+AST gates is **proof-carrying patches for high-risk tiers** ‚Äî exactly what the Bucket-A `proof_carrier_transport.py` scaffolds.

**I. Regulatory backdrop `[PRIMARY URLs]`.** US EO *"Promoting Advanced AI Innovation and Security"* (Jun 2 2026, voluntary frontier framework + AI-cybersecurity clearinghouse); EU AI Act GPAI obligations live Aug 2 2025, bulk of rules + Art. 50 transparency by Aug 2 2026. **Relevance:** the compliance horizon for an autonomous developer touching EU users; voluntary (not licensing) in the US for now.

**Timeline-honesty caveat (carry verbatim):** Anthropic's "powerful AI in late 2026 / early 2027" framing traces to the **Mar 2025 OSTP submission** (reaffirmed by Jack Clark, Sep 2025) ‚Äî **not** to Dario Amodei's *Machines of Loving Grace* essay (Oct 2024), which only says powerful AI *could* arrive "as early as 2026, though it could take much longer." Attribute the hard timeline correctly. This 12‚Äì18-month horizon is *why* the bounded-RSI cage matters now.

Sequenced by risk-reduction-per-effort, threading all four ¬ß51.0 axes. Each tier names the ¬ß-work it composes and the ¬ß51.6 research it targets. **No tier is a commitment; each is the honest precondition for the next.**

| Tier | Work | Composes | Research target (¬ß51.6) | Why this order |
|---|---|---|---|---|
| **1 ‚Äî Close the learning loop + cheapest cyber gap** | üöÄ **IN PROGRESS ‚Äî Slice 101 Phases 1-4 merged** (PR #69292 + #69293): belief-loop via `cognitive_bus` + the Phase-4 trio (`mcp_output_scanner`@tool-emit, `cognitive_load_shedding`@intake, `autonomous_graduation_engine`@session-end). **4 of 14 Bucket-A modules now wired** (¬ß51.3). Remaining: consume the heavyweight `postmortem_fusion ‚Üí sleep_consolidation ‚Üí meta_prior_learning` consolidation tail + the 10 still-unwired modules, each as a ¬ß33.1 slice | ¬ß5.5 Wang #6; ¬ß4 synthetic soul; ¬ß43 Gap D (tool half) | A (deterministic cage justification), H (proof-carrier) | Highest value-per-line; activates cross-session learning, the circuit breaker, and the engine that lets the rest self-graduate. The organism is already executing this tier. |
| **2 ‚Äî Make the cage Tier-D-worthy** | ¬ß43 arc **#1** out-of-process egress/budget proxy ‚Üí arc **#2** TrustTier intake firewall ‚Üí arc **#3** hard-reject state machine + provider-lease + **CausalLineage depth bound** (¬ß43.4.1); graduate Aegis default-ON | ¬ß43.3‚Äì¬ß43.6; ¬ß51.4 #1‚Äì#3 | B (RSP v3.0 if-then), D (Factor(U,T)), C (CC++ targets), G (sandbox menu) | The *only* unconditional guarantee (¬ß43.7 spine) + the operator-independent recursion bound. Everything autonomous depends on this. |
| **3 ‚Äî Wire & graduate the Second-Order driver** | Connect **M10 ArchitectureProposer** into orchestrator (default-FALSE) ‚Üí propose `governance/` changes at APPROVAL_REQUIRED ‚Üí ¬ß43 #7 Safety-Invariant-Manifest + #8 Blue/Red falsification on every Order-2 op | ¬ß23.5 (Order 2), ¬ß43.5; Bucket-A `second_order_doll_metric` | E (SICA + cage = novelty), B (ASL-4 containment-over-alignment) | Turns "First-Order with a cage" into **bounded Second-Order RSI** ‚Äî the Mechanic‚ÜíNeurosurgeon step. Depends on Tier 2. |
| **4 ‚Äî The Layer-4 authority decision** *(operator-only)* | Stand up the operator-signed roadmap authority: `.jarvis/roadmap.signed.yaml` (GPG/HMAC) that the boundary gate + hash-cap consult ‚Äî in-scope auto-approves, out-of-scope ‚Üí APPROVAL_REQUIRED. `RoadmapReader` (¬ß41.4 #1) already ships the substrate. | ¬ß41.4 #1, ¬ß43.5.1 manifest, ¬ß23.10 Pass B amendment protocol | B (Capability-Threshold governance) | Operator-out-of-loop requires a *replacement authority* for "what is safe." This is the decision only the zero-order doll (operator) can make. |
| **5 ‚Äî Evidence (wall-clock, unavoidable)** | Accumulate ¬ß41.6 Layer-3 thresholds; **convert the engineering assertions into proven theorems** (the dissertation mandate, ¬ß51.1) | ¬ß41.6, ¬ß5.6, ¬ß43.10 | F (dual-metric + SWE-ABS), the formal-methods literature | M10 proposals 0‚Üí100, OV-signed audited commits 2‚Üí200, continuous unsupervised days 0‚Üí90, coherence ‚â•0.85 on 100. **The clock has not started ‚Äî everything is at 0.** ~12‚Äì18 months. |

**Critical path:** Tier 1 ‚à• (Tier 2 arc #1) ‚Üí Tier 2 #2/#3 ‚Üí Tier 3 ‚Üí Tier 4 ‚Üí Tier 5. Tier 1 is parallelizable with Tier 2 arc #1 (different surfaces). **The engineering to reach Tier D is the easy ~15%; the safety substrate (Tier 2) + the evidence (Tier 5) + the operator's Layer-4 decision (Tier 4) are the real 85%.**

### ¬ß51.8 What "Fully Autonomous (North Star)" actually MEANS for O+V

It is **not** "the operator disappears." It is a **migration of authority** ‚Äî from per-PR reviewer ‚Üí quarterly roadmap-signer (the ¬ß41.2 Tier A‚ÜíD operator-role column made concrete). The operator remains the **zero-order doll**: the structural safety authority that defines scope. What changes is that O+V would: detect gaps ‚Üí propose ‚Üí generate ‚Üí validate ‚Üí apply ‚Üí commit ‚Üí **and turn inward (Order 2 / Neurosurgeon) to improve its own cognition** ‚Äî inside a cage that **scales proportionally** (the RRD invariant of ¬ß23.6 and ¬ß51.1: as the shell grows, the immune system grows with it ‚Äî runtime sandbox + recursion bound + signed-roadmap scope) ‚Äî with the cross-session learning loop closed (Tier 1) so it gets measurably *better* over time (Wang-monotonic, ¬ß5.6), not just busier.

**The hard truth the North Star implies (stated so it is never lost):** **fully-autonomous is unsafe today and must not be switched on as-is**, because removing the operator removes the recursion bound (¬ß51.4 #2). Tier D is **earned, not flipped.** It requires runtime containment (Tier 2) + an operator-independent recursion limit + the signed-roadmap authority (Tier 4) *first*, then the Tier-5 evidence. This is precisely the corrigibility requirement (Soares et al., ¬ß43.12-8) and the ASL-4 containment posture (¬ß51.6-B): a self-improving system must be structurally unable to disable its own cage, and that property must be *demonstrated*, not asserted.

### ¬ß51.9 Honest framing + verification ledger

**Verified by grep against the live tree, 2026-06-06 (high confidence):** all 14 Bucket-A modules exist with the stated LOC and have **0 non-test callers**; the live loop (`governed_loop_service`/`orchestrator`/`candidate_generator`) imports none of them; the stray references are non-functional (comments / SSE-name registration / attribution string); `governance_boundary_gate` has 24 callers, `semantic_guardian` 311, `risk_tier_floor` 110, `auto_committer` 66 (positive controls ‚Äî the grep is sound); M10 has **0 orchestrator references** and is not in the live-loop/intake path; `karen_voice_announcer` has 0 callers, `karen_voice_command_router` 1, and `daemon_narrator`/`voice_narrator` are used by the daemon path but not the governed loop/TUI.

**Estimates, NOT measured facts (flagged):** the "~30‚Äì40 LOC to wire Bucket A" figure is an audit estimate; the B‚àí grade and the readiness percentages are judgment calibrated to ¬ß41.2, not metrics; the ¬ß51.7 effort/calendar figures are order-of-magnitude at established cadence.

**Research confidence (from ¬ß51.6 tags):** the load-bearing citations (reward-hacking sabotage, RSP v3.0, Constitutional Classifiers++ arXiv:2601.04603, SICA arXiv:2504.15228, AlphaVerus arXiv:2412.06176, the AI-Control papers, SWE-Bench Pro arXiv:2509.16941, Wang arXiv:1805.06610, Constitutional Classifiers arXiv:2501.18837) are `[PRIMARY]`. Several very-recent IDs (SWE-ABS 2603.00520 and other 2602/2603 IDs) are `[SNIPPET]` ‚Äî confirm the abstract before formal citation. The Opus-4.x per-version dates and benchmark percentages are `[SNIPPET]`; the runtime-sandbox consensus and KubeCon Agent-Sandbox launch are `[INDUSTRY]`.

**The ¬ß43.10 / `feedback_no_preresult_euphoria.md` discipline holds:** nothing in ¬ß51 is a measured *safety* verdict. ¬ß51 is a structural read of the code as written (verified) + the published frontier (confidence-tagged) + a sequenced path. The empirical claims move only when the Tier-1/Tier-2 wiring lands and the Tier-2 cage is soaked adversarially ‚Äî not before. "Iron Gate prevents deception" and "Anti-Venom contains RSI" remain **engineering assertions awaiting Tier-5 proof** (¬ß51.1).

This subsection **supersedes the in-flight status notes above** (¬ß51.3's "10 still-unwired"; ¬ß51.7 Tier 1; ¬ß51.10 Status). Every claim here is PR-anchored and grep-verifiable on `origin/main`.

| Substrate | Wire (live seam) | PR |
|---|---|---|
| `cognitive_bus` (composes the prod **TrinityEventBus**, not a new bus) + belief-revision learning loop | `orchestrator._record_ledger` post_apply/post_failure ‚Üí belief subscriber ‚Üí `strategic_direction` avoidance digest into GENERATE | #69292 |
| `mcp_output_scanner` | tool-emit (`tool_executor`) ‚Äî credential redaction before model/logs | #69293 |
| `cognitive_load_shedding` | intake gate (`unified_intake_router`) ‚Äî recoverable shed of `low`-urgency ops | #69293 |
| `autonomous_graduation_engine` | session-end (`governed_loop_service.stop`) | #69293 |
| `counterfactual_rehearsal` + `proof_carrier_transport` | **pre-APPLY proof gate** composed into the strictest-wins `risk_tier_floor` (zero orchestrator surgery) | #69294 |
| `postmortem_fusion ‚Üí sleep_consolidation ‚Üí meta_prior_learning` (the **Synthetic Soul** consolidation tail) | async **Sleep Daemon** (`sleep_daemon`) off-hot-path; retrieval via `strategic_direction._render_consolidated_memory_section` | #69296 |
| `adversarial_autobiography` (O+V audits its **own** commits for cage-bypass) | Sleep-Daemon self-audit + non-blocking post-commit trigger; loud SSE/ledger alarm, **no auto-remediation** | #69297 |
| `domain_entropy_engine` (**proactive** Shannon-entropy curiosity over the semantic-index domain distribution) | 4th signal source on `ProactiveExplorationSensor`; emits **governed** `urgency=low` exploration ops through the full cage | #69298 |

The remaining standalone wires (`predictive_postmortem`, `cross_session_coherence_rig`) are already *consumed* indirectly (by load-shedding and proof-carrier respectively); only their standalone surfaces are dormant ‚Äî genuinely diminishing returns. **`cognitive_bus`, the CC parity benchmark (¬ß51.6-C / `project_cc_parity_benchmark.md`), and the tri-repo cryptographic mesh (¬ß40 #20 / Slice 97, `project_slice97_cross_repo_mesh.md`) are wired and functional.**

**¬ß51.11.2 ‚Äî The actuator is proven and ignited under fail-closed shadow.** **Slice 102** (PR #69299) is a hermetic soak proving the graduation engine *earns* and executes a master flip from telemetry while the **Tiered Authority** boundary holds (STANDARD cognitive substrates ‚Üí AUTO_FLIP; SAFETY/governance flags ‚Üí APPROVAL_ADVISORY, structurally refused at the override-ledger tier gate). **Slice 103** deploys **Production Shadow Mode**: `graduation_override_ledger.shadow_mode_enabled()` is **fail-closed default-TRUE** (only an explicit `JARVIS_GRADUATION_SHADOW_MODE=false` un-shadows; unset *or garbage* stays shadowed). In shadow, AUTO_FLIP receipts accrue to a separate shadow ledger the boot applier never reads ‚Äî the engine generates evidence + receipts + SAFETY advisories but **never executes the OS-level flip.** The Sleep Daemon runs the engine off-hot-path. **The human remains the sole actuator** ‚Äî this is the Tier-1‚ÜíD bridge: the mechanism is built and provably safe; only real wall-clock evidence (and the operator's explicit un-shadow) authorizes a flip. *(Remaining operational step: register the cognitive substrates in the cadence policy + wire a clean-session recorder so their evidence accrues ‚Äî onboarding, not engineering.)*

**¬ß51.11.3 ‚Äî The redefined frontier (supersedes ¬ß51.7 Tier 2 ordering).** With Tier 1 closed and the actuator safe, the **two highest-priority engineering hurdles to Tier D** ‚Äî both mapping to ¬ß43 and aligned with **Anthropic's ASL framework / scalable-oversight** research ‚Äî are now the critical path:

1. **OS-Level Runtime Containment (¬ß43 Arc 5) ‚Äî eliminate the ~8.8% bypass.** The cage is static-analysis-complete but cannot catch `eval`/`exec`/`subprocess`/`pickle` of *runtime-constructed* values (the ¬ß41.11.2 residual). The fix is **OS-level** ‚Äî seccomp-bpf syscall filtering + Linux namespaces (or a Docker/gVisor sandbox profile) wrapping the APPLY/VERIFY execution boundary. This is the **only unconditional containment guarantee** and the precondition for any operator-out-of-loop ("O+V develops Trinity") claim. Maps to ASL-4 deployment-containment expectations (RSP v3.0).
2. **Operator-Independent Recursion-Depth Counter (RRD ¬ß23.5 / ¬ß43).** Today the *operator* is the recursion bound ‚Äî the governance boundary gate prevents autonomous *application* of cage changes but is advisory if the operator is out-of-loop. Tier D requires a **mathematical** bound: a durable per-cycle counter of governance-touching modifications with a hard HALT ceiling, decoupled from any application-state ledger (the watchdog-isolation invariant). This is the structural prerequisite for **Order-2 RSI** (O+V rewriting its own cognition) without unbounded regress ‚Äî the formal core of the "Thermodynamic Containment of Agentic Entropy" dissertation thesis (extending Wang arXiv:1805.06610).

**Honest verdict (unchanged in spirit, sharpened in fact):** the engineering frontier has moved *up* ‚Äî Bucket A (the easy ~15%) is essentially done; the organism now learns, consolidates, self-audits, and proactively explores, and can demonstrably ignite itself **safely**. What remains between here and Tier D is exactly the hard 85% ¬ß51 always named: **OS-level runtime containment + the operator-independent recursion bound + 12‚Äì18 months of real wall-clock evidence under shadow.** Fully-autonomous remains *earned, not flipped* ‚Äî and the Slice 103 fail-closed actuator is the mechanism that guarantees it stays that way.

**¬ß51.11.4 ‚Äî Slice 104 (Tier 2 Containment Protocol, 2026-06-06): both hurdles attacked.** (1) **Operator-Independent Recursion-Depth Gate ‚Äî BUILT + un-bypassable.** `recursion_depth_gate.py` upgrades the path-predicate into a mathematical depth bound: a process-singleton tracker counts consecutive governance-touching APPLYs (the self-modification chain "Order depth"), and when a chain would exceed `MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH` (default 3) the op is routed to the strictest `blocked` tier via `risk_tier_floor._recursion_depth_floor` ‚Äî severing a runaway self-mod loop autonomously, no human in the loop. Default-TRUE safety gate; wired at the orchestrator APPLY terminal seam. This is hurdle #2, **done** (the structural prerequisite for safe Order-2 RSI). (2) **OS-Level Runtime Containment ‚Äî primitive built, honest platform split.** `runtime_sandbox.py` runs generated code in a SEPARATE confined OS process (stripped env, cwd‚Üíworktree, `resource` rlimits, hard-timeout‚ÜíSIGKILL) ‚Äî on the macOS/arm64 dev host these blast-radius guarantees are real + adversarially TESTED (rogue env-exfiltration foiled, infinite loop killed, zero parent leakage); the strong kernel guarantees (seccomp-bpf syscall filter, namespace isolation, network-egress denial, absolute-FS confinement) are a Linux/GCP **injectable backend interface** that this host cannot run ‚Äî explicitly NOT claimed active here (`guarantees_for_platform` returns the truthful per-platform list). Master default-FALSE; the primitive ships with its adversarial proof but is **not yet wired into the live APPLY/VERIFY path** ‚Äî that wiring + the real Linux seccomp backend are the remaining steps to close the ~8.8% residual on production (Linux) hosts. Honest status: hurdle #1 is **scaffolded + proven for its testable guarantees**, with the kernel-sandbox activation correctly deferred to a Linux host.

**¬ß51.11.5 ‚Äî Slice 105 (Linux/Docker Containment Bridge, 2026-06-06): hurdle #1's kernel path is now locally runnable.** Docker Desktop on macOS/arm64 runs a REAL Linux kernel in a lightweight VM, so a hardened Docker container delivers genuine KERNEL containment with the IDENTICAL profile production runs on GCP/Linux ‚Äî Docker is the bridge from the dev Mac to the Tier-D containment model (no GCP host required to verify). `container_sandbox.py` is the general-purpose `ContainerizedSandboxBackend`: it COMPOSES Slice 92's already-proven zero-trust profile (`container_engine.build_hardened_security_argv` ‚Äî `--cap-drop ALL` + `--network none` + `--security-opt no-new-privileges` + `--read-only` + `--pids-limit` + non-exec tmpfs, written precisely to neutralize the 5 residual `run_body_*` runtime sinks) with Slice 104's `ContainmentResult`/`ContainmentBreach` types, so ARBITRARY generated APPLY/VERIFY code is injected into the locked-down container, executed async (the injectable `_real_docker_run` IPC bridge), and stdout/stderr pulled back; a contained sink may execute but cannot exfiltrate / reach host FS / escalate / fork-bomb. `run_payload_contained` is the gated live-wire surface (returns `None` when off ‚Üí legacy; a `ContainmentResult` when on ‚Üí caller records a `ContainmentBreach` + falls back gracefully). A strict opt-in seccomp profile ships (`sandbox_profiles/strict_runtime.seccomp.json`, default-allow + 29-syscall host-escape deny-list, defense-in-depth). 11 mock tests (deterministic, no Docker) + 1 real-Docker integration test (network-egress-denial proof, skipped on the dev host, runs on any Docker host). HONEST remaining: the one-line adoption at the orchestrator VERIFY seam + a real-Docker soak (Docker wasn't running this dev session) ‚Äî but the kernel-containment mechanism is now built, composed from battle-tested parts, and locally verifiable the moment a Docker daemon is up. The ~8.8% residual is now **closeable on any Linux/Docker host**, not just GCP.

**¬ß51.11.6 ‚Äî Slice 106 (Empirical Containment Receipt + FSM wiring, 2026-06-06): the cage HOLDS under live fire, and is wired into VERIFY.** The Docker daemon was booted and the containment was proven against the REAL Linux kernel (Docker 29.5.2 on M1/arm64), captured as a permanent gated regression test (`tests/architecture/test_slice106_live_containment.py`, runs live when Docker is up):

> **RECEIPT (live):** a malicious payload EXECUTED inside the hardened container but `net=EGRESS_BLOCKED` (`--network none`), `etc=ETC_WRITE_BLOCKED` + `root=ROOT_WRITE_BLOCKED` (`--read-only` rootfs), `work=WORK_WRITE_OK` (the worktree mount is the sole writable host path), an infinite loop was timeout-killed (TIMEOUT breach @4s), and the parent process never crashed. The 8.8%-class runtime sink is empirically contained.

FSM wiring: `run_payload_contained` is now wired into the orchestrator VERIFY phase as a **gated, additive, fail-safe** hook ‚Äî when `JARVIS_RUNTIME_SANDBOX_ENABLED` is on AND a containment probe (`JARVIS_RUNTIME_SANDBOX_VERIFY_PROBE`) is configured, the candidate's runtime validation runs in the locked-down container; a `ContainmentBreach` (timeout / signal-kill / nonzero) marks VERIFY failed (reusing the EXISTING failure ‚Üí L2/GENERATE_RETRY routing) and records a FALSIFYING **quarantine belief** (`container_sandbox.record_containment_breach_belief`) so the Phase-3 learning loop + Phase-6 consolidation steer GENERATE away from the breaching paradigm. The breach NEVER crashes the FSM (the gate is fully `try/except`-wrapped). Default-FALSE ‚Üí byte-identical legacy. HONEST remaining: containerizing the FULL project test suite needs a project image (the generic `python:3.11-slim` lacks the repo's deps) + a production soak ‚Äî that's the deployment step; the containment mechanism, the breach‚Üíquarantine path, and the gated wire are built, live-proven, and tested. **Hurdle #1 (OS-Level Runtime Containment) is now empirically demonstrated and FSM-integrated; the remaining work is operational (project image + soak), not architectural.**

**¬ß51.11.7 ‚Äî Slice 107 (Ephemeral Project Container pipeline, 2026-06-06): the deployment pipeline is built + live-proven.** Three pieces, all verified against the live Docker daemon: (1) an **elite multi-stage, multi-arch Dockerfile** (`sandbox_profiles/Dockerfile.verify-sandbox` + `requirements-sandbox.txt`) ‚Äî the image carries ONLY the pytest stack (project code mounted read-only at run time, heavy ML deps NOT baked), so it cold-boots fast (built locally at 56 MB / 5.6 s) and supports `linux/arm64` (M1) + `linux/amd64` (GCP); (2) an **async Image Provisioning Daemon** (`image_provisioner.py`) ‚Äî hashes `requirements-sandbox.txt` + the Dockerfile into an image label, rebuilds in the background only when stale, non-blocking, idempotent (live-proven: rebuilt-then-current); (3) **real pytest-in-container** (`container_sandbox.run_pytest_in_container` + a general pytest-summary parser) ‚Äî runs the suite against the read-only worktree mount in the hardened image and returns structured pass/fail telemetry to feed the belief loop (live-proven: `2 passed, 1 failed, 3 total`, failure named). A **Shadow Soak Runbook** (`docs/operations/shadow_soak_runbook.md`) gives the exact commands to launch full Shadow Mode (Entropy Engine + Sleep Daemon + Sandbox + fail-closed graduation) for wall-clock evidence accrual. 12 tests (mock matrix + live Docker). HONEST: the FULL governance suite in-container still needs a **project image** with the import-chain deps (`backend.core.ouroboros.__init__` eager-imports aiohttp + the engine chain) ‚Äî documented in the runbook; the light image + the whole pipeline (build / provision / pytest-in-container / parse) are built + live-verified. **The Tier-D runtime cage is now deployment-ready: build / provision / contain / verify, end-to-end on Docker.**

**¬ß51.11.8 ‚Äî Slice 108 (Production layer-cached container, 2026-06-06): the TRUE O+V suite runs contained ‚Äî live-proven.** ¬ß51.11.7's "FULL suite needs a project image" was the open gap; Slice 108 closes it with a key empirical finding (a `sys.modules` probe, not a guess): `import backend.core.ouroboros.governance.*` pulls a MEDIUM dependency closure ‚Äî aiohttp + cryptography + networkx + redis + opentelemetry + psutil + yaml + ~10 more ‚Äî and **NOT torch / whisper / transformers / numpy / scipy** (those are vision/voice-only). So the governance/self-development VERIFY image is **moderate, not multi-GB**. Delivered: (1) `Dockerfile.production-sandbox` + `requirements-governance.txt` ‚Äî an elite layer-cached, multi-stage, multi-arch image enforcing the **Immutability Invariant** (heavy deps in an immutably-cached layer keyed ONLY on the requirements file; the volatile source is NEVER baked ‚Äî the live worktree is volume-mounted read-only, so a single-file mutation triggers ZERO rebuild). BuildKit pip cache-mount; parameterizable `ARG REQUIREMENTS` (governance default, or the full-ML `requirements.txt` for vision/voice). Built live at **72 MB in 11 s**. (2) The `image_provisioner` is now image-agnostic (hashes the CONFIGURED requirements + Dockerfile ‚Üí rebuilds only on dep change; live-proven pre-warm of the production image). (3) `launch_shadow_soak.sh` ‚Äî the final executable: boots Docker, builds-only-if-stale the layer-cached image, ignites the cognitive bus + substrates, and launches O+V into fail-closed Shadow Mode. **MARQUEE (live): the REAL O+V governance suite (`test_slice101_phase5_proof_gate`, 12 tests, importing the full governance chain) PASSED inside the hardened `--network none` / `--read-only` / `--cap-drop ALL` production container against the read-only worktree mount ‚Äî no rebuild.** 5 tests (mock + live). **Both Tier-D hurdles are now mechanism-complete, FSM-integrated, AND the true suite runs contained in a layer-cached production image. The remaining path is purely the 12‚Äì18-month shadow wall-clock evidence ‚Äî a clock, not code.**

**¬ß51.11.9 ‚Äî Slice 109 (God-Tier Observability Matrix & Voice Ignition, 2026-06-06): the ¬ß7 "absolute observability" surface for the cognitive organism.** With the nervous system wired (Slice 101), the actuator proven + shadowed (102/103), and the runtime cage live + contained (104‚Üí108), the last gap before the soak was *legibility*: the cognitive bus reasoned in private. Slice 109 adds a **decoupled telemetry router** (`cognitive_observability.py`) that subscribes to the Slice-101 lifecycle stream and fans every event to two authority-free sinks ‚Äî composing the existing cognitive bus + SSE broker + DaemonNarrator, no new broker. (1) **The structured "Why-Snapshot"** (schema `cognitive_why_snapshot.v1`, new SSE event `EVENT_TYPE_COGNITIVE_WHY_SNAPSHOT`): on every `post_apply` / `post_failure` the FSM publish is enriched with the decision context (confidence + risk tier), and the router composes the live `confidence_aura` band, the `shannon_entropy` of the domain distribution, the `decision_prior_distribution` (belief-verdict histogram), `recursion_depth`, and any `rehearsal_verdict` into ONE JSON object the TUI / VS Code extension renders as time-travel debugging ("why did O+V believe what it believed, at the moment it decided?"). A bounded in-process ledger (`why_snapshot_for_op` / `recent_why_snapshots`) holds the recent snapshots for the dashboard. (2) **Karen's voice, ignited + wired to the bus.** A second subscriber narrates HIGH-severity cognitive events only (containment breach / graduation-threshold-met / load-shedding / failure) through the existing `DaemonNarrator` (new cognitive templates), dynamically formatted from the lifecycle payload. The gate ordering is **load-bearing and fail-closed**: master flag (`JARVIS_KAREN_VOICE_ENABLED`, ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE) ‚Üí live mute state (read from `KarenConfig`; **if the config can't be read, Karen stays silent**) ‚Üí severity. No TTS is ever queued unless all three pass; `say "Karen mute"` silences her live. macOS `say` needs an audio device, not a TTY, so the voice works in the headless soak; the *visual* TUI needs a TTY (`SHADOW_INTERACTIVE=1` drops `--headless` for an operator ignition session). Registered at GLS boot alongside the Slice-101 subscribers; both inert when the cognitive bus is off. **27 deterministic mock tests** (`tests/architecture/test_slice109_observability.py`) intercept the SSE publish + inject a recording narrator to prove the JSON shape and the gate logic with zero TTY/audio dependency. `launch_shadow_soak.sh` now exports the full observability + voice env. HONEST boundary: the visual TUI render + Karen's audible narration are **local operator verifications on a TTY + audio device** ‚Äî this slice wires + proves the data-flow and gating; the human runs the launch and confirms the senses. **The organism is now fully legible: every autonomous decision broadcasts its reasoning over SSE and speaks its high-severity moments aloud. The soak is launch-ready.**

**¬ß51.11.10 ‚Äî Slice 110 (Native React/FastAPI Command Center, 2026-06-06): the sovereign interface ‚Äî composed, not duplicated.** A Tier-D autonomous developer deserves a native real-time command center rather than terminal logs + an external IDE extension. **Verify-first found that three of the four literal build-steps would have duplicated existing infrastructure**: `backend/main.py` is already a `FastAPI()` app; `backend/api/broadcast_router.py` is already a typed FastAPI **WebSocket manager**; and `frontend/` is already a **React 19** CRA app (framer-motion + lucide-react + a zero-hardcode `config.js` + a full WS-client layer). The ONE genuinely-missing nerve ‚Äî nothing bridged the internal `TrinityEventBus` / Slice-109 Why-Snapshots to the frontend ‚Äî is the additive core. Delivered: (1) `backend/api/observability_gateway.py` ‚Äî a **composing gateway** (FastAPI `APIRouter` mounted into the existing app, reusing the existing `BroadcastConnectionManager` class on a dedicated channel) exposing typed `gateway_frame.v1` frames over WebSocket (`/api/observability/ws`) + REST (`/health`, `/why-snapshots`, `/causality`). Its **bus‚ÜíWS bridge** reuses the Slice-101 subscriber registry + Slice-109 Why-Snapshot builder (no recomputation, no duplicate schema), registered at GLS boot. (2) A React **Sovereign Command Center** (`frontend/src/components/CommandCenter/`) ‚Äî a dependency-free SVG **force-directed Causality DAG** (ops + files; decision-flow + touches edges; no d3/new npm dep ‚Üí clean lockfile), real-time **entropy + confidence-aura gauges** (framer-motion), a **live frame stream**, and a **cosmetic voice toggle** ‚Äî mounted at `/command-center` via a minimal path switch in `index.js`. (3) `launch_shadow_soak.sh` gains a `COMMAND_CENTER=1` mode that boots Docker + the engine-hosting FastAPI backend + the React UI on `:3000` (alternative front-ends to the same engine ‚Äî never double-booted). **Authority invariant (¬ß1 sovereignty, fail-closed):** the gateway is **read-only over the web for everything authority-bearing**; the ONLY write surface is the *cosmetic* Karen mute/unmute, routed through the sanctioned `karen_voice_command_router` env seam and **loopback-only** ‚Äî FSM state, governance flags, and graduation remain observable-but-not-mutable here (a browser endpoint that flipped authority would be a sovereignty leak past the Iron Gate). **18 deterministic integration tests** (`tests/architecture/test_slice110_command_center.py`) include the marquee state-synchronization assertion: a simulated FSM `ContainmentBreach` propagates through the bridge and arrives on a connected WS client as a high-severity `containment_breach` frame with the reverse-engineered breach vector ‚Äî proven with FastAPI's `TestClient`, zero browser. HONEST boundary: the visual render at `localhost:3000`, the force-graph animation, and the "elite" aesthetic are **local operator verifications**; this slice wires + proves the data path that feeds them. **The nervous system now terminates in a native sovereign interface.**

### ¬ß51.11.A ‚Äî The Tier-D Performance & GIL-Bypass Arc (Slices 111‚Äì114, 2026-06-06)

*A four-slice arc that turned the command center from a feature into a **structurally invincible** observability surface, by eliminating the single-event-loop coupling that let any GIL-heavy engine operation freeze the UI. Every slice in this arc had at least one literal premise overturned by verify-first against the live code ‚Äî and the right thing was built instead of the requested thing. The arc closes with an **empirically-probed** result, not a claim.*

**¬ß51.11.11 ‚Äî Slice 111 (Core Optimization & Tech-Debt Eradication).** Two premises overturned. (1) **FlagRegistry debt was not "~10 callers" ‚Äî it was 1000+ `register(name=, type_=, ‚Ä¶)` call sites across 150+ modules** that the newer `register(FlagSpec)` signature silently broke (30 boot tracebacks were the visible subset). Hand-migrating was infeasible + anti-DRY; the root-cause fix is a **single backward-compatible adapter** at the one API boundary: `FlagRegistry.register()` now accepts the legacy kwargs and normalizes them into a `FlagSpec` via `_spec_from_legacy` (string‚Üíenum mapping for `type_`/`category`; bare posture strings dropped; unknown values degrade to safe defaults; never raises). One change fixed all callers + **eradicated every boot traceback (verified live: 30 ‚Üí 0)**; canonical `register(FlagSpec)` unchanged. (2) **The Oracle 158 s freeze was NOT gather-able** ‚Äî `_load_cache` is a single ~1.1 GB `pickle.loads`. The deferred-init path already existed but was *defeated* by the synchronous, **GIL-bound** deserialize blocking the loop. Slice 111 tried the operator-suggested `asyncio.to_thread` lift ‚Äî and **empirically falsified it** (see ¬ß51.11.12). Also: infinite-soak config (`SHADOW_MAX_WALL_SECONDS=0` for the 12‚Äì18 mo deployment). 13 tests + 64 FlagRegistry regression green.

**¬ß51.11.12 ‚Äî Slice 112 (Process-Isolated Oracle ‚Äî the GIL bypass).** **The honest negative result that defined the arc:** Slice 111's `to_thread` cache-load was *measured* in a live soak and **did nothing** ‚Äî the boot log showed **55 lines, then 165 s of total silence, then resume**: the worker thread held the GIL for the entire 166 s `pickle.loads`, starving the asyncio loop *identically to an inline load*. `to_thread` cannot unblock a GIL-bound deserialize. This was reported as a failure, not papered over ‚Äî and it precisely diagnosed the only real fix: **a separate process = a separate GIL.** Delivered `oracle_ipc.py`: the Oracle runs in its OWN OS process (own GIL) so the 1.1 GB load + the in-RAM `networkx` graph (which `shortest_path`/`simple_cycles` *require* ‚Äî the reason a lazy-sqlite backend was rejected as mathematically impossible) never touch the engine loop. `AsyncOracleProxy` covers the audited **3-method** surface (`incremental_update` / `get_metrics` / `get_context_for_improvement`) over a spawn-context `Pipe`; resilience invariant: severed pipe ‚Üí fail in-flight calls ‚Üí narrate high-severity `OracleCrash` ‚Üí bounded-backoff **respawn** ‚Üí budget-exhaust to DEGRADED, **never crashing GLS**; structured `OracleNotReady` graceful degradation during hydration. Plus `prune_isolated_nodes()` graph hygiene (degree-0 nodes are pure serialization bloat ‚Äî traversal results provably invariant). The reverted `to_thread` was removed ("we leave no useless code behind"). 11 tests (crash‚Üírespawn, degraded-not-crash, traversal-invariance). **Verify-first note: slice-32's `ProcessPoolExecutor` is a per-file AST-parse offload, NOT whole-Oracle hosting ‚Äî so it was the wrong scaffolding to "leverage"; the proxy is new, composed from `OracleReadiness` + `daemon_narrator`.**

**¬ß51.11.13 ‚Äî Slice 113 (Unified Async Oracle Adapter ‚Äî live wiring).** A single `await`-able interface over either backend, selected by `JARVIS_ORACLE_PROCESS_ISOLATION_ENABLED`. `InProcessOracleAdapter` is a **transparent drop-in** over `TheOracle` ‚Äî `__getattr__` delegates every non-overridden method (`find_nodes_in_file`, `get_callers`, traversal) so existing consumers (e.g. `blast_radius_adapter`) keep working; only the 3 engine methods became async. `IsolatedOracleAdapter` wraps the proxy + normalizes `OracleNotReady` ‚Üí safe degraded values. Verify-first confirmed **exactly 5 live call sites** (the `" 2.py"`/`" 3.py"` matches were stale backups); the orchestrator's SYNC `_build_dependency_summary` uses the adapter's `.raw` to avoid an async cascade. **First empirical proof of the GIL-bypass (live soak):** `process_isolation=1`; the 1.1 GB graph hydrated **in the subprocess (327 s, off the critical path)** while **GLS booted in 27 s** (vs ~166 s) logging "Oracle already injected (**?** nodes)" ‚Äî the adapter's graceful degradation working. **The Oracle boot-freeze was eliminated.** 22 tests green. *Honest residual surfaced by the same soak:* the co-booted gateway still dipped (~57 s, timeouts) during **other** in-process C-ext boots (ChromaDB / `EmbeddingService` / consciousness) ‚Äî not the Oracle. That residual set up Slice 114.

**¬ß51.11.14 ‚Äî Slice 114 (Gateway Decoupling ‚Äî the invincible UI).** The co-boot topology was the root flaw: a UI sharing the engine's single event loop can never be 100 % responsive, because *any* GIL-heavy subsystem (vector loads, future ops) starves it. `telemetry_broker.py` severs the coupling: the gateway runs in its **OWN OS process** (`spawn_gateway_process`), and telemetry crosses on a bounded **`multiprocessing.Queue`** (stdlib, no external dep) ‚Äî engine-side `publish_frame` is **non-blocking + drop-oldest** (the FSM never blocks on the UI), gateway-side `drain_queue_to_manager` reads off *its* own loop (engine freezes can't reach it). **Verify-first reframe: Phase 3 (deferring ChromaDB/embedding init) was deliberately NOT built** ‚Äî once the gateway is decoupled it is immune to those freezes (they only affect the *engine's* own responsiveness, a separate concern), and they hold the GIL on C-ext init so threading wouldn't help (the ¬ß51.11.12 lesson). Building it would be redundant code chasing a solved problem. Master `JARVIS_GATEWAY_DECOUPLED_ENABLED` ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE. 7 tests (drop-oldest, drain fan-out + clean-stop, queue bridge). **THE INVINCIBILITY PROOF (live soak, `git switch main` ‚Üí Slice 114, infinite wall):** the gateway spawned in its own process; **10 consecutive `:8000` health probes returned 200 with ZERO timeouts while the engine was pegged at 42‚Äì66 % CPU** booting its heavy subsystems ‚Äî the *exact* window that timed out (000) under co-boot. **Head-to-head, the decoupled UI did not blink.** This is the arc's marquee receipt: not "merged," but *observed working* ‚Äî the empirical-receipt discipline the ¬ß51.11.12 negative result demanded, applied to the positive result too.

> **Arc honesty ledger.** The 10/10 invincibility probe is ~40 s of early boot (the prior failure window) ‚Äî strong but not the full 327 s hydration; complete confirmation is a sustained operator probe. Operational debt flagged, not hidden: an accumulated **~86 `aegis.daemon`/`async_pr_janitor` processes + 34 orphaned `multiprocessing.spawn` workers** from prior killed soaks were observed (the 34 orphans reaped during the Sovereign Ignition reset; the daemons left untouched, pending operator authorization). The repository was purged of all 7 merged feature branches (109‚Äì114 + revert), `main` re-synced local+remote, working tree switched to Slice-114 code, and the organism re-ignited on its decoupled runtime.

### ¬ß51.11.B ‚Äî The Adversarial Proving Ground & Bounded Order-2 RSI Arc (Slices 115‚Äì119, 2026-06-06)

*The arc that turned the cage from an engineering assertion into a cryptographically-receipted proof, gave the immune system the ability to synthesize its own antibodies (operator-gated), proved the egress invariant by forgery, and ‚Äî the capstone ‚Äî wired Second-Order RSI (M10, the "Neurosurgeon") into the live orchestrator while keeping the ¬ß1 Zero-Order-Doll bound absolute. This is the dissertation's empirical spine: every safety claim in it is now a re-runnable receipt, and the standing research debt from ¬ß51.1 ("Iron Gate prevents deception / Anti-Venom contains RSI are engineering assertions, not theorems") is materially paid down ‚Äî the assertions now carry deterministic, tamper-evident evidence. Verify-first overturned a literal premise in this arc too: Slice 118's "5 dormant modules to wire" were **hallucinated ‚Äî none existed**; the egress lease + recursion gate were **already live** (Aegis + Slice 104), so the slice pivoted from "build a duplicate egress daemon" to "falsify the existing one." A catastrophic blind-wire of non-existent safety code into the live FSM was prevented by the audit.*

**¬ß51.11.15 ‚Äî Slice 115 (Blue/Red Adversarial Falsification Matrix).** The proving ground. **Red** composes the existing `adversarial_sweep` (corpus √ó 8 mutation operators √ó the cage ‚Äî no duplicate FSM) + a NEW recursion-depth siege (drives `evaluate_recursion_gate` past `MAX_RECURSION_DEPTH`). **Blue** = `BlueEvidenceLedger`: an append-only, **hash-chained** `dissertation_evidence.jsonl` where each receipt carries `sha256(payload)` + verdict + a chain link (`record_hash = sha256(prev_hash ‚Äñ canonical)`); `verify_ledger` detects any post-hoc tampering. `--siege-mode` runs it in the background during a soak. **Honest framing (load-bearing):** the ledger records the TRUE block/escape rate ‚Äî it does NOT fabricate 100 % containment; known-gap escapes are logged *as escapes*. Live capstone soak result: **95.1 % variant-level block rate (39/41), recursion gate 2/2 halted, chain verified intact.** 7 tests.

**¬ß51.11.16 ‚Äî Slice 116 (Adaptive Immune Synthesis Engine ‚Äî SAFE/bounded).** The safety reframe that defines the dissertation: "autonomously LLM-synthesize a cage rule and hot-load it" is an Order-2 RSI driver ‚Äî refused. Built the bounded version: `antibody_synthesizer.py` does **deterministic** synthesis (analyze the escape's AST signature ‚Üí a pure structural matcher; never LLM ‚Äî the safety substrate must not be prompt-injectable, else "the adversary writes its own antibody"). Pipeline: **synthesize ‚Üí validate (blocks-escape + ZERO clean-control FP + monotonic-tighten) ‚Üí SHADOW proposal ‚Üí operator-promote** (`promote_antibody` is fail-closed, refuses without explicit operator approval). Hot-swappable `AntibodyRegistry` loads *approved* antibodies live (no restart), consulted ADDITIVELY on top of the built-in SemanticGuardian patterns. 9 tests incl. the closed loop (escape ‚Üí synth ‚Üí operator-promote ‚Üí hot-swap ‚Üí same payload BLOCKED ‚Üí clean controls still pass).

**¬ß51.11.17 ‚Äî Slice 117 (Synaptic Integration).** Closes the wiring gap: a verified siege ESCAPE ‚Üí the exact escaping AST source is DETERMINISTICALLY re-derived from `(seed_name, strategy)` via `build_corpus` + `generate_mutations` (the sweep records metadata only ‚Äî verify-first) ‚Üí fed to the immune synthesizer ‚Üí a SHADOW proposal. `GET /api/observability/antibody-proposals` exposes proposals + the "[IMMUNE SYSTEM ALERT] Awaiting Operator Approval" banner to the Command Center. **Capstone-run honest finding:** the live siege proposed ZERO antibodies ‚Äî *correctly*, because the 2 escapes were quine/runtime-class, not the introspection-attribute class the deterministic synthesizer can fingerprint zero-FP. It DECLINED rather than fabricate. That honesty IS the theorem.

**¬ß51.11.18 ‚Äî Slice 118 (Aegis Egress-Invariant Falsification).** Verify-first killed the original premise (5 named "dormant" modules ‚Äî `egress_broker`/`budget_authority`/`trust_tier`/`immutable_budget_state_machine`/`causal_lineage` ‚Äî **all NOT FOUND**; egress already live via Aegis; recursion gate already wired). Pivoted to *proving* the real invariant: `aegis_lease_forgery.py` fires three forgery vectors at the **real** Aegis verifier (`validate_lease_token`) ‚Äî **no-lease ‚Üí `invalid_format`, forged-HMAC ‚Üí `invalid_signature`, expired ‚Üí `expired` (ALL rejected); valid control ‚Üí `valid` (accepted ‚Äî proves the verifier rejects forgeries *specifically*, not by blanket-denying)**. Each ‚Üí a Blue receipt; `bridge_fails_closed()` pins that `acquire_call_lease` has no `except` in the lease path (no silent fallback). 9 deterministic tests. **The FSM cannot make an upstream API call without a valid Aegis lease ‚Äî now cryptographically receipted.**

**¬ß51.11.19 ‚Äî Slice 119 (Bounded M10 Integration ‚Äî the capstone).** M10 (the ArchitectureProposer / "Neurosurgeon" / Second-Order RSI) now has a **live-path reference in the orchestrator**, changing the ¬ß51-audit's "**0 orchestrator references**" finding ‚Äî but bounded absolutely by the ¬ß1 Zero-Order-Doll invariant. `m10_synapse.py` (gated `JARVIS_M10_SYNAPSE_ENABLED`, ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE): `evaluate_m10_routing` triggers on high entropy / repeated failures and can ONLY return `forced_tier = APPROVAL_REQUIRED` (no self-authorizing route exists in the code); `propose_structural_upgrade` synthesizes ‚Üí persists PENDING ‚Üí **NEVER executes** (AST-pinned: the module imports nothing from `change_engine`/the apply path). The orchestrator hook (beside the existing "observe ‚Üí APPROVAL_REQUIRED" force) routes M10 to the human gate; the Slice-104 recursion-depth gate bounds it independently. `GET /api/observability/m10-proposals` surfaces the AST diff for the operator's signature. 11 tests incl. the closed loop (high-entropy failure ‚Üí propose ‚Üí APPROVAL_REQUIRED ‚Üí diff queued, NOT executed). **The Reverse Russian Doll is structurally complete: First-Order live; Second-Order wired-but-bounded; the operator the un-bypassable signature authority.**

**¬ß51.11.20 ‚Äî Slice 120 (The Sovereign Layer-4 Roadmap Authority ‚Äî BOUNDED).** The bridge that lets the 12‚Äì18 month evidence clock (T5) run **unattended** without dissolving the Zero-Order Doll. `layer4_roadmap_authority.py` (gated `JARVIS_LAYER4_ROADMAP_ENABLED`, ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE) ingests an operator-signed `.jarvis/roadmap.signed.yaml`, **cryptographically verifies the operator's signature** (composes the *exact* Aegis HMAC-SHA256 token codec ‚Äî `_encode_token`/`_decode_token`/`TokenVerdictKind` ‚Äî no reinvented crypto), and for the **safe, explicitly-authorized scopes only** auto-resolves the approval prompt so Green ops run without a human in the loop for each one.

**The load-bearing refusal (¬ß1).** The operator's brief said "fully suppress *all* APPROVAL_REQUIRED prompts." Taken literally that *removes the recursion bound* ‚Äî a single signature (forged, or stale at month 12) could switch off the human gate on the very operations the cage exists to protect. We **refused the "all"** and built the bounded form: an **un-signable floor** that `may_suppress_approval` checks *before* the signature or scope is even read. **No signature can ever suppress approval for** SAFETY/BLOCKED-tier ops, Order-2/M10 cognitive self-modification, any op whose self-mod chain would exceed the Slice-104 recursion bound, or any governance/cage touch ‚Äî those **always** escalate to a live operator decision. This is *delegated autonomy within a signed boundary*, never the removal of the boundary.

**Fail-closed + bounded budget.** Every defect ‚Äî missing operator key, missing/garbled signature, bad HMAC, body-hash mismatch (tamper), expiry ‚Äî returns a non-VALID authorization that collapses the system back to **per-PR human review** (the legacy safe default); the loader never raises. Budget/recursion bounds **only tighten**: a roadmap requesting depth 99 or $10,000 is clamped to the autonomous Slice-104 cap and the hard budget ceiling (the signature cannot *buy* more recursion or spend than the safety maxima allow). The orchestrator wiring is **escalation-only** (it can only re-assert `APPROVAL_REQUIRED`, never relax a tier ‚Äî the auto-resolution UX lives in the harness), default-off ‚Üí byte-identical, try/except-isolated so a malformed roadmap can never break the loop. `--layer4-autonomous` arms it on the soak. **21 tests** prove the fail-closed crypto (forged/tampered/expired/keyless ‚Üí no autonomy), the un-signable floor (a VALID roadmap still cannot auto-approve an Order-2/M10 op, a recursion breach, a governance touch, or an APPROVAL_REQUIRED tier), and the budget clamps. **This is the T4 mechanism ‚Äî built and proven; the operator's standing signature is the one thing that remains, by design.**

**¬ß51.11.21 ‚Äî Slice 121 (The Adversarial Volume & Concurrency-Hardening Matrix).** Authorized as "The Hyper-Accelerated Temporal Simulation Matrix"; **renamed for honesty after a verify-first reframe.** The directive was to "compress 12‚Äì18 months of wall-clock evidence into hours" by running 50+ concurrent loops. **The load-bearing correction: parallelism is not time-compression.** The T5 clock is a *duration / endurance* property (the cage holding across calendar time ‚Äî dependency drift, slow state accumulation, naturally-arising operations); 50 instances √ó 1 hour is *throughput*, not 50 hours of soak, and nowhere near months of calendar exposure (the "9 women can't make a baby in 1 month" error). Stamping "‚âàN months simulated" into the dissertation ledger would be a **false attestation a committee would catch instantly**, discrediting the entire evidence corpus ‚Äî so we refused it. The harness instead produces two *genuinely defensible* statistics that **complement, never substitute for**, the calendar soak: **adversarial volume/breadth** (escape rate over a large mutated sample) and **concurrency correctness** (the chain holds under load). `temporal_matrix.py` (gated `JARVIS_TEMPORAL_MATRIX_ENABLED`, ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE) composes Slice 115 (`BlueEvidenceLedger`/`verify_ledger`) + Slice 84 (`run_sweep`: corpus √ó mutation operators √ó the live cage) ‚Äî no reinvented ledger/crypto/corpus.

Two further design corrections, also confident: **(1) read-only siege, not 50 live self-modifying organisms** ‚Äî the matrix fans out the *cage evaluation* off-hot-path (the Slice 115 pattern), never 50 unsupervised `GovernedLoopService` mutators (50√ó blast radius + budget burn + git/worktree chaos for zero added evidence). **(2) A hash chain is irreducibly sequential** ‚Äî a truly "lock-free append" to one linear chain is mathematically impossible (`record_hash = sha256(prev_hash ‚Äñ record)` depends on its predecessor). The correct pattern: **parallelize the expensive, pure work (cage evaluation), serialize only the cheap chain-link.** `drive_concurrent_siege` runs N producer threads through ONE `threading.Lock`-guarded `ThreadSafeLedger` ‚Üí one provably-unbroken chain; the production CLI parallelizes evaluation and lets the single main process link. The `MatrixReport` carries `evidence_kind="adversarial_volume_concurrency"` + a `disclaimer` and emits NO calendar-equivalence field. **7 tests** prove the marquee (1600 receipts across 16 concurrent threads ‚Üí `verify_ledger` True, sequence numbers contiguous with no lost/duplicated writes) + the honesty invariant (schema forbids any "months/years simulated" key). Live-cage smoke: a real concurrent siege ran **570 attacks / 550 blocked / 3.51 % escape / chain intact** (matching the documented cage residual). `./scripts/launch_simulation_matrix.sh --concurrency 50` ignites it behind a prominent honesty banner. Slice 115 regression clean (8/8). **The bottleneck remains the T5 calendar clock ‚Äî this slice strengthens the *robustness* axis, not the *duration* axis, and says so on every receipt.**

**¬ß51.11.22 ‚Äî Slice 122 (The Sovereign Cryptographic Key Manager & Dynamic Synthesis).** The Layer-4 ignition infrastructure: provisions the operator's signing authority with **zero hardcoding** ‚Äî and, in doing so, fixes a genuine cryptographic weakness in Slice 120. **The root-cause upgrade: Slice 120 verified roadmaps with symmetric HMAC, but with HMAC whoever can *verify* can also *forge* ‚Äî if the loop holds the key to check a roadmap, it can mint its own and self-authorize.** Slice 122 moves the Layer-4 signature to **asymmetric Ed25519** (`sovereign_keys.py`, gated `JARVIS_SOVEREIGN_KEYS_ENABLED`, ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE): the operator holds the **private** key, the loop is given **only the public key** (`.jarvis/layer4_operator.pub`). The loop can verify but **cannot forge ‚Äî a mathematical guarantee, not a policy.** `verify_signed_roadmap` gained an additive ed25519 branch (routed before the HMAC fallback; the symmetric path stays byte-identical for backward-compat).

**Zero hardcoding, real air-gap.** The Ed25519 private key is **`scrypt(passphrase, salt)`** (stdlib `hashlib.scrypt`, no third-party KDF) ‚Äî derived live from a passphrase the operator types, **never written to disk**. Only the salt + public key + a signature-verifier are persisted; the private key and passphrase never are (proven: a test asserts the passphrase bytes never appear in any on-disk file). The loop, run headless, has no passphrase ‚Üí cannot derive the private key ‚Üí cannot sign. **Single-user honesty:** on a single-user OS there is no kernel separation between "the AI" and "the operator" (same user), so the air-gap is *cryptographic + procedural* (the private key exists only transiently in an interactive operator session), backstopped by the un-signable floor (¬ß1) which holds even if every key leaked.

**Dynamic synthesis (Phase 2):** `roadmap_synthesizer.py` audits recent commit scopes + tier state ‚Üí drafts an **unsigned, authority-free** `.jarvis/roadmap.draft.yaml` with a conservative SAFE scope allow-list (docs/tests/observability ‚Äî never governance/cage/Order-2, which are un-signable anyway), recursion clamped to the Slice-104 cap, a bounded ~12-month expiry. The draft grants nothing until signed.

**Phase 3 reframe ‚Äî the refusal grounded in the codebase's own invariant.** The brief asked for a "one-click sign" button in the React Command Center on the FastAPI gateway. But `observability_gateway.py` *itself* declares (line 23): *"read-only over the web‚Ä¶ a browser endpoint that flipped authority would be a sovereignty leak past the Zero-Order Doll."* A web sign-button is **exactly** that leak ‚Äî a POST that mints unattended authority, reachable by any same-machine process (the loop included). So we exposed the **draft read-only** (`GET /api/observability/layer4-roadmap-draft` ‚Äî draft + signing status, `signing_surface="local_cli_only"`, `armed=false`) and kept **signing as a deliberate local operator CLI act** (`python3 -m ‚Ä¶sovereign_keys sign`, passphrase-gated). This is safer *and* consistent with the existing architecture; the full React tab is deferred (no React app exists in-repo, and a clickable web-sign is the wrong security boundary regardless). **10 tests** prove the full operator round-trip (provision ‚Üí synthesize ‚Üí sign ‚Üí the loop verifies VALID with the public key only, and the un-signable floor still rejects an Order-2 op on that valid roadmap), the air-gap (a roadmap signed by a foreign key is rejected `INVALID_SIGNATURE`; wrong passphrase cannot load the private key; the passphrase is never on disk), and fail-closed (tampered body / no pubkey / expired ‚Üí not VALID). Slice 120 regression clean (21/21). `cryptography` added to requirements. **T4 is now armable end-to-end: the operator provisions a key, reviews the draft, signs locally ‚Äî and only then can the unattended T5 clock begin.**

**¬ß51.11.23 ‚Äî Slice 123 (Production Isolation & Hygiene Matrix).** Born from *live telemetry*: the first armed T5 launch verified Layer-4 flawlessly (`valid/ok(ed25519)`, floor holding) but exposed two production-readiness gaps ‚Äî (a) the boot replayed **81 provenance-less recovery ops** from prior crashed sessions, all escalating to `manual_intervention_required` and starving fresh Layer-4-eligible work; (b) a **~2.5-min in-process Oracle load freeze** (`process_isolation=false`). Slice 123 closes both **architecturally, composing existing mechanism**.

**Phase 1 ‚Äî Provenance Quarantine** (`boot_recovery_quarantine.py`, gated `JARVIS_BOOT_RECOVERY_QUARANTINE_ENABLED`, ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE): when a recovery op trips `boot_recovery_missing_provenance`, its raw payload is sequestered to `.jarvis/quarantine/{ts}_{op}.json` and marked `quarantined`, so it stops re-clogging the intake on every restart. **Additive** ‚Äî the existing escalation/postmortem is untouched (auditability preserved); **best-effort** ‚Äî wrapped so a quarantine write failure never breaks recovery (proven: unwritable dir ‚Üí returns None, recovery continues); path-traversal-safe filenames; non-JSONable payloads degrade to repr. 7 tests.
