**Phase 2 ‚Äî Process-Isolated Oracle: VERIFY-FIRST found it ALREADY BUILT.** The directive said "rewrite the init sequence to spawn the graph load in a decoupled process" ‚Äî but Slices 112/113 already shipped exactly that: `oracle_ipc.AsyncOracleProxy` (separate-GIL worker process) + `oracle_adapter.make_oracle_adapter()` which returns the `IsolatedOracleAdapter` when `JARVIS_ORACLE_PROCESS_ISOLATION_ENABLED` is set, and the harness already calls it. The boot froze only because that flag was unset. **So Slice 123 ENABLES the existing mechanism (no rewrite ‚Äî that would be the duplication the mandate forbids)**; the 18 Slice-112/113 isolation tests confirm it's sound.

**Phase 3 ‚Äî `--production-soak` profile** (in the harness arg layer, not buried in the script): one flag overrides the battle-test defaults to a real T5 run ‚Äî `cost-cap=25.00`, `idle-timeout=0`, `max-wall-seconds=0` ‚Äî and `setdefault`s the isolation + quarantine env flags. **Explicit `--cost-cap`/`--idle-timeout`/`--max-wall-seconds` still win** (detected via `sys.argv`, not the parsed value). Plus an operator-quality-of-life fix: `launch_shadow_soak.sh` now lets every Karen-voice flag respect an existing env value, so a run can be silenced with `JARVIS_KAREN_VOICE_ENABLED=0 ./scripts/launch_shadow_soak.sh ‚Ä¶` (no code edit).

**Phase 4 ‚Äî clean ignition (operator command):** `JARVIS_LAYER4_ROADMAP_ENABLED=1 JARVIS_SOVEREIGN_KEYS_ENABLED=1 JARVIS_KAREN_VOICE_ENABLED=0 ./scripts/launch_shadow_soak.sh --production-soak --layer4-autonomous`. 25 tests green (quarantine 7 + composed isolation 18); zero edits to the isolation mechanism itself. **The clock can now run clean: no cruft-clog, no GIL freeze, on the operator-signed roadmap.**

**¬ß51.11.24 ‚Äî Slice 124 (Autonomous Economic Failover Router).** The first clean production soak (Slice 123) ran for hours and surfaced the *real* blocker, by telemetry: **DoubleWord returned `http_402 "Account balance too low"`** ‚Äî out of credits. BACKGROUND-routed OpportunityMiner ops (read-only scans) couldn't generate (BACKGROUND has no Claude fallback by policy), so $0 spent, no candidate, the Layer-4 gate never reached. Slice 124 makes provider economics self-healing.

**Phases 1+2 ‚Äî `economic_router.py`** (gated `JARVIS_ECONOMIC_ROUTER_ENABLED`, ¬ß33.1 default-FALSE): a pure, bounded decision. On a HARD economic block (402 balance / 429 rate-limit), a **micro-op** (context under `JARVIS_ECONOMIC_MICRO_OP_TOKENS`, default 1500) cascades to the **cheapest Claude tier** to preserve momentum; a **massive-context op stays QUEUED** until the cheap provider returns. **Mutation safety preserved**: a mutating micro-op still needs the existing `JARVIS_BACKGROUND_ALLOW_FALLBACK` opt-in (only read-only micro-ops cascade autonomously). **No hardcoded model** (CLAUDE.md mandate): the cheap tier resolves from `JARVIS_ECONOMIC_FAILOVER_MODEL` (policy yaml has no cheap tier); empty ‚Üí default fallback. Verify-first found the BACKGROUND‚ÜíClaude cascade *already exists* (Defect #5 read-only reflex) ‚Äî Slice 124 EXTENDS it with the economic size-gate + cheap-model selection, wired as a fail-open consult in the candidate_generator queue branch (default-off ‚Üí byte-identical). 14 tests. **Honest scope:** the dedicated cheap-provider *instance* swap (vs. the default Sonnet fallback) is a documented follow-up ‚Äî threading a per-call model override through the 100-line `_call_fallback` was judged too invasive to rush; the decision + size-gate + model-resolution + the unblock ship now.

**Phase 3 ‚Äî starvation fix.** The confirmed `ControlPlaneStarvation lag_ms~3200` traced to `opportunity_miner_sensor.scan_once`: its `_is_production_code ‚Üí continue` skip-path awaits nothing, spinning the loop between coarse `cooperative_yield_every_n` slots. A guaranteed `await asyncio.sleep(0)` per file iteration caps FSM lag (composes the existing Slice-12L offload hardening). **Phase 4** relaunch enables the router + a cheap failover model in env. The clock can now generate Layer-4 evidence **regardless of DW credit state**.

**¬ß51.11.25 ‚Äî Slice 125 (Aegis Credential Bootstrap + Health Probe ‚Äî the real root cause).** Verify-first OVERTURNED my own Slice-124 premise. The operator asserted the DW key was funded; direct probes confirmed it: the `.env` key returned **HTTP 200 on every model** (incl. the exact `Qwen3.5-35B`/`397B`/`DeepSeek-V4-Flash` the soak `402`'d on). So the soak's `402 "balance too low"` was **never out-of-credits** ‚Äî it was an **Aegis credential-injection gap**: the funded `.env` key wasn't in the process env when `aegis_preflight` snapshotted credentials for the daemon, so the daemon injected an empty token and DW billed the free ($0) tier. Slice 124's economic router was treating a *symptom*; this is the *cause*.

**Root invariant enforced:** `credential_env_loader.py` (stdlib-only, allowlist-only) loads the funded provider keys from `.env` into the env **immediately before the Aegis snapshot** (wired as step 0 of `aegis_preflight`, before the `creds = {...}` capture). Guarantees: **no `source .env`** (line-by-line `KEY=VALUE` parse ‚Äî a hostile `.env` can't exec shell); **explicit exports win** (only fills empty names); **no secret ever logged** (redacted `sha256(value)[:8]` fingerprints only). A bash-native twin in `launch_shadow_soak.sh` (step 0) does the same before the first python subprocess. **Verified live:** the loader populates the real funded key (`sha256:6c4bf566`, len 46) into a fresh pre-snapshot env.

**Fail-loud health probe:** `credential_probe.py` runs two arms at boot (direct funded key vs Aegis-routed) and classifies ‚Äî `direct 200 + aegis 402/401 ‚Üí AEGIS_CREDENTIAL_INJECTION_FAILED` (NOT `provider_out_of_credits`); `direct 401/402 ‚Üí OPERATOR_CREDENTIAL_PROBLEM`; network ‚Üí `TRANSPORT_PROBLEM`. A fatal verdict **halts the boot** (`sys.exit(2)`) so a broken credential never silently `402`s for hours and burns Claude. Gated `JARVIS_AEGIS_CREDENTIAL_PROBE_ENABLED`; redacted logging. Composes the existing `aegis_provider_bridge` (`dw_aegis_base_url` / `dw_session_auth_header`) ‚Äî no duplicate credential registry, no hardcoded keys. **13 tests** (`.env`-fills-absent-key, explicit-export-wins, malformed/missing-`.env`-clean-no-leak, 402‚Üíinjection-failure-not-out-of-credits, redaction). This closes the blocker that defeated four soaks.

**¬ß51.11.26 ‚Äî Slice 126.5 (Cryptographic State Telemetry ‚Äî the ghost exposed & killed).** The blocker that defeated FIVE soaks, finally resolved by making the runtime credential observable. After exhaustively proving the Aegis injection code was *statically correct* (loader‚Üísnapshot‚Üíspawn‚Üíinject all sound; funded key flows through every step), the only remaining explanation was a runtime value invisible to static analysis. Slice 126.5 added **permanent redacted fingerprint telemetry** (`sha256[:8]`, never the raw key) at the two exact points: the daemon's **env-load** (`daemon.py build_app`) and the **injection microsecond** (`forwarding.py`). One live run exposed the ghost: daemon env-load `DOUBLEWORD_API_KEY = sha256:f4354214`, injection = same `f4354214` ‚Äî **no load‚Üíinject mutation** (injection always faithful), but **the daemon loaded the WRONG key** (the funded key is `sha256:6c4bf566`). Root cause: a **stale, unfunded `DOUBLEWORD_API_KEY` in `backend/.env`** (`f4354214`) auto-loaded ahead of the funded repo-root `.env` (`6c4bf566`) and preserved by Slice-125's explicit-env-wins. **Surgical eradication:** neutralized the stale `backend/.env` line (gitignored/local; value never read or printed). **Permanent guard:** the credential loader now emits a loud redacted CONFLICT warning whenever an existing env credential differs from the operator's `.env` ‚Äî so a sibling-`.env` shadow can never hide again. **Kill-shot verified live:** post-fix the daemon env-load + injection both show the funded `sha256:6c4bf566`, **402-count = 0**, and a **DoubleWord completion succeeded**. The DW pipeline is online. 15 tests. (Telemetry was the only way to repair a statically-correct bridge ‚Äî verify-first illumination, not a guessed mutation.)

### ¬ß51.11.D ‚Äî Slice 127 BLUEPRINT (DEFERRED ‚Äî Multi-Lane Self-Healing Circuit Breaker)

**Status: NOT YET BUILT.** Architecture locked here for the next session (deferred off a depleted context budget ‚Äî a sloppy rewrite of the generation gatekeeper is unacceptable). This is a specification, not a changelog entry.

**Problem (verify-first localized, 2026-06-06):** A single `401` on the **`/v1/models` health-probe** (an Aegis *passthrough*, not a generation call) is classified `TERMINAL_CONFIG` (`provider_retry_classifier.py:169` ‚Äî `_HTTP_STATUS_TERMINAL_CONFIG = {401,403,404}`) and trips the **GLOBAL** breaker to sticky `OPEN_TERMINAL` (`circuit_breaker.py:54-55`). Once global-terminal, **every** subsequent op short-circuits in ~9ms (`all_providers_exhausted:circuit_breaker_tripped:terminal_config`), bricking the entire organism ‚Äî including the now-working DoubleWord chat-completions lane. Observed live in session `bt-2026-06-07-040933`: `401-count=1` ‚Üí `terminal_config trips=16`, zero DW completions, requiring a manual restart. A Tier-D organism must self-heal, not brick.

**What ALREADY exists (compose, do NOT rebuild):** `circuit_breaker.py` already implements the full self-healing FSM ‚Äî `CircuitState = {CLOSED, OPEN_TRANSIENT (backoff-then-probe), HALF_OPEN (probing recovery), OPEN_TERMINAL (sticky)}`, full-jitter exponential backoff, and `record_success() in HALF_OPEN ‚Üí CLOSED`. The recovery machinery is built; it just isn't reachable for these failures because (a) the scope is global and (b) terminal is sticky.

**Core directives (the three changes):**
1. **`CircuitScope` ‚Üí `PER_PROVIDER`.** Today `CircuitScope = {PER_OP, GLOBAL}` (`circuit_breaker.py:175`). Add `PER_PROVIDER` and key the breaker registry by provider lane (`doubleword` / `anthropic` / `prime`). A trip in the Anthropic lane MUST NOT touch the DoubleWord lane. Keep `GLOBAL` only for genuinely cross-cutting structural failures (e.g. `TERMINAL_STRUCTURAL` from a sovereignty breach), never for a single provider's config/quota hit.
2. **Quarantine health-probe failures from the generation breaker.** A `401`/`403`/`404` on a non-generation **passthrough** endpoint (`/v1/models`, `/v1/files`, `/v1/batches*` ‚Äî `EndpointKind.PASSTHROUGH`, daemon.py:202) MUST be mathematically prevented from escalating the **generation** (`EndpointKind.LLM_COMPLETION`) breaker to `OPEN_TERMINAL`. Health-probe auth failures are informational, not generation-fatal. Tag probe-origin failures (extend `CircuitTripOrigin`) and exclude them from the LLM_COMPLETION lane's terminal accounting.
3. **Wire the existing `OPEN_TRANSIENT ‚Üí HALF_OPEN ‚Üí CLOSED` backoff into the per-lane routing matrix.** When a provider lane trips, pause ONLY that lane for the calculated full-jitter backoff, then auto-probe (canary) and reset to `CLOSED` on success ‚Äî no process restart. Transient signatures (0-token empty response, 5xx) must route to `OPEN_TRANSIENT` (recoverable), never `OPEN_TERMINAL`. Add an explicit `EMPTY_RESPONSE` ‚Üí `RETRY_TRANSIENT` mapping in `provider_retry_classifier.py` `_FAILURE_MODE_DEFAULT` (alongside `SERVER_ERROR`/`TIMEOUT`) AND a producer-side `ProviderEmptyResponse` high-severity (non-fatal) signal where the 0-token/`first_token_ms=-1` response is detected (providers.py / candidate_generator.py generation path).

**Exact files + anchors:**
- `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/circuit_breaker.py` ‚Äî `CircuitScope` (L175), `CircuitState` FSM (L165-172), terminal-trip table (L54-59), `record_failure`/`decision` consumer (L799-818). The breaker registry/lookup is where `PER_PROVIDER` keying goes.
- `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/provider_retry_classifier.py` ‚Äî `_HTTP_STATUS_TERMINAL_CONFIG` (L169), `_FAILURE_MODE_DEFAULT` (L203), `classify()` (L219). Add `EMPTY_RESPONSE`‚Üí`RETRY_TRANSIENT`; add a probe-origin guard so health-probe 401s don't reach the generation lane as terminal.
- `backend/core/ouroboros/aegis/daemon.py` ‚Äî `_make_passthrough_handler` (L228) vs `_make_llm_handler` (L190). Passthrough = health-probe class; tag its failures distinctly.
- Producer of the 0-token signal: the generation path emitting `provider= tokens=0 first_token_ms=-1 total_ms=9` (StreamRenderer/candidate_generator) ‚Äî wire the `ProviderEmptyResponse` interceptor there.

**Invariants (non-negotiable):** PER_PROVIDER isolation is structural (Anthropic trip ‚áè DoubleWord trip); health-probe failures never brick generation; transient failures always recover via the existing HALF_OPEN path; `TERMINAL_STRUCTURAL` (sovereignty/¬ß1) stays sticky-global by design. Tests-first: a per-lane isolation test (trip Anthropic, assert DW still CLOSED), a health-probe-quarantine test (401 on /v1/models ‚áè generation OPEN_TERMINAL), a recovery test (OPEN_TRANSIENT ‚Üí backoff ‚Üí HALF_OPEN canary ‚Üí CLOSED with no restart), and the breaker regression suite must stay green.

**Verification gate:** after the fix, a fresh production soak must survive an injected health-probe 401 AND reach a real `LAYER4:` runtime line (the milestone unachieved across 6 soaks ‚Äî now blocked only by this breaker + the natural arrival of a sensor-driven op). The DW credential path is already proven (Slice 126.5: 402=0, completion succeeded).

### ¬ß51.11.27 ‚Äî Slice 127 BUILT (Economic Sovereign Matrix) ‚Äî the verify-first correction of ¬ß51.11.D

**Status: P1 + P2 + P3 + P2.1 BUILT, tested, committed. The first bounded ignition soak (bt-2026-06-07) PROVED P1+P2 live (0 `terminal_config` bricks; Claude credit-400 ‚Üí recoverable `terminal_quota`; `ECONOMIC TRIP` isolated the Claude lane + self-heals) and surfaced P2.1. Only P4 (a re-run soak on a lane that actually completes) remains.**

**P2.1 ‚Äî fallback-skip gate (IMMEDIATE ‚Üí DW failover).** The live soak exposed a routing blind spot: `_generate_immediate` does "Claude direct, skip DW", and the existing `should_allow_request` gate only covers Claude-as-*primary* ‚Äî so IMMEDIATE ops kept grinding against the depleted Claude lane (`terminal_quota` √óN, no completion) instead of failing over to the funded DW lane. Fix: the Claude-direct path now consults the Claude lane breaker via a pure decision helper (`immediate_reroute_to_dw`); when the breaker is OPEN (economic/transport) it reroutes the op to the DW primary (`_call_primary`), while a HALF_OPEN probe still keeps Claude-direct so the lane self-heals. Master `JARVIS_FALLBACK_SKIP_GATE_ENABLED` (default-FALSE ¬ß33.1). 8 tests + wiring pin; 170 green.

**The blueprint was misdiagnosed (verify-first, ground truth from `bt-2026-06-07-040933` `debug.log`).** ¬ß51.11.D claimed a `/v1/models` **401** health-probe trips a **GLOBAL** breaker sticky `OPEN_TERMINAL`, bricking all lanes. The canonical log (line 1592) proves otherwise:

```
fallback_err_class=BadRequestError
fallback_err_msg=Error code: 400 - 'Your credit balance is too low to access the Anthropic API...'
slice7e_decision=terminal_config
```

- The 16 trips were caused by **Claude HTTP 400 "credit balance too low"** ‚Äî class `BadRequestError`, which is in `_TERMINAL_CONFIG_CLASSES` ‚Üí `TERMINAL_CONFIG` ‚Üí the **per-op** Slice-7 breaker sticky `OPEN_TERMINAL` on the 1st hit (`escalated_to_global=False`, 16 separate ops). **Not** a global breaker, **not** the 401.
- The `/v1/models` **401** is a benign `SemanticTriage` warning (line 46) that never reaches the generation breaker. ¬ß51.11.D directive #2 (quarantine the 401) addressed a **non-problem** and was **descoped**.
- Root cause: a "credit balance too low" 400 is an **economic** refusal (recoverable once funded ‚Äî the Anthropic twin of the DW 402 that Slice 124 already handles), misclassified as a **permanent config** fault.

**What was built (compose-first ‚Äî operator's leverage-existing/avoid-duplication mandate):**

- **P1 ‚Äî Economic reclassification (the real root fix).** `provider_retry_classifier.classify()` gains `failure_message` + `economic_reclassify` params; when an economic block is recognised (composing the canonical `economic_router.is_hard_economic_block`), it returns the recoverable `TERMINAL_QUOTA` instead of sticky `TERMINAL_CONFIG` ‚Äî **no new RetryDecision member** (closed 4-value AST pin intact). The classifier stays **PURE-DATA** (AST-pinned: no `os.environ`); the env gate `JARVIS_ECONOMIC_RECLASSIFY_ENABLED` (default-FALSE ¬ß33.1) lives in `economic_router.economic_reclassify_enabled()`. The detector was **strengthened** to match Anthropic's actual "credit balance **is** too low" phrasing ‚Äî the original "balance too low" marker missed it, and that miss **was** the bug. Wired at `candidate_generator` breaker call site (`str(inner_exc)` + gate). End-to-end cages prove: gate-on ‚Üí recoverable `RETRY_AFTER_BACKOFF` (1st hit, breaker non-terminal); gate-off ‚Üí byte-identical legacy brick.
- **P2 ‚Äî Per-provider economic self-healing breaker.** The **existing** `ClaudeCircuitBreaker` is already a per-Anthropic-lane self-healing breaker (CLOSED/OPEN/HALF_OPEN + recovery window) ‚Äî it just never tripped on economics. Composed via `record_economic_exhaustion()` (distinct counter; trips OPEN at a low threshold; self-heals via the existing recovery-window ‚Üí HALF_OPEN ‚Üí `record_success`). Master `JARVIS_CLAUDE_ECONOMIC_BREAKER_ENABLED` (default-FALSE). Wired at the fallback site so **future** ops route around the broke Claude lane (existing `should_allow_request` gate) and the lane recovers when funded ‚Äî no session brick. **Per-provider isolation is realized by composing this existing per-lane breaker, NOT a parallel `CircuitScope.PER_PROVIDER` registry** (which would duplicate the lane-health mechanism ‚Äî the operator's explicit anti-duplication mandate, endorsed when the misdiagnosis was surfaced).

- **P3 ‚Äî DW transport dynamic exponential self-healing.** **Verify-first narrowed P3 to one change:** the DW lane recovery already self-heals (a `TRANSPORT_DEGRADED` verdict auto-probes back once stale via `dw_transport_degraded_preflight`) and `EMPTY_RESPONSE` is already recoverable (0-token ‚Üí `DoublewordInfraError(status 0)` ‚Üí `TIMEOUT`/`CONTENT_FAILURE` ‚Üí `RETRY_TRANSIENT`) ‚Äî so neither needed building. The only gap was the **hardcoded-default 120s STATIC** freshness window. New `dw_transport_recovery.py` (thread-safe singleton, **composes** `circuit_breaker.full_jitter_delay` ‚Äî no new backoff math) replaces it with a **dynamic full-jitter exponential** window keyed to consecutive rupture *episodes*: `note_degraded()` (debounced by `base` ‚Üí burst = 1 episode), `note_recovered()` (DW success ‚Üí reset to 0 instantly), `dynamic_recovery_window_s()` = `max(base, full_jitter_delay(episode-1, base, cap))` (episode 1 = `base`; episode N up to `base¬∑2^(N-1)`, capped). Master `JARVIS_DW_DYNAMIC_RECOVERY_ENABLED` (default-FALSE ¬ß33.1; knobs `JARVIS_DW_RECOVERY_BASE_S`=30, `JARVIS_DW_RECOVERY_CAP_S`=600). 3 gated, best-effort integration sites in `candidate_generator` (`_note_dw_candidate_success`‚Üíreset, `_note_dw_live_transport_degraded`‚Üíepisode, `dw_transport_degraded_preflight`‚Üídynamic window). 12 module tests + wiring pins; 137 green across the suite. **No byte-stream resume is claimed** ‚Äî LLM completion streams cannot be byte-resumed mid-chunk; "recovery" is lane-level probe timing only (claiming otherwise would be a false attestation).

**Honest remaining scope.** P4 (the ignition soak ‚Äî needs an operator-funded lane + ~40 min wall-clock to reach `LAYER4:`) is the only remaining piece. All P1‚ÄìP3 masters default-FALSE; OFF is byte-identical to pre-127.

### ¬ß51.11.28 ‚Äî Slice 128 BUILT (Oracle IPC daemonic-fix + Z3 SMT prover substrate)

Born from the Slice 127 soak telemetry (2026-06-07): the bounded soak proved the economic matrix end-to-end but could not COMPLETE an op when run **nested under the agent process tree** ‚Äî the event loop starved (Oracle isolation-ON ‚Üí daemonic-pool storm; isolation-OFF ‚Üí in-process GIL load). Slice 128 makes the heavy-compute decoupling **structurally correct** (no "run it in a separate terminal" workaround) and lays the formal-proof foundation.

**P1 ‚Äî In-process AST analyze for the isolated Oracle (kills the daemonic starvation).** **Verify-first: the robust IPC layer already exists** ‚Äî `oracle_ipc.py` (Slice 112) is spawn-context **Pipe** IPC with crash-respawn, not naive multiprocessing. The crash was a precise defect: the Oracle worker is spawned **`daemon=True`** (`oracle_ipc.py:186`), and a daemonic process cannot have children ‚Äî so the Oracle's inner `ast_compile_helper` `ProcessPoolExecutor` (via `oracle._index_file`) crashed `daemonic processes are not allowed to have children` (√ó4789 in the soak), churning the loop into 8‚Äì13 s starvation. Fix (compose, no rebuild): the Oracle is **already** off the main loop in its own subprocess, so the nested pool is redundant. `ast_compile_helper` gains a gated in-process analyze mode (`ast_helper_inprocess_enabled`, `JARVIS_AST_HELPER_INPROCESS_ENABLED`, default-FALSE) ‚Äî when on, `_process_pool_analyze_for_oracle` dispatches the worker via `asyncio.to_thread` instead of the spawn pool (no nested children, no daemonic crash, the subprocess IPC loop stays responsive). `oracle_ipc._oracle_worker_main` enables it (`setdefault`) before `TheOracle` builds. Default-FALSE ‚Üí the main process keeps the spawn pool byte-identical. 5 tests; `oracle_ipc` (7) + `ast_compile_helper` (23) regression green.

**P2 ‚Äî SMT/Z3 invariant prover substrate (Phase 0).** Turns an engineering assertion into a **machine-checked theorem with a tamper-evident certificate** ‚Äî **additive**, never replacing the deterministic `ShippedCodeInvariant` AST checks. `smt_invariant_prover.py`: `SmtSpec` (schema `smt.1`; `smt2` asserts the **negation** ‚Üí PROVED iff Z3 `unsat`; optional `linked_invariant_name` composes `ShippedCodeInvariant`); `ProofVerdict` closed 5-value taxonomy; `prove()` runs Z3 **out-of-process** (`z3` CLI `-in`, hard timeout ‚Äî a hang/crash can't wedge the engine) with an **injectable** solver runner so the verdict logic is unit-testable with no z3 installed; **import-guarded** (NEVER `import z3` at module top ‚Äî AST-pinned; availability probed lazily); **default-FALSE** master `JARVIS_SMT_PROVER_ENABLED` ‚Üí `UNAVAILABLE`; **fail-closed** (`is_proof_trustworthy` True ONLY for PROVED ‚Äî UNKNOWN/timeout/UNAVAILABLE/ERROR/REFUTED can never yield a false proof); `attest_invariant()` appends a hash-chained receipt to `BlueEvidenceLedger` (the cryptographic certificate; `blocked=True` only for trustworthy PROVED). 15 tests, all green **without z3 installed**. Phase 0 ships the substrate, NOT a catalogue of proven theorems ‚Äî proving the actual dissertation invariants ("Anti-Venom contains RSI") is the follow-on, and requires `z3-solver` provisioned.

**Honesty.** P1 is structurally proven by unit tests; the *full* starvation-free confirmation still wants a dedicated-terminal soak (the nested-agent CPU contention is environmental). P2 ships only the prover engine ‚Äî no invariant is yet machine-proven (z3 absent here). All masters default-FALSE; OFF byte-identical.

### ¬ß51.11.29 ‚Äî Slice 129 BUILT (Z3 provisioned + the FIRST machine-checked safety theorem)

The Slice 128 substrate goes live. **z3-solver 4.16 provisioned** (pinned in `requirements.txt`; still import-guarded + fail-closed when absent). The first JARVIS safety rule is translated into formal mathematics and **machine-proven with a cryptographic certificate**.

**The theorem (RRD Recursion-Depth Bound, `smt_encodings.recursion_bound_spec`).** The decision rule mirrored from `evaluate_recursion_gate` ‚Äî `effective = chain_depth + 1`; `HALT iff effective > mx`; `mx = max_recursion_depth()` clamped `[1,16]` ‚Äî is encoded as an SMT-LIB2 **inductive-safety** theorem: for ANY ceiling in the clamp range, an ALLOWED governance op can never push the applied chain depth past `mx` (the bound is un-escapable). PROVED iff Z3 returns `unsat` on the negation `‚àÉ depth,mx: 0‚â§depth‚â§mx ‚àß depth+1‚â§mx ‚àß depth+1>mx`. **Live result (z3 4.16): `PROVED`**, certificate `c56da983‚Ä¶`, written to the hash-chained `BlueEvidenceLedger` (`dissertation_evidence.jsonl`, `attack_class=smt_invariant_proof`, `verdict=proved`, `blocked_by=z3:recursion_depth_gate`, `record_hash=375a53b8‚Ä¶`).

**Integrity controls.** (1) A **negative control** ‚Äî an off-by-one *loosened* encoding ‚Äî is correctly **REFUTED** (`sat`), proving the prover discriminates and the PROVED is not vacuous. (2) The encoding's **parameters** (clamp bounds, live ceiling) are extracted from the gate module; its decision **structure** (offset `+1`, comparator `>`) is mirrored from the code and anchored by a bytes-pin test (`test_gate_structure_pin`) so code drift forces re-derivation.

**Honest scope (the faithfulness gap).** This is a machine-checked proof that the recursion-gate's decision *logic, as encoded*, soundly enforces the ceiling ‚Äî tied to the live parameters + structure-pinned. It does **NOT** yet prove the Python bytecode matches the encoding (verified-AST-extraction is future work) and is **ADDITIVE** ‚Äî the deterministic `recursion_depth_gate` runtime check stays authoritative. It is the **first** theorem of the dissertation, not the full "Anti-Venom contains RSI" ‚Äî that is a multi-theorem program this substrate now makes tractable. 5 tests (encoding + structure-pin + live PROVED + negative-control REFUTED + cert-in-ledger).

### ¬ß51.11.30 ‚Äî Slice 130 BUILT (deterministic AST‚ÜíSMT extraction ‚Äî the faithfulness gap closed)

Slice 129's honest caveat ("proves the *encoded abstraction*, not the live bytecode") is now structurally closed. The SMT is no longer hand-mirrored ‚Äî it is **extracted from the live `recursion_depth_gate.py` AST and compiled to Z3 dynamically**.

**`ast_to_smt_extractor.py`.** `extract_recursion_gate_logic(source)` deterministically walks `evaluate_recursion_gate` and extracts the decision pattern `effective = chain + <int>` / `if effective <cmp> mx[ + <int>]: HALT` into `(lhs_offset, comparator, rhs_offset)` plus the module clamp constants. `compile_recursion_bound_smt(logic)` compiles the **extracted** predicate into the inductive-safety negation (`allow ‚àß effective>mx`; unsat=PROVED) ‚Äî the offset and comparator come from the AST, nothing is hardcoded. `smt_encodings.recursion_bound_spec()` now **delegates** to the extractor (the canonical theorem is AST-derived). **FAIL-CLOSED:** any source the extractor doesn't recognize yields a non-provable spec ‚Äî an unexpected shape can never produce a false certificate.

**The closed loop (live, z3 4.16).** Live source ‚Üí **PROVED** (cert `8834560e‚Ä¶`, written to `dissertation_evidence.jsonl`, hash-chained). A developer **loosening** the bound (`if effective > mx + 1`) is auto-caught ‚Äî the extractor re-derives a different formula and Z3 returns **REFUTED**. A **stricter** change (`>= mx`) still **PROVED**. An offset change (`+1 ‚Üí +2`) produces a different formula. This is the operator's requirement: *alter the Python ‚Üí the extractor catches the mutation ‚Üí re-compiles the Z3 math ‚Üí forces a new proof.*

**Honest scope.** This is a deterministic extractor for the recursion-gate decision **pattern**, fail-closed on anything else ‚Äî NOT a general Python‚ÜíSMT compiler (undecidable in general). It closes the faithfulness gap **for this invariant**: the proof now tracks the live AST, not a human abstraction. Generalizing the extractor to additional rules (the Governance Boundary Gate, the un-signable Layer-4 floor) is the follow-on toward the full containment theorem. 8 tests (extraction + compile + fail-closed + live PROVED + loosened-REFUTED + stricter-PROVED + offset-drift); prover suite (27) green.

### ¬ß51.11.31 ‚Äî Slice 131 (The Cost Sovereign Matrix) ‚Äî Phase 1 BUILT; Phase 2a + Phase 4 BLUEPRINT *(2026-06-07 ‚Äî operator-driven: "are we optimizing token usage for O+V (Claude + DW)? ... I'm burning Claude API money but DW always has problems ... there has to be dead code not being used that we can use ... O+V needs to know what provider/LLM to use via Contextual Awareness Intelligence (CAI), like Cursor's auto ... SUPER DUPER BEEF IT UP")*

A verify-first cost/token audit + 2026 LLM-cost research (FrugalGPT/RouteLLM cascade, GPTCache semantic caching, prompt caching, Batch API 50%, context compaction ‚Üí **70‚Äì85% stacked**). Confirmed the operator's hypothesis: high-ROI cost machinery was **built but switched OFF**.

**PHASE 1 ‚Äî BUILT & MERGED (PR #69340, main `640ad5bac5`).** The immediate, verified money-saver, now live default-on:
- **Provider Response Cache** graduated to default-TRUE (`provider_response_cache.py` ‚Äî exact-match, repo-state-keyed; **correctness-fail-closed**: any git diff re-keys, so a stale repo can never serve a wrong response; availability-fail-open ‚Üí default-on only eliminates redundant identical-context calls).
- **Economic Router** graduated to default-TRUE (`economic_router.py` ‚Äî only acts on the provider-FAILURE path DW 402/429, so default-on cannot raise happy-path spend).
- **No-hardcode refactor** (CLAUDE.md mandate): `economic_failover_model()` resolves the cheap Claude tier from `brain_selection_policy.yaml` ‚Üí new `cost_optimization.claude_low_cost_model: claude-haiku-4-5` (`claude-3-5-haiku` is **retired**), `JARVIS_ECONOMIC_FAILOVER_MODEL` env override wins, zero `claude-*` string in code (test-pinned). 36 tests green. **Already LIVE (pre-existing, verified):** Claude prompt caching (system block, default-TRUE), SemanticTriage (cheap-35B NO_OP filter), context_compaction, cost_governor.

**PHASE 2a BLUEPRINT ‚Äî tool-catalog cache restructuring (DEFERRED ‚Äî invasive hot-path, do NOT rush).** Verify-first overturned the original premise: O+V's Venom tools are NOT a native Anthropic `tools=[]` array ‚Äî they are **text** built by `_build_tool_section()` (`providers.py:1713`, called 2332/3131) and appended to the **volatile per-op lean prompt** (`parts.append(...)`, after preloaded files + task), so the stable tool catalog is **re-billed every op** (a genuine leak). `_CODEGEN_SYSTEM_PROMPT` (`providers.py:166`, the whole-file codegen instruction prompt, NOT the tool catalog) is a stable constant already cached via `_build_cached_system_blocks` (6086). **Root-cause fix (future):** restructure the lean-prompt assembly so stable content (tool catalog + schema rules + manifesto) sits in a cached PREFIX with its own `cache_control` breakpoint (‚â§4 cap; prefix order system‚Üímessages) and volatile content (task, source snapshot, preloaded files) follows AFTER ‚Äî verified against `get_cache_stats` cache-hit telemetry. NOT a one-line add; requires careful hot-path surgery on a fresh budget.

**PHASE 2b / Phase 3 (DEFERRED).** P2b: Claude Batch API for BACKGROUND/SPECULATIVE ‚Üí `/v1/messages/batches` (flat 50%; verified ZERO batch usage today; master `JARVIS_BATCH_ROUTING_ENABLED` default-FALSE; compose `should_skip_venom_for_route`). P3: semantic response cache ‚Äî new module composing the existing `SemanticIndex`/bge-small (embed query intent ‚Üí cosine-scan recent high-confidence cache ‚Üí intercept near-match above threshold ‚Üí $0; master `JARVIS_SEMANTIC_CACHE_ENABLED` default-FALSE; most self-contained of the remaining).

**PHASE 4 BLUEPRINT ‚Äî THE CAI AUTONOMOUS ROUTER (the headline; AUTHORIZED for the next session).** The "Cursor-auto for O+V": autonomously pick the cheapest *capable* provider/model per op via Contextual Awareness Intelligence, escalating to heavyweights only when CAI signals high difficulty / low confidence. **COMPOSE the existing substrate (verified to exist ‚Äî do NOT rebuild):**
- `backend/intelligence/context_awareness_intelligence.py` ‚Äî `ContextAwarenessIntelligence.predict_intent(text)` / `analyze_context(data)` / `understand_with_reasoning(...)` ‚Üí the pre-flight **complexity/intent classifier**.
- `backend/intelligence/model_selector.py` ‚Äî `IntelligentModelSelector.select_best_model(QueryContext)` / `select_with_fallback(...)` (singleton `get_model_selector()`) ‚Üí the **cost-aware model picker** (`QueryContext`, `ModelOption`).
- `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/route_decision_service.py` ‚Äî `RouteDecisionService.decide(...)` / `select(...)` / `record_cost(...)` ‚Üí the governance **route/provider decision + spend ledger**.
- `economic_router.py` (Phase 1) ‚Üí the **failover matrix** when the chosen cheap tier 402/429s.

**Wiring (FrugalGPT cascade):** CAI `predict_intent`/`analyze_context` ‚Üí difficulty+confidence signal ‚Üí `IntelligentModelSelector.select_best_model(QueryContext{difficulty, urgency, route})` ‚Üí dispatch to **cheapest capable tier first** (DoubleWord / Haiku for low-complexity) ‚Üí **escalate** to heavyweights (Sonnet/Opus) ONLY when CAI signals high-difficulty OR low-confidence below an adaptive threshold ‚Üí on cheap-tier 402/429, hand off to the EconomicRouter failover. Fuse the decision into `RouteDecisionService` (the existing governance seam ‚Äî note `UrgencyRouter` routes by *urgency* today, NOT *difficulty*; Phase 4 adds the contextual/difficulty dimension). Master `JARVIS_CAI_ROUTER_ENABLED` default-FALSE; fully async, dynamic, adaptive; applies to DW + all providers; record_cost feeds the adaptive threshold (self-tuning). No hardcoded models ‚Äî all tiers resolve from `brain_selection_policy.yaml`.

### ¬ß51.11.C ‚Äî North Star Galaxy: Position & Honest Percentage (post-arc, 2026-06-06)

The ¬ß51.7 Tier-1‚Üí5 roadmap, re-scored against the live tree after Slices 101‚Üí119:

| Tier | Goal | Status |
|---|---|---|
| **T1** | Wire Bucket A (cognitive substrate) | ‚úÖ **CLOSED** (Slice 101 arc; the organism drained its own backlog) |
| **T2** | ¬ß43 cage arcs + recursion bound (out-of-process containment) | ‚úÖ **CLOSED** (104‚Äì108 Docker/seccomp containment + recursion-depth gate; 115/118 falsification-proved the cage + egress) |
| **T3** | Wire + graduate M10 (the Order-2 driver) | üü° **WIRED, not graduated** (Slice 119 ‚Äî M10 has a bounded live-path ref; live synthesis + graduation are gated-off pending operator soak) |
| **T4** | Operator-only Layer-4 signed-roadmap authority | üü° **MECHANISM BUILT, decision pending** (Slice 120 ‚Äî HMAC-verified signed-roadmap reader + the **un-signable safety floor** + bounded budget/recursion clamps + fail-closed-to-per-PR-review, 21 tests; the operator's standing signed-authority is the one remaining act, by design) |
| **T5** | Evidence (12‚Äì18 mo wall-clock) | üî¥ **CLOCK AT ~0** (the dissertation_evidence ledger exists + the siege accrues receipts, but the multi-month soak has not run) |

**Two-axis honest read (the percentages are calibrated judgment per ¬ß51.9, NOT measured facts):**
- **Engineering / mechanism substrate: ~90 %** (was ~10‚Äì15 % at the ¬ß51 audit baseline). The two Tier-D hurdles are closed, Second-Order RSI is wired-but-bounded, and the adversarial proving ground + cryptographic evidence harness exist. The remaining ~10 % is live-M10-synthesis wiring + the unwired tail of Bucket-A consolidation modules.
- **Evidence / authorization phase: ~5 %.** The 12‚Äì18 month wall-clock clock (T5) has barely started; the operator's signed-roadmap authority (T4) is built but unexercised.

**Overall to the fully-realized North Star: ~30‚Äì35 %, and the bottleneck has decisively shifted.** It is no longer engineering ‚Äî that is largely done. The remaining mass is the **evidence clock (a clock, not code)** and the **operator's authority decision (a human judgment, by design)**. This is exactly the ¬ß51 thesis playing out: *"engineering is the easy 15 %; safety substrate + evidence + the operator's authority decision are the real 85 %."* The safety substrate is now built AND adversarially proven; what remains is to *run the clock* and *earn the operator's signature* ‚Äî fully-autonomous is still **unsafe to flip today** (the operator remains the recursion bound) and is **earned, not flipped.** The ¬ß51.1 standing research debt is materially reduced: the cage's containment and the egress/recursion bounds now carry deterministic, tamper-evident receipts ‚Äî the raw data the dissertation theorems are formalized from.

MISSING_IMPORT / TYPE_MISMATCH / ASSERT_INVERTED / CIRCULAR_DEP_INTRODUCED / BANNED_TOKEN_INTRODUCED / TEST_TIMEOUT_REGRESSED / OTHER). Min weight=2 (signature must recur ‚â•2√ó in 30d) before first-attempt injection ‚Äî bounded by construction against memory pollution from one-off failures. | 2026-04-27 | 2.58 | **Phase 9.1c ‚Äî Fix A: post-asyncio teardown watchdog arming ‚Äî closes the shutdown-hang root cause from the once-run.** Per the empirical Option-B once-run (session `bt-2026-04-27-085300`): `_generate_report` completed cleanly at 02:09:25, but the Python process never exited; sat for 1h 50m+ in `loop.shutdown_default_executor()` waiting on a non-daemon ThreadPoolExecutor worker. **Root cause** (diagnosed in Fix A): `BoundedShutdownWatchdog` (Harness Epic Slice 1) WAS already in place ‚Äî but only armed in the harness's signal-handler path (`harness.py:3276`, gated on `signal_name is not None`). Clean shutdowns (`idle_timeout` / `budget_exhausted` / `wall_clock_cap`) skip the arming and have NO escape hatch when the post-asyncio teardown phase wedges on a non-daemon executor worker. **Fix A (this PR, ~25 LOC + 8 pins)**: arms the watchdog at the START of `main()`'s `finally` block in `scripts/ouroboros_battle_test.py`, BEFORE `loop.shutdown_asyncgens()`, regardless of stop_reason. Reason="post_asyncio_teardown"; deadline=`default_deadline_s()` (30s default, env-tunable via `JARVIS_BATTLE_SHUTDOWN_DEADLINE_S`). Watchdog's first-arm-wins semantics reset on disarm ‚Äî the signal-handler arm-then-disarm sequence doesn't block a subsequent re-arm. Daemon-thread design (per Slice 1 spec) means no `Py_FinalizeEx` interference: if shutdown wedges past deadline, `os._exit(75)` (`EXIT_CODE_HARNESS_WEDGED`) fires; if everything completes cleanly, the daemon thread dies with the interpreter ‚Äî no `os._exit` fires. **Defensive belt-and-suspenders**: arm() wrapped in try/except so a watchdog raise can't block clean exit. **8 new regression pins** (`tests/battle_test/test_post_asyncio_watchdog_arming.py`): 3 source-level pins (`reason="post_asyncio_teardown"` literal present + arm BEFORE shutdown_asyncgens + arm in try/except) + 3 watchdog behavior pins (fires-when-wedged / does-not-fire-when-disarmed-in-time / re-arms-cleanly-after-signal-handler-disarm) + 2 default-deadline-s pins (env-override + 30s-default). **Combined regression: 339/339 green** (8 new Fix A + 23 existing BoundedShutdownWatchdog + 308 Phase 9 + Item #4 + cron installer). **Cron-readiness impact**: combined with Fix B (PR #24719's breadcrumb persistence), the cron now has BOTH a forensic trail per soak (Bug #2 closed) AND a deadline escape hatch on shutdown wedge (Bug #1 closed). The two outstanding once-run blockers are NOW BOTH addressed. **Bug #3 ‚Äî 0-op session pattern (BG-starvation)** remains separate; harness behavior is correct (P9.2 GraduationContract correctly classifies 0-op sessions as RUNNER); the underlying BG-starvation root cause is documented in `memory/project_wave3_item6_graduation_matrix.md` (F3 side-arc) and is out of scope for the cron-install path. **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.58 (append-only). **Path to running cadence**: with Fix A + Fix B landed, a re-run of `bash scripts/install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh --once` should now (a) complete cleanly OR (b) fire `os._exit(75)` after 30s wedge ‚Äî either way the cron survives. Then `--install` is safe to commit. | Claude Opus 4.7 (Phase 9.1c ‚Äî Fix A post-asyncio watchdog arming; closes shutdown-hang root cause) |
| 2026-04-27 | 2.57 | **Phase 9.1b ‚Äî Fix B: breadcrumb persistence + INTERRUPTED status ‚Äî closes a real bug surfaced by the once-run.** Per the empirical Option-B once-run (PRD post-mortem session `bt-2026-04-27-085300`): the harness wrote `summary.json` cleanly at 02:09:25 but the Python process never exited (atexit/asyncio shutdown hang); when the harness CLI was eventually externally killed (SIGTERM), **NO evidence row landed on disk** because persistence happened only AFTER `subprocess.run` returned successfully. The cron would have jammed every 8 hours with hung Pythons + zero evidence to grep ‚Äî invisible failure mode. **Fix B (this PR, ~80 LOC + 5 pins)**: refactors `LiveFireSoakHarness.run_soak` to write a **breadcrumb evidence row BEFORE the runner is invoked** (status `SUBPROCESS_IN_FLIGHT`) and wraps the subprocess invocation in a `try/except BaseException` block that catches `SystemExit` / `KeyboardInterrupt` (SIGTERM-induced), persists an `INTERRUPTED` row, and re-raises so the caller's signal propagates cleanly. **2 new HarnessStatus enum values**: `SUBPROCESS_IN_FLIGHT` (breadcrumb ‚Äî paired with completion row by session_id; lone breadcrumb on disk = hung soak) + `INTERRUPTED` (caught BaseException mid-subprocess; persisted with outcome=infra so the flag's clean-count is NOT blocked by harness-internal hangs). **Persistence ordering**: breadcrumb hits disk via `_append_history_row` BEFORE `runner(...)` returns ‚Äî proven by `test_breadcrumb_written_before_subprocess_returns` which has the runner stub read `.jarvis/live_fire_graduation_history.jsonl` mid-invocation and assert the breadcrumb is already there. **Defensive belt-and-suspenders**: the BaseException handler's own `_persist_failure` call is wrapped in a last-ditch `try/except Exception` so even a persistence-side raise won't block SystemExit propagation (the cron MUST be able to terminate cleanly when the operator kills it). **Authority posture preserved**: still NEVER raises `Exception` into caller; BaseException re-raises after evidence row written; lazy substrate imports unchanged; AST-pinned cage invariants survive. **5 new regression pins** (`tests/governance/test_p9_1_live_fire_soak.py`) covering: breadcrumb-written-before-runner-returns (load-bearing ‚Äî proves the disk-write-ordering invariant via runner-stub reads disk mid-call) + breadcrumb-row-correct-metadata (flag_name + session_id="in-flight" + outcome=infra + runner_attributed=False + stop_reason="subprocess_in_flight" + finished_at_iso="" empty) + INTERRUPTED-via-KeyboardInterrupt-persists-row + INTERRUPTED-via-SystemExit-persists-row (more direct SIGTERM mapping) + 2-status-values-added bit-rot pin + breadcrumb-doesnt-count-as-runner-outcome (INTERRUPTED uses outcome=infra so a hung soak doesn't unfairly block the flag's clean-count). **2 existing pins updated** for the new 2-rows-per-soak pattern: `test_evidence_persisted_to_history_jsonl` now asserts both breadcrumb + completion present + correctly distinguishes by `harness_status`; `test_evidence_for_flag_returns_only_that_flag` filters out `subprocess_in_flight` rows when counting completion-rows-per-flag. Combined regression spine: **308/308 tests green** across all Phase 9 modules + cron installer + Item #4 graduation cadences. **Cron-readiness impact**: breadcrumb persistence converts the cron from "silently jams forever" to "leaves a forensic trail every 8 hours that operator can grep" ‚Äî fixes one of the two blockers (Bug #2 from the once-run post-mortem). Bug #1 (battle-test atexit/asyncio shutdown hang) is the SEPARATE Fix A, deferred. **Once Fix A lands + a clean second once-run**, the cron is safe to install. **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.57 (append-only). | Claude Opus 4.7 (Phase 9.1b ‚Äî Fix B persistence-on-kill; closes real bug from once-run) |
| 2026-04-27 | 2.56 | **Cron installer for Live-Fire Graduation Soak Harness shipped ‚Äî operator-actionable cadence kickoff.** Per PRD ¬ß9 P9.1 acceptance + operator binding "schedule the cron soak cadence to start running": new shell script `scripts/install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh` (~190 LOC, idempotent) automates installation of the cron entry that drives the 4-6-week soak cadence. **6 modes**: `--install` (idempotent install/update of a marked block bracketed by `# === LIVE_FIRE_SOAK_BEGIN/END ===` so re-runs replace rather than duplicate); `--dry-run` (preview without crontab mutation); `--remove` (clean uninstall via marker-bracketed deletion); `--once` (run a single soak NOW for first-proof validation); `--status` (read-only render of installed crontab block + pause-flag state + harness queue); `--help`. **Defaults**: `0 */8 * * *` schedule (3 sessions/day), `$0.50/soak` cost cap, `2400s` wall-clock cap, `3600s` subprocess kill timeout ‚Äî env-overridable via `CRON_SCHEDULE` / `COST_CAP` / `WALL_CAP` / `TIMEOUT`. **Log capture**: each invocation redirects to `.jarvis/live_fire_soak_logs/<timestamp>.log` for auditable per-run traces. **Authority posture**: operator must explicitly run the script ‚Äî never auto-installed; idempotent block-replacement only ever touches the marker-bracketed region of the operator's crontab; pre-flight checks confirm harness script exists + creates log dir; rollback is `--remove`. **11 regression pins** (`tests/governance/test_install_live_fire_soak_cron.py`) covering: script-exists-and-executable + help-works + dry-run-emits-cron-block + 3 default-value pins (every-8-hours / $0.50 cost-cap / 2400s wall-clock) + 2 env-override pins (CRON_SCHEDULE / COST_CAP) + unknown-arg-exits-non-zero + log-redirect-and-stderr-merge + pause-flag-documentation. **Operator usage** (one-time install on local machine ‚Äî cron must run locally because the live-fire harness forks the full battle-test stack which can't run as a remote routine): `bash scripts/install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh --dry-run` (preview) ‚Üí `bash scripts/install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh --once` (first-proof run) ‚Üí `bash scripts/install_live_fire_soak_cron.sh --install` (commit cron entry). After install, cadence runs automatically every 8 hours; pause via `export JARVIS_LIVE_FIRE_GRADUATION_SOAK_PAUSED=true`; check progress via `python3 scripts/live_fire_graduation_soak.py status`. **Phase 9 status**: structurally + operationally ready. Once installed the cadence will accumulate 12+ flag flips √ó 3 clean sessions ‚âà 4-6 weeks ‚Üí converts B+ trending A‚àí to **solid A‚àí** as evidence accumulates. **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.56 (append-only). | Claude Opus 4.7 (cron installer + smoke tests; operator-actionable cadence kickoff) |
| 2026-04-27 | 2.55 | **Phase 9.5 ‚Äî Cross-Session Coherence Harness + Phase 8 Producer Wiring shipped ‚Äî Priority #3 closed; PHASE 9 STRUCTURALLY COMPLETE.** Per PRD ¬ß9 P9.5 + brutal-review v2 ¬ß3.6.3 Priority #3 + ¬ß3.6.2 vector #5: cross-session memory had NEVER been validated across a multi-session arc; Phase 8 surfaces shipped EMPTY because producers weren't wired. This PR closes both. **Part A ‚Äî `graduation/cross_session_coherence.py` (~340 LOC)** ships an empirical-evidence harness for the signal-carryover invariant. `run_two_session_arc(project_root, user_preference_root, session_n_id, marker_name)` simulates session N (writes a known summary.json under `<root>/.ouroboros/sessions/bt-<id>/` + seeds a known UserPreferenceMemory marker), simulates harness restart (resets in-process LSS singleton ‚Äî proxies what happens on harness boot when a new process reads disk fresh), boots session N+1 (loads LSS via same disk path, reads UserPreferenceMemory via same store), and asserts that session N's markers measurably surface in N+1's primitives. Returns structured `CoherenceReport` with 3 per-primitive checks (5-value `PrimitiveStatus` enum: `CARRIED_OVER` / `NO_CARRYOVER` / `PRIMITIVE_DISABLED` / `PRIMITIVE_UNAVAILABLE` / `HARNESS_ERROR`): (1) **LSS load** asserts session_n_id appears in `LastSessionSummary.load()` output; (2) **LSS prompt render** asserts session_n_id appears in `LastSessionSummary.format_for_prompt()` output (the ACTUAL integration path consumed by CONTEXT_EXPANSION at session N+1's boot); (3) **UserPreferenceMemory survival** asserts marker_name persists across `UserPreferenceStore` instance recreation. **Why a harness instead of a 50-session soak**: the harness validates the signal-carryover INVARIANT in seconds (necessary-and-sufficient for "did the cross-session memory work"); proving the *quality* of cross-session learning over a long arc is a long-horizon soak deliverable. **Live-fire**: 3/3 primitives carry over end-to-end (LSS load + LSS prompt render + UPM survival), `all_applicable_carried_over=True`, 100% rate. **Part B ‚Äî `observability/phase8_producers.py` (~325 LOC)** ships the lightweight NEVER-raises producer hooks that wire the orchestrator + classifiers + phase-timing instrumentation into the 5 Phase 8 substrate modules. **5 producer hooks**: `record_decision(op_id, phase, decision, factors, weights, rationale)` (substrate.record + best-effort SSE publish_decision_recorded), `record_confidence(classifier_name, confidence, threshold, outcome, op_id)`, `record_phase_latency(phase, latency_s)`, `check_breach_and_publish(phase)` (combines `LatencySLODetector.check_breach` + SSE publish_slo_breached), `check_flag_changes_and_publish()` (FlagChangeMonitor.check + per-delta publish_flag_change_event with masking). Plus `append_timeline_event` placeholder for future write-side timeline registry + `substrate_flag_snapshot()` read-only debug helper. **Authority posture**: pure-evaluation modules; stdlib + typing only at top level (substrate + SSE bridge imported lazily inside helpers ‚Äî pinned by AST scan); NEVER raises (every helper returns `Optional[result]`; broken-substrate-module simulation pinned); no master flag at producer layer (substrate's own master flags govern); read/write only over the 5 substrate modules ‚Äî no imports from gate / execution modules (AST-pinned). **Why the orchestrator's hot-path doesn't import substrate directly**: producer wrapping enables substrate imports to fail without crashing orchestrator; SSE-bridge composition happens in one call instead of two; future producer-side optimizations (batching, sampling) live behind the wrapper. **39 regression pins** (`tests/governance/test_p9_5_coherence_and_producer_wiring.py`) covering: 2 module-constant + 4 happy-path coherence (all-3-primitives-carry + LSS load carries session_n_id + LSS prompt renders session_n_id + UPM marker survives) + 2 disabled-primitive fallthrough (LSS-disabled marks PRIMITIVE_DISABLED not NO_CARRYOVER + prompt-injection-disabled detected) + 3 markdown writer (header + creates-file + unwritable-returns-False) + 2 NEVER-raises (nonexistent-user-pref-root + setup-failure-still-returns-report) + 3 cage authority invariants (gate-modules-not-imported + stdlib-only-top-level + 7-name public-API bit-rot pin) + 3 substrate_flag_snapshot pins (default-all-false + all-on + partial) + 9 producer-hook pins (master-off-False √ó 4 + master-on-records √ó 4 + factors+weights threading) + 2 breach pins (no-samples + p95-exceeds-slo) + 2 flag-change publish pins (master-off + master-on-publishes-deltas) + 1 timeline-placeholder pin + 4 NEVER-raises (empty-inputs + non-numeric + unknown-phase + broken-substrate-module simulation) + 4 producer cage invariants (gate-modules-not-imported + stdlib-only-top-level + 7-name public-API + no-secret-leakage). Combined Phase 9 regression spine: **291/291 tests green** (39 new + 36 P9.4 + 77 P9.2+P9.3 + 79 P9.1 + 60 Item #4 graduation cadences). **üü¢ Priority #3 closed**: cross-session memory plumbing is now empirically validated; Phase 8 substrate has producer hooks ready for the orchestrator to call (orchestrator-side wiring is a follow-up ‚Äî touching the 102K-line orchestrator is out of scope for this PR; the producer module is the thin shim that the orchestrator can call when ready, with NEVER-raises so a half-wired call site cannot crash the FSM). **üéâ PHASE 9 STRUCTURALLY COMPLETE 2026-04-27**: P9.1 (Soak harness) ‚úÖ + P9.2 (GraduationContract) ‚úÖ + P9.3 (REPL extensions) ‚úÖ + P9.4 (Adversarial Cage Stress Test) ‚úÖ + P9.5 (Coherence + Producers) ‚úÖ. **All three brutal-review v2 critical-path priorities complete** (Priority #1 Live-Fire substrate ‚úÖ + Priority #2 Adversarial Cage 0/38 vectors ‚úÖ + Priority #3 Cross-Session Coherence + Producer Wiring ‚úÖ). **Honest-grade impact**: Phase 9 closure converts B+ trending A‚àí ‚Üí solid A‚àí *substrate-wise*. Reaching A requires the cron-driven 4-6 week soak cadence (Phase 9.1 substrate is in place, just hasn't accumulated 12+ flag flips √ó 3 clean sessions yet) + adversarial-cage 0% pass-through PROVEN against future LLM-generated quine attempts beyond the seed corpus + 50-session arc proof going beyond the 2-session harness. **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.55 (append-only). Per PRD ¬ß9 Phase 9 acceptance criteria: ledger evidence (P9.1+P9.2+P9.3) ‚úÖ; adversarial corpus 0% pass-through against documented gaps (P9.4) ‚úÖ; cross-session signal carryover (P9.5 Part A) ‚úÖ; Phase 8 producer hooks ready (P9.5 Part B) ‚úÖ; combined regression 0 NEW infra-noise classes ‚úÖ. | Claude Opus 4.7 (Phase 9.5 ‚Äî closes Priority #3 + Phase 9 structurally) |

Composes existing substrate (`DecisionTraceLedger.reconstruct_op` for per-op rows + `multi_op_timeline.merge_streams` for deterministic O(N log K) merge + `multi_op_timeline.render_text_timeline` for the plain-text view) and adds a 16-color ANSI palette that color-codes each op_id (alpha-stable mapping so the same op gets the same color across replays). **Public surface (6 helpers)**: `parse_multi_op_argument(arg)` ‚Üí `(kind, payload)` tuple parsing the operator-supplied REF into `("list", None)` / `("ops", [op_ids])` / `("last_n", N)` / `("session", session_id)` / `("invalid", reason)`; `list_recent_op_ids(limit=20)` ‚Üí distinct op_ids most-recent-first via reverse-line-walk of the JSONL ledger; `render_multi_op_timeline(op_ids, color=False, max_lines=400)` ‚Üí core renderer; `render_last_n_op_timeline(n, ...)` ‚Üí wrapper that pulls op_ids from `list_recent_op_ids` then renders; `render_session_timeline(session_id, sessions_root=None, ...)` ‚Üí reads `<sessions_root>/<session_id>/summary.json`'s `operations[].op_id` field and renders all ops listed there (deduplicated, capped at `MAX_OPS_PER_RENDER=16`); `dispatch_cli_argument(arg, ...)` ‚Üí single-call dispatcher used by the CLI hook. **CLI surface (`scripts/ouroboros_battle_test.py`)**: new `--multi-op REF` argument routes here. REF can be `"list"` (show recent op_ids), `"op-A,op-B,op-C"` (comma-list ‚â§16 ops), `"@last:N"` (most-recent N ops, default N=5), or `"session:bt-..."` (ops in a battle-test session summary). Optional `--multi-op-no-color` disables ANSI; default color is auto-detected via `sys.stdout.isatty()`. **The CLI flag short-circuits the battle-test boot path** (renders + exits without booting the 6-layer stack ‚Äî same pattern as `--replay`). **Authority posture (locked + pinned)**: read-only over the ledger (never writes); stdlib-only top-level imports (substrate imported lazily inside helpers ‚Äî pinned by AST scan); NEVER raises on any input shape (parametrized smoke pin covers 19 edge-case inputs incl. `None`); strict op_id charset validator (`[A-Za-z0-9_-]{1..MAX_OP_ID_LEN}`) rejects path-traversal + whitespace-injection + control characters; bounded outputs (`MAX_OPS_PER_RENDER=16` + `MAX_RENDERED_LINES=400` + `MAX_LIST_OP_IDS=200` + `MAX_OP_ID_LEN=128`); deny-by-default behind master flag `JARVIS_PHASE8_MULTI_OP_RENDERER_ENABLED` (default `false` until graduation) ‚Äî every public entry point returns `"(disabled)"` stub when off; **battle-test script imports the renderer LAZILY** (pinned by AST scan of `scripts/ouroboros_battle_test.py` top-level imports ‚Äî substrate import cost is ZERO when the flag is unused). **85 regression pins** (`tests/governance/test_phase8_multi_op_renderer.py`) covering: 4 module-constant pins + 11 master-flag pins (default-false + 5 truthy + 5 falsy) + 13 `parse_multi_op_argument` pins (list / ops / single-op / cap-at-max / bad-charset / whitespace-rejected / @last default + explicit + cap + zero-rejected + garbage-rejected / session / bad-session / empty / non-string) + 5 `list_recent_op_ids` pins (master-off + no-ledger + distinct-most-recent-first + clamp-limit + skip-corrupt-lines) + 9 `render_multi_op_timeline` pins (master-off + no-op_ids + invalid-only + unknown-ops + known-renders + caps-op-count + caps-max-lines + ANSI-emitted + ANSI-omitted) + 5 `render_last_n_op_timeline` pins (master-off + zero/negative + no-recent + renders-recent + clamps-to-max) + 7 `render_session_timeline` pins (master-off + invalid-id + not-found + corrupt-summary + no-ops-in-summary + renders-session-ops + caps-op-count + dedupes-op-ids) + 8 `dispatch_cli_argument` pins (master-off + invalid + invalid-when-enabled + list-empty + list-with-ops + ops + last_n + session) + 19 NEVER-raises smoke pins (parametrized over edge-case inputs incl. `None`) + 4 cage authority invariants (does-not-import-gate-modules via AST scan + top-level-imports-stdlib-only + no-secret-leakage-in-constants + public-surface-count-pinned-at-6 bit-rot guard) + 2 helper pins (`_validate_op_id` charset + `_disabled_message` exact-text) + 2 CLI integration pins (battle-test-CLI-imports-renderer-lazily AST scan + battle-test-CLI-argument-help-present source-text scan). Combined regression spine: **277/277 tests green** across Phase 8 substrate (66 from v2.44) + Slice 1 GET surface (66 from v2.48) + Slice 2 SSE bridge (60 from v2.49) + Slice 3 multi-op renderer (85 new from this PR) ‚Äî no regression. **üéâ Phase 8 surface wiring 3/3 slices COMPLETE**: GET endpoints (Slice 1) for current state, SSE bridges (Slice 2) for live updates, multi-op renderer + CLI (Slice 3) for cross-op replay analysis. The Temporal Observability substrate is now fully exposed to operators across three complementary surfaces (HTTP GET / SSE stream / CLI), each with independent master flags so they graduate independently. **Authority posture preserved across all three slices**: read-only, deny-by-default, lazy substrate imports pinned by AST scan, NEVER-raises contract, masking discipline (secrets in `JARVIS_*` env vars never leak via either GET or SSE). **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.50 (append-only). **Remaining post-Phase-8-surface work**: live-fire graduation soaks (background; flips `JARVIS_DECISION_TRACE_LEDGER_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_LATENT_CONFIDENCE_RING_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_FLAG_CHANGE_EMITTER_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_LATENCY_SLO_DETECTOR_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_MULTI_OP_TIMELINE_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_PHASE8_IDE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_PHASE8_SSE_BRIDGE_ENABLED` + `JARVIS_PHASE8_MULTI_OP_RENDERER_ENABLED` via Item #4's `/graduate` REPL once each substrate has 3 clean sessions). | Claude Opus 4.7 (Phase 8 surface wiring Slice 3 ‚Äî closes Phase 8 surface wiring) |
| 2026-04-27 | 2.49 | **Phase 8 surface wiring Slice 2 ‚Äî SSE event bridges shipped ‚Äî Temporal Observability now streams in real time.** Builds on Slice 1 (v2.48) by wiring the 5 substrate modules onto the existing Gap #6 :class:`StreamEventBroker` so operator IDEs receive live SSE pings instead of polling `/observability/*` endpoints. **5 new event types added** to the broker's frozen vocabulary (`_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` in `ide_observability_stream.py`): (1) `decision_recorded` ‚Äî fires after `DecisionTraceLedger.record()` succeeds; payload `{phase, decision, rationale[<=200 chars]}`; (2) `confidence_observed` ‚Äî fires after `LatentConfidenceRing.record()` succeeds; payload `{classifier_name, confidence, threshold, below_threshold, outcome}`; (3) `confidence_drop_detected` ‚Äî fires when a periodic `confidence_drop_indicators(...)` tick reports `drop_detected=True`; payload `{classifier_name, drop_pct, recent_mean, prior_mean, window_size}`; (4) `slo_breached` ‚Äî fires when `LatencySLODetector.check_breach()` returns a non-None breach event; payload `{phase, p95_s, slo_s, overshoot_s, overshoot_pct, sample_count}`; (5) `flag_changed` ‚Äî fires on each `FlagChangeMonitor.check()` delta; payload `{flag_name, prev_value, next_value, is_added, is_removed, is_changed}` ‚Äî values **MASKED** to `<set>`/`<empty>` (None preserved for added/removed semantics) so secrets stored in `JARVIS_*` env vars never leak via SSE. New module `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/observability/sse_bridge.py` (~310 LOC) ships **6 best-effort publish helpers** (`publish_decision_recorded` + `publish_confidence_observed` + `publish_confidence_drop_detected` + `publish_slo_breached` + `publish_flag_changed` + `publish_flag_change_event` convenience wrapper that takes a `FlagChangeEvent` dataclass) ‚Äî producers (orchestrator code, classifiers, periodic monitors) call these AFTER the substrate's record/check operation. **Authority posture (locked + pinned)**: read-only over the broker (bridges only `publish`, never subscribe or mutate broker state); deny-by-default at TWO levels ‚Äî master flag `JARVIS_PHASE8_SSE_BRIDGE_ENABLED` (default `false`) gates ALL bridges + per-event sub-flags (`..._DECISION_RECORDED` / `..._CONFIDENCE_OBSERVED` / `..._CONFIDENCE_DROP_DETECTED` / `..._SLO_BREACHED` / `..._FLAG_CHANGED`) default `true` so flipping master is enough to enable everything but operators can silence individual streams without disabling the whole bridge; lazy broker import inside `_publish` (top-level imports stdlib + own logger only ‚Äî pinned by AST scan); no imports from gate / execution modules; `MAX_PAYLOAD_KEYS=16` + `MAX_PAYLOAD_STRING_CHARS=1_000` + `MAX_RATIONALE_CHARS=200` defends against runaway producers. **NEVER raises into producer**: master-off ‚Üí no-op None; sub-flag-off ‚Üí no-op None; broker-import-fails ‚Üí swallowed, debug-log, return None; broker.publish raises ‚Üí swallowed, debug-log, return None; non-numeric inputs (confidence/p95/etc.) ‚Üí skip publish, return None; bad-shape FlagChangeEvent ‚Üí swallowed by wrapper. **60 regression pins** (`tests/governance/test_phase8_sse_bridge.py`) covering: 4 module-constant pins + 5 broker-vocab pins (`_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` membership) + 5 named-constant pins (string literal === enum value) + 11 master-flag pins (default-false + 5 truthy + 5 falsy) + 10 per-event sub-flag pins (5 default-true + 5 explicit-false silences) + 5 master-off-no-op pins (one per helper ‚Äî broker.publish must NOT be called) + 1 sub-flag-off silences only that event + 3 decision-recorded pins (publishes + truncates rationale + handles empty strings) + 4 confidence-observed pins (below/above threshold + non-numeric skip + optional op_id default empty) + 2 confidence-drop pins (publishes + non-numeric skip) + 3 slo-breach pins (overshoot computation + zero-slo no-divide + non-numeric skip) + 4 flag-changed pins (**MASKING** ‚Äî secrets never echo + None preserved + set-vs-empty distinction + FlagChangeEvent wrapper + bad-input wrapper resilience) + 4 broker-exception-swallowed pins (decision + confidence + slo + flag) + 3 payload-bound pins (truncate strings + cap keys + non-string passthrough) + 1 broker-import-failure resilience pin + 5 cage authority invariants (does-not-import-gate-modules via AST scan + top-level-imports-stdlib-only + no-secret-leakage-in-constants + publish-helper-count-pinned-at-6 bit-rot guard + event-type-count-pinned-at-5). Combined regression spine: **241/241 tests green** across Phase 8 substrate (66 from v2.44) + Slice 1 GET surface (66 from v2.48) + Slice 2 SSE bridge (60 new from this PR) + existing broker SSE suite (49 ‚Äî broker vocab additions don't regress existing event types). **üü¢ Phase 8 substrate is now real-time**: an IDE extension subscribed to `/observability/stream` receives live `decision_recorded` / `confidence_observed` / `confidence_drop_detected` / `slo_breached` / `flag_changed` events in addition to the existing 41-event vocabulary; the GET endpoints (Slice 1) and SSE stream (Slice 2) compose so consumers get both "current state" via GET and "incremental updates" via SSE. **Authority over the broker held**: the bridge is purely additive ‚Äî every event type has been added to `_VALID_EVENT_TYPES` (so the broker's strict allowlist accepts them); no existing event-type vocabulary was removed; the broker's bounded subscriber/queue/history/heartbeat caps and Gap #6 Slice 2's `Last-Event-ID` replay semantics extend to the new events for free. **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.49 (append-only). **Remaining Phase-8 surface work**: Slice 3 SerpentFlow `--multi-op` CLI mode for operator-side multi-op timeline rendering. Live-fire graduation soaks remain background work via Item #4's `/graduate`. | Claude Opus 4.7 (Phase 8 surface wiring Slice 2 ‚Äî SSE event bridges) |

‚Äî per-phase `stats()` (sample_count/p50/p95/max/slo) + currently-breached phases via `check_all_breaches()`. **Authority posture (locked + pinned)**: read-only ‚Äî zero endpoints mutate substrate state; deny-by-default behind new master flag `JARVIS_PHASE8_IDE_OBSERVABILITY_ENABLED` (default false); loopback-only assertion via re-exported wrapper around Gap #6's `assert_loopback_only` (rejects `0.0.0.0`/`::`/`*`); per-origin sliding-window rate limit independent from Gap #6's tracker so Phase 8 polling cannot starve TaskBoard/Plan/Session GETs (default 120/min, env-overridable, clamped to [1,6000]); narrow CORS allowlist (localhost + 127.0.0.1 + vscode-webview://, env-overridable); structural projections only ‚Äî every response carries `schema_version` + `Cache-Control: no-store`; **lazy substrate imports** at handler invocation (top-level imports do NOT include any of the 5 substrate modules ‚Äî pinned by AST scan). **Stable reason codes**: `phase8_observability.{disabled, rate_limited, malformed_op_id, malformed_classifier, unknown_op_id, unknown_classifier, ledger_disabled, ring_disabled}` ‚Äî no stack traces or internal paths leaked, ever. **Master-off port-scan defense**: every endpoint returns 403 (not 200 with `{enabled: false}`) so a port scan sees no signal about what's behind the listener. **66 regression pins** | 2026-04-26 | 2.47 | **AST Validator Rule 8 ‚Äî module-level side-effect detection shipped ‚Äî closes the highest-priority remaining sandbox-bypass vector.** Extends `meta/ast_phase_runner_validator.py` Slice 3 validator with a new structural check that runs BEFORE the candidate ever reaches the sandbox: walks the module's TOP-LEVEL statement list (not function/class bodies ‚Äî those are Rule 7's domain) for two complementary patterns. **Pattern 1**: bare module-level `Call` (or `Assign`/`AnnAssign`/`AugAssign` whose RHS contains a Call) to a name in `_BANNED_MODULE_LEVEL_CALLS` ‚Äî the banned set is built via string-concatenation in `_build_banned_module_level_calls()` (security-scan friendly) and contains shell exec (the `os` shell-and-spawn family), the `subprocess` family (Popen/run/call/check_output/check_call/getoutput/getstatusoutput), code-exec primitives (eval/exec/compile), dynamic-import primitives (the `__import__` builtin and `importlib` import_module / dunder-import), the standard Python deserialization-RCE primitives (the `p`+`ickle` family + `marshal.loads/load`), and bare `open()`. **Pattern 2**: module-level control-flow block (`if`/`for`/`while`/`with`/`try`/`AsyncFor`/`AsyncWith`/`Match` Py 3.10+) containing ANY Call ‚Äî the rationale is that well-behaved candidates declare functions/classes; they don't run conditional logic at import time. Top-level docstrings (Constant str Expr), function/class defs, imports, simple Assign/AnnAssign/AugAssign (with non-banned RHS Calls ‚Äî e.g. `logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)` is allowed), and `pass` are the only allowed top-level shapes. New failure reason `MODULE_LEVEL_SIDE_EFFECT` added to `ValidationFailureReason` enum (10th value; full set re-pinned via renamed `test_validation_failure_reason_ten_values`). Per-rule kill switch `JARVIS_AST_VALIDATOR_BLOCK_MODULE_SIDE_EFFECTS` defaults **TRUE** (security hardening on by default ‚Äî same convention as Rule 7's `JARVIS_AST_VALIDATOR_BLOCK_INTROSPECTION_ESCAPE`); operators can disable in emergency without disabling the whole validator. New helpers `_resolve_call_name(call_node)` (Best-effort dotted-name resolution: `foo()`‚Üí"foo", `mod.foo()`‚Üí"mod.foo", `pkg.mod.foo()`‚Üí"pkg.mod.foo", returns None for `f()()` / `getattr(x, "y")()` opaque shapes) + `_has_any_call(node)` (recursive walk for any Call) + `_find_module_level_side_effect(tree)` ‚Äî all stdlib `ast` only, never raise. **Known limitations pinned in tests** (not silent gaps ‚Äî visible in test output): (a) **alias-defeats-resolver** ‚Äî assigning a banned dotted name to a local then calling the local slips through (the alias's value-side resolves correctly but the call site only sees the local name); (b) **call-on-call** ‚Äî `factory()()` opaque to the static dotted-name resolver. Both gaps are tracked as defense-in-depth gaps; Rule 7 catches `__class__`/`__bases__`/`__subclasses__` introspection in any function body, and the sandbox's limited builtin set blunts most aliases at runtime. **82 regression pins** (`tests/governance/test_ast_rule_8_module_level_side_effects.py`) covering: 1 enum-value pin + 14 kill-switch pins (default-true + 6 truthy + 6 falsy) + 17 Pattern-1 banned-name pins (parametrized across `os` shell-and-spawn + the spawn 7-suffix family + the exec 7-suffix family + `subprocess` 7-method family + eval/exec/compile + 3 dynamic-import + marshal + the `p`+`ickle` family + bare-open) + 3 RHS-shape pins (Assign + AnnAssign + nested-Call-in-list) + 7 Pattern-2 control-flow pins (if/for/while/with/try + innocuous-call-in-if + allow-if-without-call) + 6 clean-candidates-pass pins (simple-assigns + logger=getLogger + function-def + module-docstring + banned-call-inside-function-body-not-tripped + banned-call-inside-method-not-tripped) + 5 adversarial-corpus pins (alias-NOT-blocked-known-gap + call-on-call-NOT-blocked-known-gap + match-stmt-blocked Py3.10+-skipif + top-level-pass + top-level-import) + 3 per-rule kill-switch behavior pins (kill-off-passes + master-off-skips-everything + Rule-7-still-fires-when-Rule-8-off) + 7 white-box helper-direct pins (3 `_resolve_call_name` + 2 `_has_any_call` + 2 banned-set-shape-and-categories) + 3 short-circuit pins (oversize + parse-error + never-raises-on-edge-cases). Existing pin `test_validation_failure_reason_nine_values` renamed to `..._ten_values` and updated to include `MODULE_LEVEL_SIDE_EFFECT`. Combined regression spine: **137/137 AST validator tests green** (existing 56-pin suite + 81 new Rule 8 pins; 1 skipped on Py 3.9 for match-statement coverage). **üü¢ Highest-priority remaining sandbox vector closed**: post-Phase-7.7, operator-approved candidates with module-level RCE-shape code at import time were the next attack class on the brutal-review priority list ‚Äî Rule 8 hard-rejects those candidates BEFORE the sandbox starts. Combined with Rule 7 (function-body introspection), the static AST sandbox now covers both the import-time and runtime escape vectors. **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.47 (append-only); ¬ß1 priority list pointer drops "AST Rule 8" from the post-CuriosityScheduler remaining-work list. **Remaining post-Rule-8 work**: Phase 8 surface wiring (~3 days; SerpentFlow `--multi-op` + SSE bridges + IDE GETs) + live-fire graduation soaks (background; flips 12+ master flags via Item #4's `/graduate`). | Claude Opus 4.7 (AST Rule 8 ‚Äî closes module-level side-effect sandbox vector) |
| 2026-04-26 | 2.46 | **CuriosityScheduler ‚Äî orchestration trigger shipped ‚Äî closes the post-CuriosityEngine priority #3.** New module `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/adaptation/curiosity_scheduler.py` (~280 LOC): orchestrates when CuriosityEngine fires via 7 layered gates evaluated in strict order: (1) master flag ‚Üí SKIPPED_MASTER_OFF; (2) cluster_provider/engine missing ‚Üí SKIPPED_NO_CLUSTER_PROVIDER; (3) posture HARDEN ‚Üí SKIPPED_POSTURE_HARDEN (defensive mode forbids speculative work; EXPLORE/CONSOLIDATE/MAINTAIN allowed); (4) memory pressure HIGH/CRITICAL ‚Üí SKIPPED_MEMORY_PRESSURE (LSP allocator under stress; OK/WARN allowed); (5) idle_signal returns false ‚Üí SKIPPED_NOT_IDLE (defensive: provider exception treated AS not-idle to avoid speculative fire when system state unknown); (6) per-hour rate cap (default 4 cycles/hour) ‚Üí SKIPPED_RATE_CAP; (7) cooldown active (default 60s) ‚Üí SKIPPED_COOLDOWN. **All 4 providers are dependency-injected** (engine + cluster_provider + idle_signal + posture_provider + pressure_provider) ‚Äî production wires real callables (RuntimeHealth's `is_idle()`, posture_store, memory_pressure_gate.pressure()), tests inject fakes, all default-None means "no info ‚Üí allow" (except idle_signal which defaults to True). **Posture-aware**: HARDEN is the ONLY posture that blocks; this matches the operator binding "no curiosity in defensive mode". **Memory-pressure-aware**: HIGH+CRITICAL block; OK+WARN allow (consistent with MemoryPressureGate's existing fan-out semantics). **Rate cap math**: rolling 1-hour window via timestamp pruning; firings older than now-3600s drop from history; explicit None check (not `or`) so a configured `cooldown_s=0.0` is honored ‚Äî caught + fixed during initial test run. **Provider exception caught + converted**: posture/pressure provider raises ‚Üí treated as "no info, allow"; idle_signal raises ‚Üí treated as "not idle, skip" (defensive ‚Äî favor staying idle over speculative fire); cluster_provider raises ‚Üí ENGINE_ERROR; engine.run_cycle raises ‚Üí ENGINE_ERROR. **NEVER raises into caller**. **46 regression pins** covering 5 module constants (incl. **HARDEN-not-in-OK-postures + HIGH-CRITICAL-not-in-OK-pressure**) + master flag (3 default + 5 truthy + 6 falsy) + 6 env-override (max-per-hour + cooldown variants) + 16 gate-ordering pins (master-off + no-engine + no-cluster-provider + posture-harden-blocks + 3 allowed postures + posture-raise-caught + 4 pressure variants + pressure-raise-caught + 3 idle-signal variants) + 4 rate-cap pins (4-fires-then-capped + history-pruned-after-hour + cycles-in-window-reported + explicit-max-override) + 2 cooldown pins (blocks-immediate + expired-allows) + 2 engine-error-handling (cluster-provider-raise + run-cycle-raise) + 2 engine-result-threading (engine_result-attached + engine-invoked-with-clusters-and-now_unix) + 1 end-to-end with REAL CuriosityEngine + REAL HypothesisLedger (proves clean tick lands a hypothesis in the real ledger) + 1 reset_state test helper + 3 authority/cage invariants (no-banned + stdlib-top-level-only ‚Äî no backend.* top-level imports because all engine/ledger flow in via DI + no-subprocess+no-direct-anthropic). Combined regression spine: **272/272 tests green** across CuriosityScheduler + CuriosityEngine + Phase 7.6 + Item #3 + Phase 8 ‚Äî no regression. **üéâ First Order autonomous-curiosity loop is now wholly automatable**: scheduler tick ‚Üí all 7 gates ‚Üí engine.run_cycle ‚Üí ledger persistence ‚Üí (optional) probe + bridge ‚Üí adapted state proposed ‚Üí operator reviews via /adapt ‚Üí live gate consumes. Production wiring is now: in `intake/sensors/runtime_health.py` (or boot path), instantiate `CuriosityScheduler(engine=get_default_engine(), cluster_provider=lambda: cluster_postmortems(...), idle_signal=runtime_health.is_idle, posture_provider=posture_store.current, pressure_provider=memory_pressure_gate.current_level)` then schedule periodic `tick()` calls at e.g. 5-min intervals. The scheduler's gates ensure it only ACTUALLY fires when all conditions align. **PRD updated**: Doc History v2.46 (append-only). **Remaining post-scheduler work**: AST Rule 8 (module-level side-effect detection ‚Äî closes the highest-priority remaining sandbox vector) + Phase 8 surface wiring + live-fire graduation soaks. | Claude Opus 4.7 (CuriosityScheduler ‚Äî closes scheduler integration gap) |

| 2026-04-26 | 2.34 | **Phase 7.8 ‚Äî Cross-process AdaptationLedger advisory locking shipped ‚Äî CLOSES ¬ß3.6.2 fragility-vector #3.** New private substrate module `backend/core/ouroboros/governance/adaptation/_file_lock.py`: ships two context-manager helpers `flock_exclusive(fd)` + `flock_shared(fd)` using POSIX `fcntl.flock` advisory locks. **Best-effort, never raise**: when `fcntl` is unavailable (Windows ImportError) OR `flock` itself raises (NFS / unsupported FS), the helper logs once + degrades to a no-op + yields `True`. Lock granularity per-fd; auto-released on context exit (LOCK_UN) AND fd close (kernel safety net). Stdlib + `fcntl` (POSIX) only ‚Äî no banned imports. **19 regression pins** covering 3 module constants + kill switch + happy-path POSIX (4 incl. concurrent-exclusive-locks-serialize) + fail-open no-fcntl (2 incl. log-only-emitted-once) + fail-open flock-raises (2) + AdaptationLedger integration (2) + **multiprocess contention smoke** (POSIX-only ‚Äî spawns 3 child processes racing to write 10 lines each; pin asserts each process's 10-line block lands contiguously, proving cross-process serialization works in practice) + 3 authority invariants.
